shortBio.en-US: Research and recommendations for modern stack websites.

description.en-US:
If you are looking for a quick and easy salad/meal look no further. This salad is so tasty and filling! 

I love quinoa, I love salad, and I love Mediterranean food - this quinoa salad recipe right here is one of my all time favorites because of its amazing nutrient profile. 

It is made with cooked quinoa and a variety of fresh vegetables, nuts/seeds, kalamata olives and topped with our homemade oil and vinegar dressing... and there you have it, a delicious meal (or side). 

For a sweet or tart addition, feel free to add some pomegranate seeds or chunks of apple.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: *Salad--1 cup cooked quinoa -- 1 roma tomato, diced--1/4 cup red onion, diced--1/4 cucumber, diced--3 tbsp parsley, chopped--3 tbsp cilantro, chopped--2 tbsp hemp seeds--2 tbsp pumpkin seeds--sea salt and pepper to taste--*Dressing--1/4 ripe avocado, mashed--1 clove garlic, minced--2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil--2 tbsp apple cider vinegar (or lemon juice)

recipeDirections.en-US: In a medium bowl, mix the quinoa, tomato, onion, cucumber, parsley, cilantro, hemp seeds, pumpkin seeds, salt and pepper (to taste) -- For the dressing: In a small bowl, combine the avocado, garlic, olive oil and apple cider vinegar/lemon juice -- Add the dressing into the salad bowl and enjoy!

description.en-US:
This smoothie recipe has been part of my morning routine for quite some time, it is the first smoothie I began making when I bought my very first vitamix, about 12-13 years ago. 

This here is a basic and it is a good starter smoothie, if you are a smoothie newbie. It leaves you feeling refreshed and energized. I make it for friends and family who are new to the "Green Smoothie" movement. 

Adjust some ingredients to make it work for you or simply, enjoy it as is. 

Cheers to health and happy sipping!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups spinach--1 cup frozen mango--1 cup water or milk (I prefer hemp or almond) of choice --1 tsp peanut butter-- 1/2 banana -- protein powder (I prefer vanilla)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a high speed blender, blend on high until smooth. -- Taste and (if needed) add more liquid to reach desired consistency.--Serve and enjoy immediately!

metaDescription.en-US: This here is a basic and it is a good starter smoothie, if you are a smoothie newbie. It leaves you feeling refreshed and energized. I make it for friends and family who are new to the "Green Smoothie" movement.

description.en-US:
Have you ever thought about making your own yogurt? It seems intimidating but believe me it is super easy after making it a handful of times! 

I use this yogurt in my parfaits (be sure to check out my [granola recipes](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/basic-chewy-granola "Granola recipe")), for the "drizzle" on some of my grain/protein bowls, as a side for our Indian dishes (balances the "oops too much spice" really nice), etc. It is smooth and creamy, it truly is pure perfection! 

No fuss, just fun, heat up some whole milk, let it cool, throw in a splash of yogurt love, let it cozy up, and voilà!

All you need is a candy thermometer, a pot, and a glass jar to store it in (a large size Parfait or Weck jar works well). 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1/4 cup water -- 1 liter milk (may use 1%, 2%, or whole milk)--1 packet yogurt starter OR 2-4 tbsps of plain yogurt

recipeDirections.en-US: Begin by pouring 1/4 cup of water into a medium sized sauce pan and heating it, this is to wet the pan. Now add the milk into the saucepan and heating it over medium heat until it reaches a boil, stir occasionally. -- Remove the saucepan from heat and allow the milk to cool to a temperature between 110-115 degrees Fahrenheit (this may take about 30-40 minutes). -- While in the sauce pan, whisk in 2 tablespoon of yogurt. -- Pour the milk-yogurt mixture into clean glass jars, adjusting the quantity based on the jar size. -- Cover the jars with 1-2 dish towels each and place them in an unused microwave or oven for an incubation period of 6-8 hours. The longer the incubation, the firmer and tangier the yogurt will be, as well as promoting the formation of more beneficial bacteria. -- Upon completion of the incubation, remove the glass jars and refrigerate for at least 1-2 hours before using. Your homemade yogurt is ready to be enjoyed! -- Serve it plain or with your favorite toppings like fruit, honey, or granola. -- Remember to save a small amount of this batch to use as a starter for your next homemade yogurt adventure!

tools.en-US:
- Medium-sized saucepan 
- Thermometer 
- Whisk 
- Glass jars with lids 
- Dish towel
- Unused microwave or oven

metaDescription.en-US: Unleash your inner chef with our Homemade Yogurt recipe. Learn to create a velvety, live-culture-rich delight in your kitchen. Perfect for topping with fruits, honey, or granola, this DIY yogurt is a testament to your culinary prowess.

description.en-US:
This simple granola has been a staple in our home for years. We have a few different ones but this is one of the standard ones occupying our countertop  granola jar. 

You can either eat the granola on its own, made as a parfait (see my easy homemade yogurt recipe), as a topping on a smoothie bowl, etc... The options are endless. 

My favorite part about this granola are the crunchy clusters. To get these clusters you need to let it sit on the baking sheet after baking for about 10-20 mins (without stirring). 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups raw almonds, chopped--1/2 cup uncooked quinoa--3/4 cups hemp seeds--1cup unsweetened shredded coconut--3 cups rolled oats (gluten, if required)--1 tbsp cinnamon--sea salt--2/3 cup coconut oil, melted--1/3 cup pure maple syrup--1 cup raisins (may swap for cranberries)

recipeDirections.en-US: Turn on the oven to 325˚ F, line a baking tray with unbleached parchment paper, set aside. -- In a large bowl, mix all the dry ingredients (except the raisons), set aside. -- On the stove top, add the coconut oil and maple syrup into a pot, stir until melted and remove from the heat. -- Add the coconut oil and maple syrup into the bowl, mix until all the oats are covered. -- Pour the oats onto the parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes (stir at 10 minutes). -- Once 20 minutes is up, add the raisins, mix them in and return to the oven for 5 minutes. -- Remove from the oven and let the oats sit, untouched til the cool (this makes them into crunchy clusters). -- Once cool, add the granola to an airtight jar and store for up to 2 weeks

description.en-US:
One of my favorite things about fall is roasting pumpkin seeds. 

As a child, we would carve our pumpkins for Halloween, and then my sweet mama would clean and dry out the seeds. We would then coat them with oil and salt, then bake them to a crispy perfection. They were so tasty and addicting, we’d finish them up in just a few days.

Pumpkin seeds are such a delicious and healthy snack to keep on hand. They are rich in omega fatty acids, vitamins, minerals and high in fiber (more so when you consume both the shell and inner kernel aka the pepita).  

The recipe I provided is for 1 cup of seeds, so if you have 2 cups on hand, just double up on the other ingredients. 

Happy snacking!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup raw pumpkin seeds--2 tbsp avocado oil--1/4 tsp sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 275°F and line a medium sized baking sheet with non-bleached parchment paper, set aside. -- Scoop the pumpkin seeds and flesh out of the pumpkin and place them into a bowl of water. Remove as much of the flesh as you can, it is ok if some flesh is left behind. Remove the seeds from the water, and pat them dry them with a towel. -- In a small bowl mix the pumpkin seeds, avocado oil and salt. -- Spread the seeds in a single layer on the baking sheet. -- Bake for 40 minutes, stirring them every 15 minutes to help them bake evenly.  I usually remove them at the 40 minute mark and test one, as long as the inner seed does not taste burnt you can cook it for another 3-5 minutes for a little extra crunch.  -- Once the seeds are cooked, remove them from the oven and allow them to cool on the pan, they continue to crisp as they cool.

description.en-US:
This here is one of my favorite sandwich fillers, it is so creamy, healthy and satisfying. From the chickpeas, to the mustard, onions, pickles, vegan mayo, spices etc - it'll send your tastebuds to heaven. Whether you spread it on a sandwich, stuff a lettuce wrap, dip into it with pita bread, or use it to elevate your salads, you're in for a delightful and satisfying treat.

The recipe here is for the chickpea salad, however you use it it! 
For wraps and sandwiches, be sure to add some toppings such as avocado, chili flakes, tomato, sprouts, etc. If you love a heart sandwich, be sure to stack it up! 

It's easy to make and store in the fridge, so when hunger strikes you are prepared!! 

This recipe will keep for 2-3 days stored in an airtight container in the fridge. 

Stay happy, stay healthy!

ingredients.en-US: *Chickpea Salad--2 cups cooked chickpeas -- 1/2 teaspoon sea salt -- black pepper -- 1 teaspoon turmeric powder -- 2 tablespoons yellow mustard -- 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice -- 4 tablespoons mayonnaise (may substitute with tahini) -- 2 cloves garlic, minced or pressed -- 1/2 cup bell pepper, finely chopped (red or orange) -- 4 celery stalks, finely chopped -- 2 green onions, sliced (or 1/4 cup red onions, finely chopped) -- 1/2 cup dill pickle, chopped

recipeDirections.en-US: In a large bowl, smash the chickpeas with a fork or potato masher. Leave some whole, some half smashed, etc -- Add the salt, pepper, turmeric powder, mustard, lemon juice, vegan mayonnaise, and garlic. -- Now add all other ingredients and combine. Taste and adjust spices as needed. -- Serve as desired.

recipeNotes.en-US: 1 cup raw chickpeas is equivalent to 1 3/4-2 cups cooked chickpeas

description.en-US:
This smoothie here is a great choice for after a workout. 
Cherries are used as the base of this smoothie. Cherries have earned a golden star in the fitness world as they have shown to aid in muscle recovery and decrease muscle soreness.  Also found in this delicious smoothie are turmeric, ginger, and cilantro and a few other amazing ingredients. 

Feel free to use whatever milk of choice you like, I prefer to use hemp milk as the liquid in this smoothie as it gives it a delicious creamy texture while bumping up the protein, naturally.

It may sound weird... but I add a dash of black pepper, black pepper is needed to enhance curcumin absorption in the body (may help fight inflammation), I promise you don't even notice it is in there. 

Cheers to health my friend and as always, enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups frozen cherries--1/4 cup avocado -- 1/2 inch piece fresh ginger root -- 1 inch piece fresh turmeric root (may  substitute for 1/2 tsp turmeric powder) -- 1/4 cup fresh cilantro -- 1 cup milk of choice (I prefer my almond milk or hemp milk) -- 1 tbsp chia seeds -- 1 tbsp flax seeds -- 3 tbsps hemp seeds -- vanilla or plain protein powder (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a high power blender, blend until smooth -- Adjust liquid (or add ice) to reach desired texture. -- Serve and enjoy immediately!

description.en-US:
The picture looks amazing and the name sounds interesting BUT what exactly is a paratha?? Let me explain... 

A paratha is a type of flatbread, originated in India and surrounding areas. It is made from a base of flour, salt, water, and ghee. You can add so many other ingredients to this delicious flatbread to make it your own. This recipe here includes avocado, onions, garlic and so many other nourishing spices and herbs. 

We eat this paratha at breakfast or lunch, dipped in plain yogurt (refer to my [Homemade Yogurt recipe](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/homemade-yogurt)) and a side of avocado and veggies. 

It took me a few attempts before mastering the paratha cooking skills but I promise it gets easier! As they say... practice makes perfect! 

If you do not use all the dough, you can store it in the fridge for 2-3 days in an airtight container and cook when needed. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 avocado--1 1/4 cups (+ 3 tbsp) spelt flour--1/4 cup cilantro chopped--1/4 cup red onion, diced--1 clove garlic, minced--2 tsp lemon juice--3/4 tsp garam masala--3/4 tsp ground cumin powder--3/4 tsp turmeric powder--flour (extra for dusting when rolling the paratha) -- sea salt and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: Peel the avocado, remove the meat and mash it using a fork. -- Add the cumin, garam masala, turmeric, salt and pepper, cilantro, onions, and lemon juice - mix well. -- Add the spelt flour to the avocado mix and knead it with your hand into a soft dough (add warm water by the tbsp, if required). -- Let the ball of dough sit for 10-20 minutes. -- Heat a skillet on the stove top on medium heat (I use a cast iron one that my MIL brought from India). -- Make equal size balls from the dough (I make 7-9) -- Take a ball, dust it with flour and flatten it out using your hands. Then roll it out using a rolling pin. -- Place the rolled out paratha onto the heated skillet and let it cook, you will see some bubbles (45-60 seconds). -- Flip the paratha and cook this side the same. -- Flip the paratha again and pat it down with a towel or a spatula for a few seconds. --  Remove from the heat and add some butter. Serve hot with yogurt, fresh vegetables, and/or chutney. -- Enjoy!

description.en-US:
Looking for a guilt-free indulgence? Our Chocolate Date Balls recipe is the answer! Made with wholesome ingredients like soft Medjool dates, raw almonds, and cocoa powder, these delectable treats are not only delicious but also nutritious. Whether you need a quick snack or a satisfying dessert, these Chocolate Date Balls are sure to hit the spot.

Nutritious Ingredients:
Each bite of our Chocolate Date Balls is packed with goodness. Medjool dates provide natural sweetness and fiber, while raw almonds and walnuts add a satisfying crunch and healthy fats. Cocoa powder not only enhances the rich chocolate flavor but also offers antioxidants. Plus, hemp seeds and shredded coconut contribute additional nutrients and texture to these delightful treats.

Simple Preparation:
Our recipe makes preparation a breeze. Simply pulse the nuts and dates in a food processor, then mix in the cocoa powder, coconut oil, water, salt, hemp seeds, and shredded coconut. Roll the mixture into balls, chill, and enjoy! With just a few easy steps, you'll have a batch of irresistible Chocolate Date Balls ready to devour.

Versatile Storage:
One of the best things about our Chocolate Date Balls is how easily they can be stored. Keep them in the fridge for up to a week or store them in the freezer for longer-lasting freshness. Whether you're meal prepping for the week ahead or looking for a make-ahead dessert for special occasions, these treats have you covered.

Say goodbye to guilt and hello to indulgence with our Chocolate Date Balls recipe. Packed with nutritious ingredients, easy to make, and perfect for storing, these delectable treats are a must-have for any chocolate lover. Try them out today and satisfy your cravings the healthy way!

ingredients.en-US: 20 soft Medjool dates, pitted--1 cup raw almonds--1 cup raw walnuts--1/3 cup cocoa powder (unsweetened)--1 tbsp coconut oil, melted--1 tbsp water--1/2 tsp salt (adjust to taste)--4 tbsp hemp seeds--1/4 cup shredded unsweetened coconut

recipeDirections.en-US: Place the almonds and walnuts into a food processor and pulse until finely chopped.--Add the pitted dates, cocoa powder, melted coconut oil, water, salt, and hemp seeds to the food processor with the nuts. Pulse until the mixture comes together and forms a sticky dough (test by rollin a ball with your hand).--Transfer the mixture to a bowl and mix in the shredded coconut until evenly distributed.--Using slightly wet hands, scoop out portions of the mixture and roll into evenly sized balls.--Optional: Roll the balls in additional shredded coconut or cocoa powder for extra coating.--Place the rolled balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set.--Once set, transfer the Chocolate Date Balls to an airtight container and store them in the fridge for up to 7 days or in the freezer for up to 3 weeks.--Enjoy your delicious and healthy Chocolate Date Balls!

metaDescription.en-US: Indulge guilt-free with our Chocolate Date Balls recipe! Packed with nutritious ingredients like dates, almonds, and cocoa, these decadent treats are perfect for satisfying your sweet cravings. Easy to make and store, a must-try for any chocolate lover!

description.en-US:
Bread is such a WONDERFUL thing, especially this simple recipe right here! Biased? Maybe, but with good reason! 

I grew up near an amazing local farmers market and we would go weekly to pick up our fresh baked bread from the famous "Bread Man”. Some days we wouldn't make it back home without half of the loaf already eaten.        

I love my fresh baked bread with butter and a plate of veggies. It tastes great with soup, with my [chickpea salad](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/smashed-chickpea-salad), or you can serve with [eggs/tofu scramble](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/tofu-scramble-indian-spiced), the possibilities are endless.

For this recipe I like to use a white spelt variety (Vitaspelt brand), sprouted spelt flour, and 100% whole sprout. The amount of flour used may vary slightly depending on the brand.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups filtered water, warm -- 1 tablespoon honey or maple syrup (or sugar of choice) -- 1 tbsp active dry yeast -- 3 - 3 1/4 cup spelt flour (I use Vita Spelt White Flour)  --1 1/2 tsp Himalayan pink sea salt -- 2 tbsp olive oil

recipeDirections.en-US: In a medium size bowl, whisk together the warm water maple syrup or honey. Now sprinkle the yeast onto the water, set aside and allow it to foam. This may take about 10-15 minutes. --  Now add the flour, sea salt, and olive oil to the water mixture. Gradually add the spelt flour until a smooth and springy dough forms (the amount of flour used may vary). You may stir with a wooden spoon or knead with your hands. -- Form the dough into a ball. -- Transfer the ball to a bowl and cover with a lid (or wrap). Allow it to sit at a room temperature for about 12-14 hours, or until the dough has doubled in size, some bubbles should form on the top. -- When the dough is ready for baking, preheat the oven to 450˚ F. Set your dutch oven inside the oven to warm, about 25-30 minutes. -- Transfer the dough to a floured surface and fold the corners of the dough under itself, you want a round surface. -- Set out a piece of unbleached parchment paper, transfer the ball of dough onto the paper and let it sit for 10 minutes. -- With a knife make an "X" shape or 3 lines into the dough. -- Remove the dutch oven from the oven and place the parchment paper and dough into the dutch oven, return to the oven to bake. -- Bake with the lid on for 30 minutes. -- Remove the lid and bake for another 8-10 minutes, you want the top to be golden brown but not burnt. -- Transfer the bread to a cooling rack and allow it to cool for 20-25 minutes. -- Slice and serve.

recipeNotes.en-US: For the yeast, if the yeast does not become foamy within 10 minutes of sprinkling it onto the water and maple syrup, discard and start again, it may be expired.

description.en-US:
This delightful Indian dish is a regular feature in our Monday meals. It boasts incredible flavor, brings comfort, and is crafted with a handful of wholesome, nutritious ingredients. What's even better, it can be prepared and ready to savor in under 30 minutes, requiring minimal hands-on effort!

You can enjoy this daal with roti, spoon it over rice or quinoa, or simply savor it as a soup. Regardless of your preference, consider pairing the daal with a side of salad or vegetables and plain yogurt (refer to my homemade yogurt recipe [Homemade yogurt recipe](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/homemade-yogurt "Homemade yogurt "). 

### Key Ingredients:

__Red Lentils:__ Packed with protein, fiber, and essential nutrients, red lentils form the heart of this dish. They contribute to sustained energy and promote a feeling of fullness.

__Tomatoes:__ Bursting with antioxidants and vitamins, tomatoes add a vibrant touch to our daal while providing immune-boosting benefits.

__Turmeric:__ Known for its anti-inflammatory properties, turmeric not only lends a warm, golden hue but also contributes to the overall well-being of your body.

__Cumin Seeds:__ A fragrant spice that not only elevates the flavor profile but also brings digestive benefits and a dose of iron.

__Onion, Garlic, and Ginger:__ These aromatic trio not only enhance the taste but also introduce a myriad of health benefits, including immune support and improved digestion.

__Cilantro:__ A fresh and vibrant herb that not only adds a burst of flavor but also provides antioxidants and aids digestion.

__Optional: Chili Powder:__ For those who crave a hint of spice, chili powder can be added, offering metabolism-boosting properties and an extra kick.

### Health Benefits:

__Rich in Plant Protein:__ Ideal for vegans and vegetarians, this daal serves as a protein powerhouse, promoting muscle health and satiety.

__Heart-Healthy:__ The combination of lentils, tomatoes, and spices contributes to heart health, with ingredients known for supporting cardiovascular well-being.

__Anti-Inflammatory:__ Turmeric and cumin seeds bring anti-inflammatory properties, potentially aiding in reducing inflammation within the body.

__Digestive Wellness:__ The inclusion of ginger, garlic, and cumin supports healthy digestion, making this daal not just delicious but also easy on the stomach.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup dried, split red lentils (sorted and rinsed) -- 3 3/4 cups water -- 2 ripe tomatoes, diced -- 1 tsp turmeric powder -- 4 tbsp of water (more as needed) -- 3/4 tsp cumin seeds -- 1 yellow onion, diced small -- 3 cloves garlic, peeled and minced  -- 1/2 inch piece ginger, peeled and minced or grated -- 1/2 tsp chili powder (optional) -- salt and pepper to taste -- 1/2 cup fresh cilantro, rinsed and chopped

recipeDirections.en-US: Begin by placing the lentils in a bowl, sorting through them to remove any small stones or grit. Rinse the lentils thoroughly and set them aside.--In a medium saucepan, combine water, lentils, tomatoes, and turmeric. Stir the ingredients together and bring the mixture to a rolling boil over medium-high heat. Once boiling, stir the daal and reduce the heat to medium. Cook uncovered for about 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.--Lower the heat to a simmer, cover the pan, and allow the daal to cook until the lentils are soft, approximately 10 minutes. Stir occasionally and smash some of the lentils against the side of the pan until the daal achieves a thick soup-like consistency. Remove from heat.--Prepare the spices by heating water in a small skillet over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and cook for 10-15 seconds, ensuring they do not burn. Add diced onion, garlic, and ginger. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the onion becomes translucent, stirring frequently. If using chili powder, add it now and stir.--Combine the spice/onion mixture with the cooked red lentils and stir thoroughly. Cook for an additional 5 minutes over low heat, allowing the daal to meld together.--Serve the daal warm with your choice of accompaniment such as roti, rice, quinoa, or enjoy it as a soup. Garnish with cilantro when serving.--Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for 2-3 days.

metaDescription.en-US: Experience plant-based perfection with our Lentil Daal—a protein-rich, heart-healthy delight. Crafted with red lentils, tomatoes, and spices, this vegan recipe offers a flavorful journey to well-being. Elevate your meals with ease and nourishment!

description.en-US:
What's a movie without popcorn? Honestly, who doesn't love a bowl of fluffy, warm, buttery popcorn? 

Best thing about making your own is that you can make as much or little as you want of this healthier snacking alternative. 

Next time you put on a movie, give this tasty recipe a try! 

Happy snacka-lackin!!

ingredients.en-US: 2 tbsp coconut oil -- 1/2 cup dry popcorn kernals -- butter (optional) -- sea salt to taste -- nutritional flakes (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Turn on the Instant Pot and select 'saute' mode, allow it to heat up. -- Once it reads 'Hot' add the coconut oil and allow it to melt. -- Add the popcorn kernels and stir all the kernels til they are covered in oil. -- Place a glass lid (so you can see it pop) on top of the instant pot. It may take 2-3 mins before it starts to pop. -- Allow the corn to pop for about 5 minutes, the sound of the corn popping will slow down. -- Remove the glass lid and with a oven mitt, pour the popcorn into a serving bowl. -- Season the popcorn however you like (I prefer a tbsp of melted butter, salt and nutritional flakes).

description.en-US:
Let's take a minute here and TACO-bout Tuesday... 'Vegetarian Taco Tuesday', that is! We designate 2 days a week to food 'Pizza Friday' and 'Taco Tuesday!' 

This recipe here is for the taco 'meat' (walnut and brown lentils), feel free to top it with what you like (lettuce, tomatoes, red peppers, corn, avocado, cilantro, yogurt and/or taco sauce) - get creative! 

I often double this recipe and use extras for leftover lunches and/or taco quinoa bowls. 

'Buen Provecho'

ingredients.en-US: 3/4 cup brown lentils, sorted and rinsed -- 1/3 cup white onion, chopped -- 1/2 cup walnuts, chopped into pieces -- 2-3 tbsp avocado oil -- 6 cherry tomatoes, diced -- 1 tbsp garam masala -- 1/2 -1 tsp chili powder (optional) -- sea salt (1/2 tsp) and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: Rinse and sort the dried lentils. -- Add the lentils to a medium pot and cover with water by 2-3 inches. Bring it to a boil and then reduce to medium low and simmer for 25 mins. -- Chop the onions and walnuts. -- On a heated skillet, add half the oil and the walnuts. Toast the walnuts for 2-3 minutes, they will be light brown and color and fragrant. Set the walnuts aside. --  In a heated skillet add the onions, and cook for 4 mins until they are translucent. Add the cherry tomatoes  and the spices, cook for another 4-5 minutes. Now add the walnuts. -- Drain the cooked lentils and add them to the skillet and cook for another 3 mins, mix it together. Adjust the salt and pepper to taste. -- Add the the taco 'meat' to some taco shells and top as desired. Serve with a side of guacamole and/or salsa. -- Enjoy!

description.en-US:
This delicious smoothie is one of my daughters favorite smoothies. She loves the color and says it "tastes as good as candy!" 

This smoothie serves as a great substitute for breakfast or an afternoon snack. The fiber rich oats and fat fueled avocado helps keep you feeling full longer. I highly recommend using frozen fruit as it provides a more enjoyable texture. 

This recipe provided does not include any greens but feel free to add a handful of spinach to boost its nutritional profile. 

Adding protein is optional (my children love the Vanilla flavor from Vega but any clean vanilla protein will do). 

Happy sipping! xo

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup frozen mango -- 1/4 cup avocado  -- 1/4 cup rolled oats (use gluten free variety of necessary) -- 1 banana -- 1 cup frozen strawberries -- 1 tbsp almond butter -- 2 cups milk of choice (I use my homemade almond milk) -- 1-2 tbsp flaxseeds -- vanilla or plain protein powder (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a blender, blend until smooth. -- Enjoy!

description.en-US:
The name of this salad speaks for itself, strawberries and pecans on a spring mix. It is fresh, crisp, vibrant, and absolutely delicious... packed full of nutrient dense goodness! You can enjoy it as is, or eat it as a side. 

Need cheese? Feta cheese pairs well with this salad so feel free to add some on top.

For the balsamic dressing, you will have excess, simply store the extra dressing in the fridge.

ingredients.en-US: 5 cups spring mix salad--14 strawberries, sliced--1/3 cup red onion, thinly sliced--1 cup cauliflower, broken into pieces--1/2 cup pecans--1/4 cup feta cheese (optional)--1/4 cup balsamic vinegar--1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil--1 tbsp dijon mustard--2 cloves garlic, minced--salt (1/2 tsp) and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash and cut all the vegetables and strawberries, add to a large bowl. -- Add the pecans and feta (optional) to the large bowl - set aside. -- In a small bowl, mix the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, dijon mustard, minced garlic, salt and pepper. Pour over the salad. -- Serve and enjoy!

recipeNotes.en-US: Enjoy as a meal or serve as a side.

metaDescription.en-US: The name of this salad speaks for itself, strawberries and pecans on a spring mix. It is fresh, crisp, vibrant, and absolutely delicious... packed full of nutrient dense goodness!

description.en-US:
This creamy, delicious Golden Milk roots from the Ayurvedic kitchen. I first learned about Haldi (turmeric) Doodh (milk) aka Golden Milk in a book by a man named Yogi Bhajan, where he teaches about the vitality and health benefits that this drink provides. 

Turmeric (what gives this milk yellow/golden color) is a very healing food, it is thought to be beneficial for the joints, organs, inflammation, etc. I add turmeric root to a lot of foods for this very reason. 

As the base, I prefer to use coconut milk or flax milk but feel free to swap with your own milk preference. 

Be sure to include the black pepper as it assists in the bioavailability of the curcumin. 

Cheers!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups milk of choice (I prefer hemp or coconut milk) -- 1 1/2 tsp ground turmeric--1/4 tsp fresh ground ginger root -- 1 stick of cinnamon (can use cinnamon powder instead) -- pinch of black pepper -- maple syrup (sweeten to taste)

recipeDirections.en-US: In a small saucepan (on medium heat) add all the ingredients (except the maple syrup).  -- Heat for about 5 minutes. Whisk often to prevent it from burning to the bottom of the pan. -- Now add the maple syrup to sweeten as desired. -- Serve immediately.

description.en-US:
Imagine turning your morning bowl of oats into a waffle... I know right, magic! Well that's what we did here and it is definitely a breakfast worth bragging rights. 

For vegan: use a vegan friendly milk, use a vegan butter or coconut oil, and swap the eggs for flax eggs. 1 egg = 1 tbsp ground flax + 3 tbsp water, whisked. 

If you are gluten free, (GF) use a certified GF oat brand. 

Top these waffles with your desired fruit toppings, a little syrup, and you are breakfast ready!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups rolled oats, blended to a flour--1/2 tsp salt--1 tsp baking powder--2 medium eggs (if vegan use flax eggs - noted in description)--2 tbsp pure maple syrup--3/4 cup milk of choice, room temp--3 tbsp melted butter or coconut oil

recipeDirections.en-US: In a blender (such as the vitamix) add all the dry ingredients, be sure the oats are blending down to a flour consistency. -- Now add all wet ingredients and blend well (If using flax eggs, you will want to prepare these in advance, allow them to sit for 15 min in the fridge). If you do not have a vitamix this step can be done in a medium bowl as long as you are starting with an oat flour. -- Pour the waffle mix onto a heated waffle maker and cook as per manual instructions. I prefer them on a medium-high heat so that they come out golden brown. -- Serve with fresh fruit, a dash of cinnamon or cacao, drizzle some pure maple syrup, etc!

description.en-US:
This blend is my baby, I love this smoothie, especially first thing in the morning or after a workout.

It definitely fits the 'functional food' category because these ingredients work for you. This smoothie contains powerful ingredients that are thought to be medicinal such as turmeric (anti-inflammatory), ginger (relieves pain and inflammation), protein (builds them muscles), mango, avocado, greens (think popeye), hemp seed, fresh cilantro, etc... This smoothie is packed with healthy fats, nourishing vitamins and minerals, and so much more. 

I truly love this blend, it is so refreshing. I think that you will enjoy it as well!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups filtered water--1 1/2 cups flax milk (or milk of choice)--1/4 cup cilantro--1 inch piece fresh turmeric--1 inch piece ginger root--2 cups frozen mango--2 tbsp chia seeds--2 tbsp flax seeds--3 tbsp hempseeds--4 cups spinach--dash of black pepper (you cannot taste it)--handful of almonds (about 15)--vanilla or plain protein powder (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash your produce and then add all ingredients to the blender.--Blend til smooth.--Pour and enjoy!

description.en-US:
Hummus is one of our favorite snacks, it is very nutritious and an easy 'grab and go' when in a pinch. This hummus can be eaten as a vegetable or cracker dip and/or spread on a tasty sandwich or wrap. 

This roasted beet hummus is made with oven roasted beets, chickpeas, olive oil, spices, tahini, lemon juice, and fresh garlic. I like to serve this with some chopped cilantro, hemp seeds and/or pomegranate seeds... it adds some extra ummph and it looks pretty. 

More about the ingredients, beets are amazing as they are rich in antioxidants, vitamin C, potassium, and manganese. They are also high in fiber - total nutrition powerhouse! 

Prepare the chickpeas overnight by soaking them in filtered water, this is thought to improve their digestibility. It seems like extra work (compared to using a can), but trust me, it is so worth it and it gets easier to do with practice.

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/4 cup cooked chickpeas--1/2 lemon, juiced--1/2 tsp paprika--1/4 cup olive oil--1 tbsp avocado oil--2 cloves garlic--2 medium beets--2 tbsp tahini--salt and pepper to taste--optional toppings: fresh cilantro, pistachio, hemp seeds, pomegranate

recipeDirections.en-US: Cooking the chickpeas: Soak the dried chickpeas (1/2 cup dried) in a bowl of water overnight. Once soaking is complete, drain the water, rinse them and add them to an instant pot or pressure cooker with water (2 inches above the chickpeas). Set the instant pot to 'beans' and cook for 26 minutes. Save water when finished. -- Roasting the beets: Wash, peel and remove the stems from the beet (may wait to peel the beet til after roasting). Cut the beets in quarters, add to a baking pan lined with unbleached parchment paper. Drizzle 1 tbsp of avocado oil and season with salt and pepper. Bake in the oven for 40-45 minutes at 400° - the beet should be fork tender. -- To make the hummus: Add the cooked chickpeas, roasted beets and remaining ingredients to the blender (except the optional topping: cilantro, hemp seeds, pistachios, sesame seeds or pomegranate seeds). -- Blend until smooth, if needed, add the leftover water from the cooked chickpeas to reach the desired texture. -- Drizzle some olive oil when serving and top with desired toppings. -- Happy dipping!

description.en-US:
I love cookies, and what I love more are healthy and delicious cookies made with simple good for you ingredients. 

These cookies contain just a handful of ingredients. They have an almond flour base and are sweetened with honey. They melt in your mouth and pair great coffee or tea.

If you are looking for a healthy and nutritious cookie, look no further. 

Turn on your oven and pull out a mixing bowl... Let’s start baking!!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups almond flour--1/4 tsp baking soda--1/4 tsp salt (I prefer himalayan pink salt)--2 tsp vanilla--4 tbsp coconut oil--5 tbsp honey

recipeDirections.en-US: Set the oven to 350°, line a baking pan with unbleached parchment paper.--In a medium sized bowl, mix all the ingredients together.--Make 12-13 cookies and slightly flatten them using your fingers.--Bake for 9 mins. Remove from the oven and transfer to a cooling rack.--Serve with your favorite tea or see one of my other hot beverage recipes (chai, matcha, or golden milk).

description.en-US:
Pie is a Thanksgiving holiday must-have in our family. My childhood Thanksgiving memories involve food, and lots of it. I remember the wonderful dinners with our family, everyone cooked and baking delicious pies and apple crisps. My aunt was our Martha Stewart in the kitchen, she owned the 'pie crown', hands down.

As I now take on these traditions for our family, I focused on creating healthier versions of these recipes.
This recipe here is the standard crust we use for our pumpkin pie but you can use it with any pie you like.

I will add a recipe for the complete pie but right here is just the delicious pie crust.

The pecan and hemp seeds make a great crust, they bump up the protein and give the crust a delicious crispy texture. The addition of the coconut sugar and cinnamon gives the pie crust a sweet taste but it is not overbearing, it still allows the filling and toppings to take that on.
For a vegan alternative, swap the butter for vegan butter. - Hope you enjoy this delicious crust as much as we do!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup oat flour--1 cup pecans, toasted--2 tbsp hemp seeds--1/2 tsp cinnamon--1/3 cup butter, melted

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350°.--Add all the ingredients to a food processor and pulse until the mixture is finely ground and it sticks together.--Add the mixture to a 10 inch pie pan. Press it down firmly and even to the bottom and sides of the pan.--Place to the pan into the oven for 12 minutes.--Remove from the oven and set aside.--Make desired pie filling of choice.

title.en-US: Cauliflower and Chickpea Coconut Spiced Soup

description.en-US:
If you like Indian food and you like soup, you will love this delicious Chana Masala inspired soup. 

We came up with this recipe when making a chana masala one night. Typically we eat our chana masala with roti or rice but decided to add some extra spices, tofu, veggies, and broth and turned our standard dish into a soup... and this is it!

I soak my beans overnight in a bowl of water and then cook them in my Instant Pot for 26 minutes. Feel free to swap for canned, just measure out 2 cups of beans without the liquid. 

Just before serving, squeeze some lime juice into it, this must not be missed as it adds to the perfect finish. I often top this dish with fresh cilantro, crumbled cashews, sprouts, avocado, etc. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 2 tbsp avocado oil -- 3/4 tsp cumin seeds-- 3 cloves of garlic, minced -- 1 inch piece ginger, minced -- 1/2 cup of red onion, diced -- 1 roma tomato, diced -- 1/2 tsp chili powder -- 1/2 tsp cumin powder -- 1/2 tsp garam masala -- 3/4 tsp turmeric powder -- 2.5 cups cauliflower, small-medium size florets -- 1 cup firm tofu, diced -- 2 cups chickpeas, cooked -- 1.5 cups vegetable stock -- 1 cup coconut milk -- salt (1/2 tsp) and pepper to taste -- fresh cilantro, chopped finely -- lime, juiced

recipeDirections.en-US: In a larger skillet, over medium-high heat, heat the avocado oil. Add the cumin seeds and cook for 2 minutes, stirring often. -- Add the garlic, ginger and onions - cook for 2-3 minutes being careful not to let them burn. -- Add the tomato and all the other spices (except salt and pepper). Mix it around until everything is combined. -- Now add the cauliflower, tofu and chickpeas. Stir it around and cook for about 3-4 minutes, this is to soften the cauliflower a bit. -- Add the vegetable broth and coconut milk. Bring the soup to a boil over medium-high heat and then reduce it to simmer for 5-7 minutes while the soup thickens. Test the cauliflower to make sure it is cooked (you do not want it to be too soft). -- Add salt and pepper to taste. -- Serve the hot soup into bowls, squeeze some fresh lime juice  into each serving. Top with desired toppings.

description.en-US:
These delicious chocolate chip cookies are gluten-free, vegan friendly, and 100% approved by my husband (aka the cookie monster). 

We are not gluten free in our home but we like to enjoy foods, lower on the gluten side to lessen the bloat. Made with whole food ingredients like almond flour, almond butter, rolled oats (make sure they are GF), maple syrup, coconut and chocolate chips - these cookies are a WIN-WIN. They satisfy your sweet tooth and keep your energy in a good place. 

So without any further ado...  let the cookie baking begin!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup blanched almond flour--1 cup rolled oats--3/4 tsp cinnamon--2 tbsp hemp seeds--1/2 tsp baking powder--1/4 tsp salt--1/4 cup shredded unsweetened coconut--2/3 cup dairy free semi-sweet chocolate chips--1/2 cup creamy almond butter (can swap for PB)--1/3 cup maple syrup

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350°. Gather all ingredients and supplies. Line a baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper. -- In a large bowl add the almond flour, oats, cinnamon, hemp seeds, baking powder and salt, whisk and set aside. -- In the same bowl add the remaining ingredients and mix well. -- Separate the batter into even sized balls (approx 14 cookies). -- Place the cookies onto the pan and lightly press them with the back of a fork (leave just a little space between them). -- Bake in the oven for 11-13 minutes. -- Remove the pan from the oven and allow the cookies to cool on a rack for 5-7 minutes. -- Enjoy!!!

description.en-US:
As a child, I remember waking up in the morning to the smell of my mama’s fresh loaf of banana bread. The smell would spread throughout our home and everyone would know what was baking in the oven. Her bread was perfect! My goal as a mother is to create this experience for my little ones and hubby. This sweet loaf of goodness is like a hug in a pan... My children and husband love it. Mission accomplished.

This recipe relies on the bananas and some coconut sugar to give a sweet and natural flavor.

Feel free to swap the eggs for flax eggs and the butter for vegan butter to make this deliciously nutritious recipe into a vegan friendly version.

Enjoy

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cup spelt flour -- 1/2 cup walnuts or chocolate chips (optional) --1/4 cup coconut sugar --  3 tbsp hemp seeds --  1/4 tsp salt (I prefer pink Himalayan sea salt) -- 1 tsp baking powder -- 1 tsp baking soda -- 1 tsp cinnamon -- 1 tsp vanilla -- 1/3 cup butter or vegan butter, room temp -- 2 eggs (flax eggs for vegan, see notes) -- 1/4 cup milk of choice -- 3 spotty bananas, mashed

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350˚ F -- Line a 8 x 4" pan with coconut oil or unbleached parchment paper. Set aside. -- In a large bowl, mix the dry ingredients. -- In another bowl, mix the wet. Now add the wet to the dry bowl, combine well but do not over mix. -- Bake the loaf for 50-60 minutes. Check with a toothpick to make sure it is baked through.

recipeNotes.en-US: 1 flax egg = 1 tbsp ground flax + 3 tbsp water, mix well.

description.en-US:
I love salads, I really do. I eat a salad every single day and have been for years. I love the crisp texture when biting into the vegetables, the vibrant color it adds to the table, and the energy I get from it. 

If you love salads or are looking for some delicious salads to dip into, you are in the right place! Be sure to check out my other delicious salad recipes as well.

ingredients.en-US:
1/2 an avocado, sliced--1/4 cup pomegranate seeds--1/4 cup red onion, diced--1 clove garlic, minced--1 tbsp olive oil--2 cups arugula--2 tbsp balsamic vinegar
5 cherry tomatoes, halved--handful of slivered almonds--salt and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash all the vegetables and prepare as noted in the ingredients (except the avocado), place into a medium sized bowl. Add the almonds. -- In a small bowl mix the olive oil, balsamic vinegar, minced garlic, salt and pepper - mix well. -- Add the dressing to the salad and mix to coat the vegetable. Add the sliced avocado. -- Serve.

description.en-US:
I love warm beverages. Whether it be coffee (my main squeeze), tea, matcha, anything... it warms my soul (and hands, Minnesota winters are cold).

Matcha has more caffeine and more potent properties than traditional green tea as it contains the entire leaf that is dried out and blended into a powder. 

Studies shows that matcha contains small amounts of vitamins and minerals, and is rich in antioxidants - a soothing and nutritional beverage.

Feel free to swap out your preferred milk of choice and choice of sweetener.

Happy ZEN'ing!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 tbsp matcha powder--2 cups milk of choice--3 tbsp hot water--sweeten with honey or maple syrup (I prefer maple syrup)

recipeDirections.en-US: In a small bowl, add the matcha, hot water and maple syrup (optional) - whisk with either a standard whisk or traditional bamboo whisk, until the powder is completely dissolved.--Now heat your milk of choice. We heat ours in a metal pitcher and the steam wand on our espresso machine but feel free to use a saucepan on the stove. If using the stove top, whisk the milk well to get a froth.--Add the matcha and the milk to your favorite mugs, sip and zen!

description.en-US:
My 6 year old son came home from school the other day, wanting to make some fresh juice. He asked me if he could make a recipe for our site. I handed him a pen and paper and set him up with our juicer (supervised). 

He titled his recipe "Simrans Joos" and then headed over to the fruit basket, he explained why he was selecting the fruits that he did. 

"Oranges have vitamins C and will help protect my body... pineapple is sweet and really yummy". - So here you have it my friends, "Simrans Joos!" 

It's nutritious and delicious, loaded with Vitamin C. I added the option of additional ginger and turmeric to boost the nutrient value and anti-inflammatory properties. 

Cheers!

ingredients.en-US: 4 medium oranges (peeled) -- 1 medium pineapple (skin removed) -- 1 inch piece ginger (optional -- 1 inch piece turmeric (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash, peel, chop and prepare all the produce. -- Run the oranges, pineapple and if using, the ginger and turmeric, through the juicer. -- Serve immediately or store in an airtight glass container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours (better if consumed immediately)

recipeNotes.en-US: I use and recommend an Omega 8006 juicer

description.en-US:
There’s just something about homemade granola that makes the whole kitchen smell like a warm hug, isn’t there? This easy, wholesome granola recipe is lightly sweetened with maple syrup (or honey if you’d rather!), has the perfect crunch, and is packed with good-for-you ingredients like oats, nuts, and seeds.

It’s gluten-free friendly, naturally sweetened, and totally customizable. Whether you sprinkle it over yogurt, pour it with almond milk, or just snack on it by the handful (guilty), this is the kind of pantry staple you’ll want on repeat.

ingredients.en-US: 4 cups old-fashioned rolled oats (use certified gluten-free oats if needed) -- 1 ¾ cups raw nuts and/or seeds (I love using 1 cup pecans + ½ cup pepitas) -- 1 tsp fine-grain sea salt (scale to ¾ tsp if using regular table salt) -- 1 tsp ground cinnamon -- ½ cup melted coconut oil (or olive oil for a more savory edge) -- ½ cup maple syrup (or honey for a richer flavor) -- 1 tsp vanilla extract -- Add after baking: ⅔ cup dried fruit, chopped if needed (like dried cranberries, raisins, or apricots)

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat your oven to 350°F and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper. -- In a large mixing bowl, combine oats, nuts/seeds, salt, and cinnamon. -- Pour in melted coconut oil, maple syrup (or honey), and vanilla. Stir it all up until everything is nicely coated. -- Spread the mixture evenly onto the baking sheet. -- Bake for 22–25 minutes, stirring halfway through. It should be golden and your house will smell incredible. -- Let cool completely—this is when it gets extra crispy! -- Once cool, stir in your dried fruit and any other add-ins. -- Store in an airtight jar or container for up to 2 weeks. (But let’s be real, it probably won’t last that long.)

recipeNotes.en-US:
To boost the the nutritional content add 4 tbsps chia seeds and 1/2 cup pumpkin seeds prior to baking. 

Want big clumps? Press the granola down with a spatula before baking and don’t stir too much.

Keep it seasonal: add pumpkin spice in fall, lemon zest in spring, or switch up your nuts and fruit based on what you have.

Makes a thoughtful homemade gift in a pretty jar with a ribbon.

description.en-US:
This juice is so delicious and refreshing, it will leave you feeling energized and light. The perfect combination of cucumber, green apple, kale and lemon brings a delightful sweet yet sour taste to your senses.

To obtain the most nutrition and to prevent the growth of bacteria, it is best to consume the juice right away after juicing it. 

Cheers to health!

ingredients.en-US: 1 lemon (I cut the skin off)--1 medium sized cucumber--2 green apples--3 large leaves of kale (with stems)

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash all the produce. Cut the produce to fit into the juicer. -- Turn on the juicer and add the produce. I juice in the order: cucumber, lemon, apple, and kale. Drink immediately for greatest nutrient benefits.

description.en-US:
This is such a great smoothie to consume after a workout. The ingredients listed here are for one smoothie but I always add a little extra cause my children love it as well (as for the hubby, he HATES strawberries, so... more for me). 

In this smoothie there are many healthful ingredients that can help nourish your body and muscles, and decrease post workout inflammation. 

Let's break this powerhouse down: 
1. Berries: are a great addition to any 'diet' they are full of antioxidants, fiber and other great nutrients. They not only have a lower sugar content than most other fruits, they also taste amazing! 
2. Flax milk: compared to many other non-dairy milks, flax milk is very creamy which is why it is my milk of choice when it comes to smoothies. It contains omega-3 fatty acids and is naturally lactose and cholesterol free.
3. Hemp seeds: Did you know that in 3 tbsps of hemp seeds there is 10g of protein, what a powerful seed! Also within that serving you can nourish your body with manganese, magnesium, healthy fats, iron, thiamin, phosphorus, etc. 
4. Turmeric root: Turmeric has hit the mainstream market recently but is well dated from the ayurvedic diet. I began using turmeric in my smoothies about 11 years ago due to its immune boosting and anti-inflammatory properties (activated when paired with black pepper). I have continued to use it since then as I truly feel it has helped me with post workout recovery.  

I love wholesome food and I love making my food work for me. When you understand food at a micro nutritional level (I really try), you can truly appreciate what and how certain foods can provide the needed nutrients at specific times. I really hope you enjoy this blend!

Cheers!

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 inch piece turmeric root--1/3 cup frozen blueberries--1/3 cup frozen cherries--1/3 cup frozen strawberries--1 serving vanilla or plain protein powder (optional)--2/3 cup flax milk--2 tbsp hemp seeds--dash of black pepper (you cannot taste it)--handful of almonds (about 15)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to the blender and blend til smooth. -- Feel free to add more flax milk to reach desired consistency!

description.en-US:
The holidays just aren't the holidays without... GINGERBREAD cookies! And don't you worry, this amazing recipe not only has clean ingredients, it tastes ahhhhmazing. We bake plenty so we can gift to our neighbors (and Santa of course). So feel free to cut this recipe in half if needed.

This year I handed over the icing decor duty to my 6 and 3 year olds and they did an amazing job. We had a few missing legs and half bitten heads but we accomplished the mission!

Note: if not vegan swap the coconut oil for butter.

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup coconut oil at room temp (with form, not liquid)--1/2 cup coconut sugar--1/2 cup molasses--1/4 cup water--1/4 tsp sea salt--1 tsp baking soda (I use Bob's)--2 cups white spelt flour (I use Vita Spelt)--2 tsp baking powder--2 tsp ground cinnamon--2 tsp ground ginger--3/4 tsp nutmeg

recipeDirections.en-US: In a medium bowl combine all the dry ingredients.--In a medium sized bowl, using an electric mixer, combine the formed coconut oil (or butter) and coconut sugar. Mix on a low setting. Add the water and molasses.--Now gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet. Continue to beat on low setting until combined.--Divide the dough in half, form into disks and wrap them, then place them into the freezer for an hour (or in the fridge overnight).--Preheat the oven to 350° and line a baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper.--Remove one disk at a time (from the freezer), add a little flour to it and roll it out to a thickness of 1/4 inch. Use you desired cookie cutters and then add the cookies to the baking sheet.--Bake for 11 mins at 350°.--Place them on a cooling rack to chill.--If decorating be sure to chill completely before adding the icing or it will melt.

description.en-US:
Chai is such a soothing hot beverage, it is the perfect blend of herbs and spices.  Chai originated many years ago in the ayurvedic kitchen, which is why you find amazing functional ingredients like pepper, cardamom, cinnamon, nutmeg, etc. Traditionally people used dairy milk but I also enjoy swapping mine with almond, coconut or in moderation soy. 

If you want some extra caffeine, make a dirty ('Dirty Chai') by adding some espresso shots. 

For extra flavor, allow the chai to sit after cooking (5-10 mins), it will get darker and the flavor far more rich and delicious. 

Happppppy Sipping!!!

ingredients.en-US:
1 cup of water -- 1 cinnamon stick, split (to release the flavor) -- 2 black tea bags -- 3 cardamom pods, split open -- 4 whole cloves -- 11/2 cups milk of choice 
 -- sweetener of choice (I prefer maple syrup)

recipeDirections.en-US: In a medium saucepan over medium heat, add the water and all other ingredients except the milk and maple syrup. Bring to a boil (4-5 minutes). Stir throughout. -- Add the milk of choice and bring it to a boil. Stir often. Turn the heat off. Allow it to sit for 5-10 mins for extra flavor. -- Using a mesh strainer (remove all the spices, etc), pour the tea mixture into cups and serve. Sweeten as desired. -- If you like, froth some milk and add to the top of the cups when serving.

description.en-US:
Anyone else have an abundance of Pumpkin Spice sitting in the front row in their spice shelf?! I sure do, and what better way is there to use up that magical spice (beside pumpkin pie)... Granola of course! 

Often I will stay up late at night and create this delicious granola recipe to use during the week. I get it, the holidays are busy, it's nice to have an easy breakfast/snack option to eat when time is limited. 

I make extra to package up and share with friends and coworkers over the holiday! 

Hope you enjoy it!

ingredients.en-US: 4 cups oats (gluten-free if need)--1/2 tsp sea salt--1 cup raw pumpkin seeds--1 tsp pumpkin spice--1 1/2 tsp vanilla--1/2 cup coconut oil--1/2 cup maple syrup--1 cup raisins--1 cup shredded unsweetened coconut

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350° and line a large baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper. -- In a medium bowl, add all the dry ingredients except the raisins and shredded coconut. Mix and set aside. -- On the stove top, in a medium size pan - add the coconut oil and maple syrup. Stir well until the coconut oil is melted (about 3 mins). Add the melted oil to the bowl of dry ingredients. Mix well until all oats and nuts are covered with the oil/syrup mix. --  Pour the granola onto the baking sheet and bake for 28 minutes at 350°. Stir at the halfway mark. If you like you shredded coconut toasted add it now (otherwise wait until the end) --  Remove from the oven and allow it to cool. Do not stir if you would like it to have clusters. -- Add the coconut and raisins and store in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks.

description.en-US:
Butternut squash is such a fun food, its vibrant color and sweet, unique taste makes it a great addition to a veggie bowl or as a taco topping. It is  packed with vitamins and minerals, making it a nutrient dense food. Why wouldn't you consider adding this squash to your diet?

Whether you choose to eat it on its own, as a side, or in one of my delicious quinoa bowls. You cannot go wrong.

I often hear people tell me they are not sure how to cook squash (how to peel, how to cut, etc.). Simply use a potato peeler to remove the skin, chop the ends off, slice it in half and remove the seeds. Then chop it into cubes, season it, and bake it for about 30-35 mins at 400° - voila!

ingredients.en-US: 1 medium butternut squash (peeled and cubed)--2 tbsp avocado oil--1 tbsp Herbs de Provence (optional)--sea salt and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 400° and line a baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper.--Peel the butternut squash using a peeler and slice it in half. Remove the seeds. Cut the squash into 1/2 inch cubes.--Place the cubed squash and all other ingredients to a bowl and mix so that all the pieces of squash are covered with oil and spices.--Add the squash to the baking pan and place into the over for 30-35 mins.--Remove from the oven and enjoy them warm as is or add them to a rice bowl, quinoa bowl, or as a taco topping.

description.en-US:
Carrot orange ginger juice is a bright, nourishing blend made with naturally sweet carrots, fresh orange, and a hint of warming ginger. Smooth, refreshing, and vibrant, it’s a simple way to bring both flavor and nourishment into your day.

Rich in vitamin A and vitamin C, this juice supports skin health, vision, and immune function, while ginger adds a gentle warmth and digestive-supporting touch. Light yet satisfying, it’s a beautiful way to incorporate more whole, plant-based ingredients into your routine.

Perfect as a morning refresh, post-workout boost, or midday pick-me-up, this juice pairs effortlessly with breakfast or can be enjoyed on its own as a simple, feel-good ritual. For a more balanced option, you can blend the ingredients with water to create a smoothie and retain more of the natural fiber.

## Benefits of Carrot Orange Ginger Juice

- May support immune health

- Rich in antioxidants

- May promotes healthy skin

- Naturally energizing

ingredients.en-US: 1 grapefruit, peeled--2 inch piece of ginger root, skin on--2 inch piece turmeric, skin on--6 oranges, peeled--8 carrots, top cut off--dash of black pepper (you will not taste it)

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash and prepare all the produce. -- Juice the produce and enjoy the juice immediately

recipeNotes.en-US: Do not skip the black pepper, it is needed to help with the absorption of curcumin from the turmeric. I have read several articles that claim turmeric and black pepper have key ingredients that when combined may contribute to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and disease-fighting properties.

metaDescription.en-US: A refreshing carrot orange ginger juice made with fresh ingredients and naturally packed with vitamin A and C. This easy, healthy juice may support energy, skin health, and overall wellness.

description.en-US:
I was asked to share more smoothie recipes, so I decided to start the new year by sharing 7 delicious smoothie recipes... Day 1!

This recipe here is our Blueberry Spinach Refresher. It contains blueberries (high in nutrients and antioxidants), spinach (high in vitamins, minerals, spinach - think popeye), ginger, turmeric, lemon, etc.

Feel free to decrease the ginger to 1/2 inch piece or eliminate if you are not a fan. 

Happy sipping!!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cup raw spinach--1/2 lemon, juiced--1 cup frozen blueberries--1 inch piece fresh turmeric root--1 inch piece ginger root--1 tbsp hemp seeds--dash of fresh ground black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a high powered blender, blend until smooth.--Enjoy right away.

recipeNotes.en-US: Blender

description.en-US:
If you are looking for a fresh and delicious smoothie you have come to the right place... This green beauty is such a refreshing way to start your morning 'on the right sip.'

We made this smoothie recipe up on a whip today and it turned out amazing! 

Feel free to swap some of the ingredients if you don't have them on hand but I assure you, this exact recipe is one darn good blend to add to your blender! 

Sip, sip, HOORRAAYY!

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 avocado--1/2 banana--1 cup frozen mango--1 cup spinach--1 cup water--1 medium pear--handful of fresh cilantro

recipeDirections.en-US: Place all ingredients into a blender, blend until smooth. (Adjust water as needed)

description.en-US:
Who doesn't love pancakes... We grew up enjoying "Pancake Sunday" brunches with the family. Mountains of delicious fluffy pancakes topped with fresh fruit and pure Canadian maple syrup.

I wanted to carry over the memories for my babies but altered our recipe to include healthier alternatives. I replaced the base with spelt flour and removed the milk, sugar and eggs for a vegan friendly version.

I often double this recipe and keep a few extra in the freezer (separated by unbleached parchment paper in an airtight container) for a quick grab and go breakfast option.

Enjoy!!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups flour (I use spelt flour)--1 tbsp baking powder-- 1/4 tsp salt -- 1 1/2 cup nondairy milk (I prefer coconut or almond)--2 tbsp maple syrup--2 tbsp coconut oil, melted--coconut oil, for cooking--fresh fruit (optional)--maple syrup, for serving

recipeDirections.en-US: Turn on a skillet over medium low heat. Add the coconut oil and melt (I have had great luck with my cast iron pan but any one should work fine).--In a medium sized bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.--Add the non dairy milk, 1 tbsp maple syrup, and coconut oil and mix until the batter is smooth.--Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup add the batter to the pan. When you see bubbles form (approx 2 minutes) flip the pancake. Cook until golden (about 2 minutes).--Serve with fresh fruit topping and/or drizzle some pure maple syrup.

recipeNotes.en-US: All flours are a bit different, you may need to adjust the milk (add 1 tbsp of milk a time to reach desired consistency).-- May use regular spelt or unbleached white spelt flour.

metaDescription.en-US: Who doesn't love pancakes... Delicious and healthy pancakes made with spelt flour, milk of choice, and sweetened with pure maple syrup.  A easy to make, vegan friendly pancake the whole family will love!

description.en-US:
My first time having Kale salad was at a Whole Foods salad bar, I was hooked! I had to create my own delicious recipe and make it a staple in our home.

The secret to a good kale salad is tearing apart the leaves with your hands and then massaging the broken leaves with the dressing (for a minute or two). Doing this helps breakdown the cell structure and makes it softer to chew.

Feel free to swap the walnuts for another nut of choice. Toasted pine nuts or almonds pair well.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups kale, stemmed and broken into bitesize pieces--1/2 medium avocado, just the flesh--1-2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil--1 lemon, juiced--1/4 tsp sea salt-- black pepper--2 cloves garlic, minced--1/4 cup red onion, diced--1 cup red grapes, quartered--1/4 cup walnuts, toasted

recipeDirections.en-US: Remove the stem from the kale and tear it into bite size pieces. Place in a medium size bowl.--In a small bowl, smash the avocado and mix in the olive oil, lemon juice, sea salt, pepper, and minced garlic. Add it to the kale - massage it into the leaves for a few minutes.--Add the onion, grapes and toasted walnuts.--Serve and enjoy!

description.en-US:
After every workout, I always blend together a smoothie to help repair and nourish my body. Today I was craving blackberries after my workout so I used them as the base for the smoothie. 

You'll see a lot of delicious smoothie recipes on Bobbieleelicious since we are smoothie lovers. Let's be honest, is there an easier way to get this much nutrition into your (and the kiddos) body? 

Smoothies are one of the number one recipes that I am asked for, so thank you, and you are so very welcome! I get to create, sip, and then share the goodness! 

Happy sipping!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups flax milk (or milk of choice)--1/2 inch piece ginger root (optional)--1 banana--1 cup blackberries (preferably frozen)--1 cup frozen mango--1 cup spinach--2 tbsp chia seeds--2 tbsp hemp seeds--Vanilla or plain protein powder (I used Vega brand, vanilla)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to the blender, blend until smooth. --  Adjust the liquid as desired.--Enjoy!

description.en-US:
Pizza, is there anyone in the world who doesn't LOVE pizza?? Seriously! 

You see, my husband was born and raised in Chicago, so I was determined to find an "OMG that's delicious" pizza recipe that satisfied his OBP (obsessive pizza disorder). Trust me it exists! 

Initially, I would get so overwhelmed when learning how to make the perfect pizza but over time we have come up with a quick, easy, delicious, good for you crust!

What makes this crust better than the others I share is the rise time... All it takes is setting the dough, let it rise for about 10 minutes while you dance around the kitchen and cut some toppings and then, voila!! Your dough is ready for the oven. 

Are you ready for a pizza parrrtaay?

ingredients.en-US: *Crust -- 3/4 cup warm water, between 110-115° F (may need to add 1-3 extra tbsps) -- 3/4 tsp honey (or cane sugar) -- 1/2 tsp active yeast powder -- 1 1/2 cups white spelt flour (or a bread flour works well also) -- 1/2 tsp sea salt --*Toppings -- Pizza sauce -- Mozzarella cheese -- Tomatoes -- Basil

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 425°F. -- In a medium sized bowl, add the water and honey (or cane sugar), give it a stir. Sprinkle on the yeast and set it aside (allow it to sit 8-10 minutes). -- Add the flour and sea salt to the mixture and mix it with your hand until the flour is mixed in, form a ball of dough. Knead for a few minutes. -- Cover the bowl with a towel and allow it to rest for 6-7 minutes. -- Prepare your workspace by dusting some flour onto a surface. Using your hands and rolling pin, roll the dough out to your desired thickness (I personally feel pressing the dough with your hands creates a better crust). Transfer the pizza to a pizza pan. -- Using a fork, make some holes into the dough. -- Place the pizza into the oven and bake for 4 minutes. Remove the pizza from the oven. -- Spread the pizza sauce onto the pizza and top with desired toppings. Return the pizza to the oven and bake for 12-15 mins (option: for the last 5 minutes place the pizza pan on the top rack in the oven). The cheese should be slightly browned.  Remove from the oven. -- Enjoy!

description.en-US:
Another lunch... another salad! 

Arugula is one of my favourite salad greens. I love its distinct fresh and peppery taste. It is also a total nutritional rock star with its impressive amount of Vitamin C, Vitamin A, Vitamin K, and folate.

This fresh salad has many nutritious ingredients that I absolutely love.
AVOCADO, give me all them healthy fats... PINE NUTS, oh that creamy, crunchy goodness... BEETS, need I say more?! I love beets so it's a bonus that they are loaded with 'good for you' vitamins and minerals. Many articles mention how beets can help improve ones athletic performance due to its dietary nitrates (aids in energy production and oxygen consumption). 

So why not make a super delicious recipe that not only tastes ahhhhmazing but fuels your body!

For this salad I purchased a jar of (small batch) beets from the local market but you can also make your own.

ingredients.en-US: 2 tbsp balsamic vinegar--1 1/2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil -- Sea Salt and black pepper to taste -- 1 1/2 cup arugula--1/4 cup red onion, chopped -- 1/4 cup cucumber, chopped -- 1/2 avocado, slice--Kalamata olives--pickled beets (3-4 slices per serving)--Pine nuts (a small handful per serving)

recipeDirections.en-US: In a small bowl, add the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, salt and pepper - mix and set aside.--In a medium size bowl, add the arugula, red onion, and cucumber - combine.--Add the dressing to they salad and serve into individual bowls (or one big bowl, my style).--Top with avocado, olives, pine nuts, beets.--Bon appetite!

description.en-US:
If you like Indian food and you like scrambled eggs, look no further... I have a delicious fusion recipe you definitely want to try out! 

This vegan and gluten free tofu scramble is spiced with the perfect amounts of garlic and ginger, cumin, turmeric and chili powder (optional). The crumbled tofu tastes very similar to a scrambled egg (have even tricked my kids)... Go ahead and try it, you'll see what I am talking about. 

Whether you are vegan or looking for some meat and egg free meals to add to your menu, do yourself a favor and add this scramble to your plate.

ingredients.en-US: 1-2 cloves garlic, minced--1/2 cup baby spinach--1/2 cup yellow onion, diced--1/2 inch piece ginger, peeled and minced--1/8-1/4 tsp chili powder (optional)--1 package firm organic tofu, drained (14 oz)--2 tbsp avocado oil--3/4 tsp cumin seeds--3/4 tsp turmeric powder--sea salt and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: Heat your pan over medium-high heat. -- While the pan is warming up, press the tofu between 2 sheets of paper towel - this will soak up any excess liquid. Set aside. -- Once the pan is warm, add the oil and cumin seeds, cook for 30 secs, stir often to prevent them from burning. -- Now add the ginger, garlic and onion. Cook until the onion is translucent (4-5mins), stir often. -- Now add the tofu, crumbling it with your fingers as you add it to the pan. -- Add the turmeric and chili powder (optional), stir it around til the yellow hue from the turmeric covers the tofu. Cook for about 5 minutes. -- Add the salt and pepper to taste. -- While the pan remains on the heat, add the spinach and cook until wilted (1-2 mins), stirring it. -- Serve with a side of vegetables, avocado, toast and/or roasted potatoes.

description.en-US:
Smoothie bowls have been a thing in our home for quite some time, this one here remains one of our all time favorites. 

The base stays the same but we play around with the toppings to fit individual preferences. 

I typically use the Sambazon brand of frozen Acai but any kind should work.
Top with any fruit you desire, dried coconut, nuts, seeds, and/or granola (check out my granola recipes).

Whether you choose to enjoy as a breakfast or dessert option, be creative with the toppings. 

If you are making it for children, set the toppings out and let them decorate and create their own healthy and nutritious masterpiece!

ingredients.en-US: 1 100g packet unsweetened frozen acai berry (Sambazon brand)--1/2 cup frozen mango--1/2 frozen banana--1/4 cup flax milk (or nondairy milk of choice)

recipeDirections.en-US: In a high powered blender, add the acai berry, banana, flax milk, and mango.--Blend until smooth, may need to add 1-2 tbsp of flax milk to reach desired consistency.--Pour in bowl and top with desired toppings: fresh fruit, hemp seeds, nuts, granola, and/or dried coconut.--Serve immediately!

description.en-US:
Who says you can't have your donuts and eat them too? Our healthier vanilla donuts are here to satisfy your sweet tooth and keep you feeling good about your choices! Packed with wholesome ingredients like spelt flour, coconut sugar, and dairy-free milk, these donuts are light, fluffy, and full of flavor without the guilt. Whether you're craving a fun weekend treat or looking to impress at your next brunch, these goodies are baked, not fried, and come with a rich chocolate glaze that'll have everyone reaching for seconds.

The best part? You can personalize these little rings of happiness with your favorite toppings—think nuts, sprinkles, or even a sprinkle of cacao nibs for that extra crunch. Plus, if you're gluten-free, we’ve got you covered with an easy swap. So why not whip up a batch? They're quick, easy, and a healthier way to indulge your inner donut lover!

Grab your donut pan, a piping bag, a fancy apron, and let the baking begin—you’ll have donuts that are just as delicious as they are good(ish) for you.

ingredients.en-US:
*For the Donut: -- 1 cup white spelt flour (or other flour of choice) - 1/3 cup coconut sugar -- 1 tsp baking powder -- 1/4 tsp baking soda -- 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg--1/4 tsp sea salt--2/3 cup milk of choice  -- 2 tbsp melted coconut oil -- 1 tsp vanilla extract -- 1 tsp apple cider vinegar -- 
*For the glaze: -- 1/2 cup chocolate chips -- 1/2 tsp coconut oil -- 
*Optional Toppings: -- Chopped nuts, shredded coconut, sprinkles, or cacao nibs

recipeDirections.en-US:
Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly grease a 6-hole donut pan with coconut oil.--Prepare the dry ingredients: In a large bowl, whisk together the spelt flour (or other flour), coconut sugar, baking powder, baking soda, nutmeg, and sea salt. -- Mix the wet ingredients: In a separate bowl, combine the non-dairy milk, melted coconut oil, vanilla extract, and apple cider vinegar. -- Combine the mixtures: Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients. Stir until just combined, being careful not to over-mix as this can make the donuts dense. -- Fill the donut pan: Spoon the batter into a freezer or pastry bag. Pipe the batter evenly into the prepared donut pan. Fill each cavity about 3/4 full. -- Bake the donuts: Bake for 12-14 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into a donut comes out clean. Remove from the oven and let the donuts cool in the pan for 5 minutes. -- Cool completely: Gently tap the bottom of the donut pan to release the donuts. Transfer to a wire rack and let them cool completely before glazing.
-- Prepare the glaze: In a small bowl, melt the vegan chocolate chips and coconut oil in the microwave or using a double boiler. Stir until smooth. -- Glaze the donuts: Dip each donut halfway into the chocolate glaze, allowing the excess to drip off. Return the glazed donuts to the wire rack and add your desired toppings before the glaze sets. -- Serve and enjoy: Let the glaze firm up for a few minutes before serving. Enjoy your healthier donuts!

metaDescription.en-US: Bake a batch of delicious, healthier vanilla donuts made with spelt flour, coconut sugar, and dairy-free milk! These baked donuts are light, fluffy, and topped with a rich vegan chocolate glaze—perfect for guilt-free indulgence!

description.en-US:
Is there a person in the world who doesn't love hummus? Creamy, satisfyingly delicious hummus. How can anyone love this?!

Hummus is an everyday snack item in our home. I send it to school with my son for his lunches to use as a dip for his veggies. I use it as a spread on sandwiches and wraps, and also add it to my mediteranean salad bowls... Hummus is a simple and delicious way to bump up some extra protein to any meal.

For this recipe you can soak and cook your own chickpeas (I recommend) or you can use canned chickpeas. 

This hummus can be stored in the fridge for 4-5 days in an airtight container. 

Happy dipping!

ingredients.en-US: 1/2-2/3 cup dried chickpeas (soaked and cooked =1 1/2 cups cooked)--1/2 tsp paprika--1/4 cup tahini--1/8 cup pine nuts (or hemp seeds)--1 lemon, juiced--2-3 cloves garlic (love garlic = 3 clove)--2 tbsp olive oil--salt to taste (I use sea salt)

recipeDirections.en-US: Place all ingredients into a food processor or high powered blender (start with just 1 1/2 cups of the cooked chickpeas) and blend until smooth. Stop occasionally and scrape down the sides.--If you need a more creamy consistency, add 1 tbsp of tahini at a time until desired consistency is reached (max 3 tbsp extra). Taste and adjust salt and spices as need.--Plate the hummus, garnish, and serve!

description.en-US:
Papaya is one of those fruits that I never truly enjoyed until I was in my 20's. I tried my very first papaya smoothie at a Vietnamese restaurant - it was love at first sip! I only wish I would have given this delicious fruit a chance earlier!  

Over the years, I created my own healthier version to this smoothie, using more wholesome ingredients to boost its nutritional value. 

Papaya is rich in fiber, vitamin C, vitamin A and antioxidants. Pair this amazing fruit with some ginger, turmeric, flax seeds and hemp seeds and you have yourself an amazingly healthy smoothie.

Happy sipping!

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup ice--1/2 frozen banana--1/2 inch piece ginger root (optional)--1/2 inch piece turmeric root--1/2 lemon, juiced--1/2 orange--1 cup coconut water--1 cup fresh papaya (peeled and seeded)--1 tbsp flaxseeds (may use oil)--1 tbsp hemp seeds

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to the blender and blend until smooth. -- Enjoy immediately

description.en-US:
Bonjour... Hello! Welcome to my crepe recipe... (with the Frenchiest French accent ever)

Crepes!! I loooooooove crepes, my son calls them “100% delicious” and I would have to agree with him.

For this recipe I used white spelt flour but feel free to swap for your flour of choice. 

I fill these crepes with fresh fruit and sprinkle with cacao powder, however, you can top as you like. Get creative, set the littles up with bowls of fruit and let them fill their own.

Bon appétit mon ami ❤️

Note: For a savory crepe skip the maple syrup
For a delicious GF option see my [buckwheat crepes](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/buckwheat-pancakes) (my personal favorite)

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup milk (I used oat milk)--1 cup white spelt flour--1 tsp butter, melted--1 tsp maple syrup--1 tsp vanilla extract--2 eggs--pinch of sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a blender, blend until smooth OR in a medium bowl, whisk all ingredients until smooth. Set aside for 25 minutes.--Preheat a frying pan or crepe pan over medium heat. (De Buyer crepe pan is what I use)--Add some butter or coconut oil and allow it to melt.--Add about 1/4 cup of batter at a time to the pan. Give it a swirl or use your crepe tool to distribute the batter evenly.--Cook each side for 1 1/2 minutes. I often have to discard the first crepe.--Continue this method until all crepes are complete.--Fill as desired and serve immediately!

description.en-US:
Another day, another juice! And this delicious juice here will help get you moving!

I love kale so much that I grow it in the garden. Depending on where you live, it can be grown fairly easily. Learning how to cook and use Kale is essential. It can be bitter if not paired correctly, so play with the ingredient amounts if this one doesn't fancy your tastebuds... The lemon in this juice helps cut the bitterness and the apple gives it just the right amount of sweetness. 

Sip, adjust and sip again until you reach your juicy perfection!

Cheers!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cucumber--1 green apple--1 inch piece fresh ginger--1 lemon, peel removed--2 celery stalks--6 kale, large leaves

recipeDirections.en-US: Run all the ingredients through a juicer (I use the Omega 8006).--Drink immediately.

description.en-US:
With Valentines Day coming up, this perfect plant based chocolate dipping sauce is just what you need to make the day extra SWEET (while keeping it healthy)!
