Plant based CHOCOLATE?? Yes, maam! Let me explain! Cacao is a bean that grows on a tree... maple syrup comes from the sap of maple trees... coconut oil comes from a coconut - you are so very welcome!

I used this sauce as a dip and drizzle for fruit, poured some in some molds and added nutritious topping like hemp seeds, almonds, macadamia nuts etc... Endless possibilities, my friend.

ingredients.en-US: 1/4 cup (+1 tbsp) cacao or cocoa powder--1/4 cup coconut oil, melted--2 tbsp pure maple syrup

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash and prepare fruit and toppings of choice. If using fruit wash and allow about 30 mins to completely dry or the chocolate will not rest right.--In a medium bowl, add all the ingredients and mix well until combine.--Dip or drizzle as desired.--Enjoy!

description.en-US:
What is a Shhhh-Smoothie, you ask... It is a smoothie with hidden ingredients! 

Let me explain, my children will not touch cauliflower no matter what I try, and trust me, I have tried many creative ways to get it into their diets. 

One day I decided to add it to their smoothies and it was a hit! I hide it in this delicious sneaky "Shhhh-Smoothie" and am now sharing this delicious recipe with you.

This recipe is a fairly new one in our home and my children often ask for seconds. 😃

I hope you enjoy it!

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 banana--1/2 cup flax milk (or milk of choice)--1/2 cup frozen blueberries--1/2 cup frozen cauliflower--1/2 lemon, juiced--1 tbsp hemp seeds

recipeDirections.en-US: Place all ingredients into a blender, blend until smooth!

description.en-US:
This Vegetarian Chili is one that even my non-vegetarian friends love!
It is hearty, has a bit of spice (eliminate the chili powder if you want less spice), has a variety of vegetables and beans for our family of 4, and we have plenty leftover to use and get creative with during the week.
I use dried beans, 1/2 cup dry turns out to about 1 - 1 1/4 cooked (you can swap the dry for a can, drained and rinsed)
To cook dry beans: I soak them overnight in water and then pressure cook them in the instant pot for 24 mins.

Leftover/Serving ideas:
1. Add as a burrito filling
2. Quinoa/Chili bowl
3. Makes a great layer for 3 or 5 layer beans dip (nacho dip)
4. Sloppy Joes
Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 tsp dried oregano--1/2-1 tsp chili powder (optional)--1/2 cup dried black beans (soaked and cooked)--1/2 cup dried chickpeas (soaked and cooked)--1/2 cup dried kidney beans (soaked and cooked)-1 large can diced tomatoes--1 large green pepper, chopped--1 medium yellow onion, chopped--1 tsp sea salt--2 cups liquid (water or leftover from the cooked beans)--2 tbsp avocado oil--2 tsp ground cumin--2 tsp paprika--3 medium carrots, chopped--3 tbsp cilantro, chopped--4 cloves garlic, pressed--black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare all the ingredients as listed. If using dried beans, be sure to soak them overnight and cook them fully (I pressure cook all the beans together in the instant pot for 24-26 min)--In a large pot over medium heat, add the avocado oil until it simmers. Then add the green pepper, onions, carrots, garlic, salt and pepper. Cook for 8-9 minutes, until the onions are translucent - stir often to prevent it from burning to the bottom of the pot.--Add the chili, oregano, paprika and cumin. Stir it in with the vegetables.--Add the can of tomatoes with the liquid, all the cooked beans (black, kidney and chickpea), and 2 cups of water (preferably liquid from the cooked beans). Stir to combine. Allow the chili to come to a simmer, stir it and turn the heat down just a bit. Allow it to cook for 30-35 minutes, stir it occasionally.--Once finished, remove the pot from the heat and smash some of the beans with the back of a spoon or add 1 cup of the mixture to a blender, blend and return to the pot - this will give it a thicker more stew like consistency (a great trick I learned while making Rajma).--Serve the chili into individual bowls and garnish with cilantro, sour cream, cheese, hemp seeds and/or green onions.--Store in the fridge for 3-4 days.

recipeNotes.en-US: If using canned beans, decrease the sea salt used.

description.en-US:
This morning, we handed the kitchen reins to our mini maestros, and the result? "The Berry-Good Smoothie" – a creation as fresh and delightful as their imaginations! Packed with berries known for their skin-loving benefits, this smoothie is a tasty, nutrient-packed adventure perfect for breakfast or a midday snack.

Our little chefs chose a mix of antioxidant-rich berries that not only taste amazing but also bring a bundle of health benefits. With a punch of vitamins C, manganese, and K1, this Berry-Good Smoothie is a win
Berries are nutritious and well known for their skin and health benefits. They have a high antioxidant profile and contain a great amount of fiber. Berries are also high in vitamin C, manganese, vitamin K1, etc.

Here's to mornings filled with kitchen adventures, giggles, and the simple joy of a Berry-Good Smoothie! Let this kid-approved creation inspire your next flavorful journey because healthy living should always be this deliciously easy. Take a sip, enjoy the berry bliss, and make every day extraordinary! 

Enjoy!!!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup frozen blueberries--4-5 strawberries--1 cup frozen raspberries -- 1 1/5 cup milk of choice -- 2 tbsp hemp seeds--1 tbsp chia seeds--1 tbsp flaxseeds -- Optional: 1 cup spinach

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth! -- ENJOY!

metaDescription.en-US: Join the kitchen adventure with our Berry-Good Smoothie recipe! Crafted by creative kids, this delightful blend of fresh berries packs a punch of antioxidants, fiber, and vitamins. Perfect for a healthy breakfast or a wholesome snack.

description.en-US:
Like ice-cream only better! No dairy, just simple, good for you, plant based ingredients!

We have been making this for years! It all started from a Daniel Tiger show. My son was watching an episode and the mama tiger was preparing some "banana swirl". My son requested that we make our own - and so it began!

We have so many variations and we will continue to share the frozen-sweet-swirly love!

For ready to use frozen goodness, freeze the peeled bananas when they are ripe.

ingredients.en-US:
*Ice Cream--2 medium frozen bananas--1 cup frozen cherries--4-6 tbsp nut milk of choice (I used my homemade almond milk)--pinch of sea salt--
*Toppings--fresh fruit--freeze dried fruit--granola--nuts and seeds--chocolate chips--etc.

recipeDirections.en-US: Add the frozen bananas, frozen cherries, salt and 4 tbsps of milk of choice to the blender and blend till smooth. Should resemble the texture of a soft serve ice cream.--Serve immediately or put it back in the freezer for 20-40 minutes to regain the ice creamy texture.--Top with desired toppings and devour!

description.en-US:
Have you heard about the amazing benefits of green tea? Then you will enjoy this matcha beverage, a powdered form of green tea. 

Matcha is rich in antioxidants and many studies have tied it to protecting against certain diseases and also anti-aging benefits. 

This Almond Matcha Milk is easy to make and has many other nutrient dense ingredients, making it a great midday beverage for a bump in energy and nutrients.

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup almond milk--1/2 cup spinach--1/2 banana--1 tsp matcha--1 tbsp hempseed--1 cup ice--monk fruit or a date, to sweeten (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to the blender, blend until smooth and enjoy!

description.en-US:
Hellllo cookie dough!

Do you love to snack? Of course, who doesn’t! Especially when it’s cookie dough, right? 
Now you can snack on something tasty and healthy, and you will be guilt free.

So look no further, because these Cookie Dough Balls will satisfy those "cookie dough cravings''. Chaaaa-chinng!!  

Seven wholesome ingredients into one medium mixing bowl. Ready friends? Let's go!

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup salted peanut butter (be sure ingredients are just peanuts and salt)--2 tbsp pure maple syrup--1/4 cup coconut flour--1 tbsp coconut oil, melted--1 tbsp unsweetened shredded coconut--1 tbsp hemp seeds--4 tbsp cacao nibs (may swap for chocolate chips)--1tbsp chia seeds (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a medium sized bowl. Mix well (using your hand) until all ingredients are combined.--Form into balls (7-8 balls).--Keep the balls in an airtight container, stored in the fridge.

description.en-US:
I was introduced to these lovely smashed potatoes by a dear friend, they are the perfect appetizer for any occasion. 

This recipe here, I seasoned the potatoes with garlic, chives, paprika, oregano, avocado oil, salt and pepper. I will most definitely adjust the flavors with different spices and create a variety of flavor bursting crispy potato options - I will continue to share as we create, so if you love these potatoes be sure to try the others!! 

Serving options: as a side, filling on a wrap or burger, or topped on a salad.

ingredients.en-US: *Smashed Potatoes: -- 1 1/2 lb small red or yellow potatoes -- 4-5 tbsps avocado oil -- 1 tbsp sea salt -- 1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper -- 3 cloves of garlic minced -- 1 tbsp oregano -- 3 tbsps chives or green onions -- smoked paprika --*Optional Toppings: -- chives, green onions, thyme, shredded cheese, chili flakes, plain yogurt or sour cream.

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 425° F. -- Fill a large pot with water, bring it to a boil and add the potatoes. Cook them for approx 15-20 mins, they should still be a bit firm and not too soft (a fork should pierce them). -- In a small bowl, mix the avocado oil, garlic, oregano, sea salt and black pepper together. -- Once the potatoes are cooked, drain the water and place them on a lined baking sheet (I prefer using unbleached parchment paper). -- Using a fork or a potato masher, smash the potatoes. I often peel back some of the skins as it gives them a more crispy texture. -- Using a spoon or brush, add the oil mixture to the smashed potatoes. Sprinkle on some paprika to the potatoes. -- Place the baking sheet into the preheated oven, place on the bottom rack. Bake for approx 25-30 minutes, the tops should be crispy. -- Serve them immediately.

description.en-US:
So the other day I was looking on amazon for ideas to add some natural blue coloring to our baked goods and I came across a product by Foraging Organics called Blue Butterfly Pea Powder. 

While doing my research, I discovered that Blue Butterfly Pea Flowers are native to Thailand and Malaysia, as well as other parts of Southeast Asia. The flowers are high in anthocyanin, an antioxidant found in blueberries and raspberries. Lastly, there are benefits in memory and brain function. Hmmmm - "add to cart". 

Well I just received this mysterious blue powder and added varying amounts to our smoothies today and they were a hit. They tasted great and the kids LOVED the vibrant blue color! 

I cannot wait to create more magical blue recipes!!

ingredients.en-US: 2 frozen bananas -- 1 cup frozen blueberries -- 1/2 - 3/4 cup nut milk (add to reach desired consistency) -- 1 tbsp hemp seeds -- 1 tbsp flaxseeds -- 1/2 lemon, juiced -- 1 tbsp blue butterfly pea powder, freeze dried (increase to reach desired color)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a blender, blend until smooth. -- Enjoy!!

description.en-US:
These pancake kabobs are a winner in our home, myself and the kids love putting them together (I am pretty sure deep down, my husband does as well). 

Simply make mini pancakes using either of my pancake recipes (or your own), wash and prepare some fresh fruit and voila - you have a delicious and healthy breakfast that the whole family will LOVE making and eating!  

Kabob sticks can be found at most local grocery stores or you can find them on amazon. 

Ready, set, KABOB time! 

Pancake recipes:
1. Kid-approved Spelt Flour Pancake, [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/kid-approved-spelt-flour-vegan-pancake)
2. Buckwheat Pancakes, [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/buckwheat-pancakes)
3. Oat Flour Pancakes, [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/oat-flour-pancakes)

ingredients.en-US: *Kabobs--mini pancakes (see either of my recipes) -- fresh strawberries, stem removed -- fresh blackberries -- fresh blueberries --*Toppings (optional):-- bananas, chocolate drizzle, maple syrup, cacao powder, nuts, etc.

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare all the ingredients listed and add to the kabob sticks. -- Top as desired and ENJOY!!!

metaDescription.en-US: If you are looking to impress the kiddos these pancake kabobs are what you are looking for. Like pancakes but on a kabob with fruit. Delicious!!

description.en-US:
I love the simplicity of these no bake cake balls, they taste amazing, are good for you, and they are so easy to make.

These carrot cake balls take less than 10 minutes to make and are the perfect "grab and go" snack when you are in a pinch for time.

The main ingredients in these healthy balls are carrots and medjool dates. Carrots are a good source of beta-carotene, fiber, potassium and antioxidants. To boost the nutrition in these balls we also added some hemp seeds.

If you like carrot cake, give these balls a try. I enjoy them out of the freezer, just remember to  thaw them for about 10 minutes prior to eating. They pair well with our Chai Tea recipe, see [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/masala-spice-chai).

Store in the freezer for up to 3 weeks.

ingredients.en-US: 5 medjool dates, pits removed -- 1 1/2 cups carrots, peeled and shredded -- 3/4 cup oat flour (GF if need) -- 1/4 cup rolled oats (GF if need) -- 3 tbsp raisins -- 1 tbsp hemp seeds -- 1/4 tsp cinnamon -- 1/8 tsp ginger -- 1/4 tsp nutmeg -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- unsweetened shredded coconut (to roll the balls in)

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare all the ingredients as noted in the list. -- In a medium sized bowl, add all the ingredients and mix with your hand until combined (the dates will form a paste like consistency). If using a processor for this step, process until combined. -- From the mixture, form 8-10 even sized balls. Roll the balls in some shredded coconut and then store them in the freezer in an airtight container. -- They store well in the freezer for up to 3 weeks.

recipeNotes.en-US: The Medjool dates should be soft. If your dates are hard, soak them in warm water for 20 minutes prior to using.

description.en-US:
Call it what you want, a Bounty Bar or a Mound, these delicious (vegan and GF friendly) chocolate covered bars of sweet shredded coconut are mouth watering delicious. 

In Canada, we grew up loving our Bounty bars. Inside you’d find two small chocolate covered coconut bars in the package. My mama would always share hers with me - it was the easiest way to bring an ear to ear smile on my face! 

I have seen many online versions of this bar but wanted to make a healthier version for my family to enjoy. I present you with our version of the delicious, mouth watering Bounty Bar! 

I store them in an airtight container in the freezer, they store well up to 3 weeks. I remove them about 5-10 minutes before eating.

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups shredded unsweetened coconut -- 4 tbsp full fat coconut milk -- 2 tbsp unrefined coconut oil -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 1 cup + 1 tbsp chocolate chips--*Toppings (optional):-- salt flakes, hemp seeds, shredded coconut, etc

recipeDirections.en-US: In a medium sized bowl, add the shredded coconut, coconut milk, coconut oil, and sea salt - stir to combine. -- Form into 7-8 small bars and place in the freezer for about 15 minutes. -- While the bars are in the freezer, carefully melt the chocolate chips. -- Remove the coconut bars from the freezer and dip them into the melted chocolate, cover each bar completely. -- Return them to the freezer for about 10 minutes until they firm up. -- Remove from the freezer approx 10 minutes before eating. Store in the freezer for up to 3 weeks.

description.en-US:
If you are looking for an easy to make, nutritious meal, look no further!
This dish is packed full of nutrient dense whole foods annnnnd you need just 1 pot to cook the meal!

I am a strong believer that food should provide you with energy, not send to the couch for a nap. Just take a peek at the ingredient list (go ahead, take a look...). See what I am talking about?! This bowl is a powerhouse!

We continue to adjust our ingredients, using whatever vegetables we have on hand, however, the proportions remain the same.

When I am short on time, I prepare this in advance. Then when hungry, all we need to do is grab, heat, and eat. A nutritious meal in minutes!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 tbsp avocado oil -- 1/3 cup yellow onion, diced -- 2 cloves garlic, minced -- serrano pepper, diced (optional) -- 3/4 tsp cumin seeds -- 1 tsp turmeric powder -- 1/2 tsp seal salt -- black pepper -- 3/4 cup chickpeas, cooked (or you can use can) -- 5 cherry tomatoes, diced -- 1/4 cup red bell pepper, diced -- 1 cup quinoa, rinsed -- 1/4 cup unsalted cashews, halved--*Toppings:--cilantro, fresh squeezed lime, yogurt, red onion, diced, etc

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare all ingredients -- Turn on instant pot to "saute" mode. Add the avocado oil, onion, garlic, and cumin seeds. Cook for 1 minute, continue to stir. -- Add all the other ingredients except for water, stir for about 2 mins. -- Add the water and close/seal the lid. Set on "Pressure Cook" for 1 minute, allowing the pressure to release naturally for 11 minutes. -- Serve ware and top with desired toppings. -- Keeps well in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.

description.en-US:
My husband loves pakoras, so it was only a matter of time before I recreated an alternative to his otherwise fried favorite! 

What is a pakora you ask?? It is a piece of vegetable deep fried in a batter made of chickpea flour and a variety of spices, originating from India. This popular appetizer is sold as street food, and can also be found at restaurants and weddings. 

Pakoras are often served with a chutney (mint, cilantro, etc), tamarind or raita (yogurt). Pair with a cold or hot beverage (masala chai is a great choice).

You can swap around spices and vegetables, making different variations of this delicious appetizer. 

These oven baked pakoras are a healthier alternative to the standard fried version. Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: *Batter--2 cups besan (chickpea) flour -- 1 tsp baking powder -- 1 tsp sea salt -- 2 tsp garam masala -- 1.5 tsp cumin powder -- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder -- 3/4 tsp crushed red pepper -- 3 cloves  fresh garlic, crushed-- 1/2 inch piece ginger, peeled and crushed-- 1 1/4 cup water, room temperature--*Vegetables--3 cups cauliflower, bite size -- 1 medium onion (yellow of red), chopped -- 1/2 cup peas (fresh or frozen) -- 1 large carrot, peeled and grated -- 1/4 cup green pepper, chopped -- 3/4 cup spinach, finely chopped

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat oven to 375°F. Using either a baking sheet or a cast iron baking pan (my go to), grease the baking surface using avocado oil or another mild cooking oil that has a high heat smoking point. -- In a large bowl, mix together the dry ingredients for the batter. Now stir in the water, the the batter should resemble pancake batter consistency. Set aside. -- Prepare all the vegetables as listed in the ingredients section. Add them to the batter, mix them well so that they are coated. -- Using a tablespoon, scoop the batter onto the baking surface. -- Bake for 11 minutes, flip them and bake for another 11 minutes. Brush them with a little avocado oil at this time. -- Serve them warm as an appetizer with chutney, tamarind or raita. -- Store in the fridge in an airtight container for 3-4 days. -- Enjoy!!!

recipeNotes.en-US: When baking the pakora, brush them with avocado oil to give them a "fried-like" texture.

metaDescription.en-US: Discover a healthier twist on traditional pakoras with our oven-baked vegetable pakoras recipe! Packed with grated carrots, zucchini, spinach, and aromatic spices, these crispy delights are bursting with flavor. Perfect as a nutritious snack or appetizer.

title.en-US: Arugula and Purple Cabbage Salad With Lime-Dijon Dressing

description.en-US:
Spring has sprung, here in Minnesota... and although I love salads all year round, bright and colorful salads are an essential for spring and summer! 

This beautiful and delicious salad will brighten up any plate and make "eating the rainbow" fun and delicious. 

Feel free to add some other nuts and/or swap ingredients. Quinoa and chickpeas are a great way to boost the protein content. 

This salad pairs well with our black bean burger (on the bbq) and some fruit infused sparkling water.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: *Salad--4 cups arugula -- 2 cups purple cabbage chopped, thin slices -- 1/3 cup silvered almonds -- 1/2 cup fresh blueberries --*Dressing -- 1/4 cup olive oil -- 3 tbsp red wine vinegar -- 2 tbsp dijon mustard -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 1/4 tsp black pepper -- 1/2 lime (or lemon), juiced -- 1-2 cloves garlic, crushed (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare all the salad ingredients as listed, add to a large bowl. -- To prepare the dressing: in a small bowl, add all of the ingredients and stir well. -- Add the dressing to the salad bowl. Mix well, serve and enjoy!

description.en-US:
Ok, so this quarantine has definitely brought out my sweet tooth in a good way! 

We made our favorite dessert bars this morning, so I decided to snap a pic and share it on Bobbieleelicious. You are welcome! It is a mix of dates, nuts, hemp seeds, and sea salt, covered in a layer of dark chocolate. The perfect sweet, salty, and crunch-nutty balance. Darling, it is absolutely delicious (and healthy)! Perfect for a midday snack or dessert. 

Feel free to swap the nuts around with whatever you have on hand. 

If your dates are hard, soak them in room temperature water for 30 minutes prior to making the bars.

ingredients.en-US: *Nuts Bars -- 14-15 Medjool dates, pitted--1 cup pecans--1/2 cup walnuts--3 tbsp hemp seeds -- 1/2 tsp sea salt --*Chocolate Topping -- 1 cup dark chocolate chips, melted (if needed, vegan) -- 1 tbsp unrefined coconut oil

recipeDirections.en-US: Line a 9x5 loaf pan with unbleached parchment paper. -- Add the Nut Bar ingredients to a food processor. Process until the dates and nuts and seeds are broken down to a finely chopped mixture. -- Remove the mixture from the processor and add it to the 9x5 loaf pan. Press it down, set aside. (If the nut mixture is crumbly and falling apart, add 1-2 tbsp of water, this will help it stick together. It should resemble a sticky paste-like texture)-- Melt the chocolate chips and coconut oil (I placed a glass bowl on top of a pot of boiling water). Mix it well. Pour the melted chocolate over the nut mix. -- Place in the freezer for 20 minutes before eating. -- May store in an airtight container, in the freezer, for 3 weeks (though I doubt they will last that long) -- Remove from the freezer just before eating and enjoy cold (my preference) or room temperature.

description.en-US:
If you are a fan of Indian food, you have very likely heard of Matar Paneer (Peas and Fresh Cheese). Matar Tofu is our vegan friendly version of Matar Paneer... might I add, it is absolutely delicious! 

Matar Tofu is made by cooking tofu and peas in a spicy onion, tomato masala (mixture of spices). It serves well with roti, naan, rice or quinoa. 

Feel free to adjust the spice level. If you would like, experiment by adding other vegetables to the dish such as corn, potatoes and/or bell peppers. 

I often make extras to use the leftovers for my "Punjabi Style Pizza". If you love this, you have to try our Pizza! 

As always, if you try it and love it... snap/post a picture on your social media and be sure to share the LOVE!

ingredients.en-US: 2 tbsp butter (vegan butter or avocado oil for vegan) -- 3 bay leaves -- 3/4 tbsp cumin seeds -- 1 large red onion, diced -- 4 cloves garlic, crushed to paste -- 1 inch piece of ginger, crushed to paste -- 1 green chili, chopped finely -- 2 large tomatoes, chopped -- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder -- 1/4 tsp chili powder -- 3/4 tsp sea salt  -- black pepper -- dash of cinnamon -- 2 tbsp full fat coconut milk -- 1 cup water -- 3/4 cup frozen green peas, thawed -- 1 cup tofu, cut into 1/2 inch cubes -- 3 tbsp fresh cilantro, roughly chopped

recipeDirections.en-US: Set a medium size pot over medium heat. Add the butter or oil, bay leaves, and cumin seeds. Saute until the cumin seeds begin to sizzle, 2-3 minutes (be careful not to burn). -- Add the onion, garlic paste, ginger, and green chilies. Stir often, cook until the onion is translucent - 3-4 minutes. -- Add the tomatoes to the pot, stir and allow it to cook for 2-3 minutes. Then add the turmeric, chili powder, sea salt, black pepper, cinnamon, and coconut milk - stir well. Now add the water and stir again. Cook over medium heat for about 4-5 minutes. -- Remove pot from heat, remove the bay leaves and add the mixture to a blender,  and puree the mixture. Transfer the tomato mixture back to the pot set back on medium heat. -- Add the tofu, peas and garam masala to the pot. Stir it into the tomato mixture. If too thick, add 1-2 tbsp of water. Cook for 4-5 mins over medium heat. Add the fresh cilantro, stir to combine. -- Serve hot.

description.en-US:
My favorite post-workout meal is a smoothie. It's simple and easy to put together when in a pinch for time. Add a handful of nutrient dense, protein rich foods to the blender, flip the switch and VOILA! 

This smoothie here is more focused on post-workout, protein and nutrition. I personally do not use protein powders daily as many are highly modified, I would rather prefer to use a food source protien, but feel free to add some if you like (I would go with vanilla or plain). 

In this recipe I use almonds, hemp seeds, and almond milk to boost the protein content naturally. The other ingredients help boost the nutrient profile by adding, healthy fats, vitamins C, A, iron, etc... 

Be sure to consume your post workout meal within 30-45 minutes after working out to reap its full benefits! 

Hope you enjoy, cheers to our health!!

ingredients.en-US: 3/4 cup almond milk (see my recipe) -- 23 almonds -- 1 cup spinach -- 1 cup frozen mango -- 1 tbsp flax seeds -- 2-3 tbsp hemp seeds -- 3 tbsp lemon juice -- 3 tbsp fresh cilantro (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to the blender, blend until smooth!

title.en-US: Blueberry Banana Muffin with Granola Topping

description.en-US:
It was a bit cool and gloomy here in Minnesota yesterday, perfect for staying indoors and baking with the kids! 

Just before placing the muffins into the oven, my son decided we should add some fresh blueberries to the mixture, so we did just that. To be honest, they turned out even better, what a little genius. My son gives these beauties "100 fingers up"! 

We love them warm, feel free to add a pat of butter/vegan butter. 

If you do not have fresh blueberries, swap for frozen or whatever berry you have on hand... or simply go sans berry. Hello banana muffin!! 

Hope you enjoy these delicious muffins as much as we do!

ingredients.en-US: *Muffins--2 flax eggs (2 tbsp ground flax + 6 tbsp water) -- 3 medium ripe bananas -- 1/4 cup pure maple syrup (1/3 cup if you want more sweet) -- 1/3 cup coconut oil -- 1/8 cup almond milk -- 1 tsp pure vanilla extract -- 2 tbsp baking powder -- 3/4 tsp baking soda -- 2 cups spelt flour (sifted in) -- 1 1/2 cups fresh blueberries --*Granola Topping -- 1/2 cup old fashioned rolled oats -- 2 tbsp coconut oil -- 2 tbsp pure maple syrup -- sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Prepare a muffin tin by lining with muffin liners or coat the muffin holes with coconut oil, set aside. -- In a small bowl, prepare the flax eggs by mixing the water and ground flax seeds. Set aside. -- In a medium bowl, add the mashed banana, maple syrup, coconut oil, almond milk and vanilla. Mix to combine. -- Add the flax eggs to the mixture, mix to combine. -- Now add all the remaining muffin ingredients (except the blueberries) to the bowl. Stir to combine, do not over mix the batter. -- Now add the blueberries to the muffin mixture, give it a stir. -- Scoop the muffin mixture to the muffin tin evenly among the 12 holes. (Do not put in the oven yet). -- Prepare the granola topping: In a small bowl, mix all the ingredients so that the oats are well covered in coconut oil/maple syrup. -- Sprinkle the granola on top of the muffins. -- Place the muffin tin into the oven, bake for 22-24 minutes. Check if they are done by inserting a toothpick. It should come out dry.

description.en-US:
Pancakes are such a great breakfast and/or brunch food, we sometimes even make them for dinner! 

To get the kids involved, we place a batch of pancakes on a serving plate, with side bowls of nuts and fresh cut fruit. We let them make their own miraculous pancake creations. The kids love doing this! 

This recipe is gluten free (be sure to use GF oats) and dairy free. 

I highly suggest using a high powered blender such as a Vitamix for blending the oats into a flour, otherwise buy oat flour. 

Variations: Fold in 1/4-1/2 cup fresh blueberries or chocolate chips into the pancake mixture before cooking. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 2 eggs (may swap for vegan eggs, see note) --1 medium ripe banana, peeled and smashed -- 1 1/4 cup almond milk, if batter is too thick add 1-2 tbsp more -- 4 tbsp melted coconut oil--1/4 tsp vanilla extract --2 cups oat flour (about 2 1/4 cups oats ground in blender) --1 tsp baking powder--1/2 tsp cinnamon --1/4 tsp salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat a skillet (I use an iron pan) over medium-low heat, use a dab of coconut oil to prevent sticking. --  In a large bowl, add the eggs, smashed banana, milk of choice, coconut oil, and vanilla extract. Using a whisk, mix well. -- In a medium bowl, add the oat flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt. Stir to combine. -- Now add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and stir to combine. -- On the heated pan, pour 1/4 cup of batter. Allow it to cook for 1-2 minutes on each side. The pancake will start to bubble, that is when it is time to turn it over to cook the other side. -- Repeat until the batter is finished. Results in approximately 10 pancakes. -- Serve immediately while warm. Top with nuts, fresh cut fruit and/or pure maple syrup.

recipeNotes.en-US: For gluten-free be sure to use gluten-free oats -- For vegan, swap eggs for flax eggs and add approximately 1/4 cup extra almond milk.

metaDescription.en-US: Pancakes are such a great breakfast food!   To get the kids involved, we place a batch of pancakes on a serving plate, with side bowls of nuts and fresh cut fruit. We let them make their own miraculous pancake creations.

title.en-US: Peanut Butter and Chocolate Breakfast Smoothie

description.en-US:
I love starting our kids day out with a nutritious and delicious smoothie. I switch them up often but we have a select few that they prefer, this is one of them.

The chocolate (I use cacao), peanut butter and banana base has them feeling like they are indulging in an early morning dessert. I add some greens, hemp seeds, and chia seeds to boost the nutrient profile (you can't even tell it is in there)! 

Whether you choose to make this as a breakfast option or midday snack, I am sure you will love this recipe! 

Happy sipping!

ingredients.en-US: 2 frozen bananas -- 2 cups almond milk (see my recipe or use nut milk of choice) -- 4 tbsp cacao powder -- 1/4 cup peanut butter -- 1 1/2 cups ice cubes -- 2 tbsp hemp seeds -- 2 tbsp chia seeds

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a high powered blender, blend until smooth. -- Serve and enjoy immediately!

title.en-US: Rosemary and Thyme Spelt Flour Bread

description.en-US:
Bread is my love language, for real! There is nothing more satisfying than making a fresh loaf of bread. I enjoy preparing it the night before, baking it first thing in the morning, and then letting the fresh baked bread aroma spread throughout our home. 

It really is quite easy, bread contains such simple ingredients: flour, water, yeast and salt!! Once you got that down you can experiment, add herbs, cheese, garlic, onions, etc. I have been baking bread for quite some time, this one here has become one of my personal favorites. 

You will need a heavy pot with a lid. I use a cast iron dutch oven that I purchased from amazon. (no worries if you do not own one, a heavy pot should work well). 

For the flour, I always experiment with different types of flours. My current favorite flour for bread is an ancient grain all purpose spelt flour by Nature's Legacy.   

Ok, enough bread talk (I could go on), let's get started!

ingredients.en-US: 3 cups all purpose spelt flour -- 1 1/2 tsp instant yeast -- 2 tsp sea salt -- 1 1/2 tbsp fresh rosemary, finely chopped -- 1 1/2 tbsp fresh thyme, finely chopped -- 1/3 cup olive oil -- 1 1/4 cups water, room temperature (may need to add 1-2 tbsp)

recipeDirections.en-US: In a large bowl, combine the flour, yeast, sea salt, rosemary, and thyme. Give it a stir. Now add in the water and olive oil, stir until the dough is well mixed and the flour is fully incorporated. -- Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or tight fitting lid. Let it rest in a warm spot in your kitchen for 10-16 hours. The dough will double in size. -- Set a piece of parchment paper on a workable surface area. Dust the surface with flour, set the dough onto the area. Fold the dough a few times, using your hands. Gently stretch the dough  and fold the edges under it. Form a ball. -- Place the dough in a warm area in the kitchen, allow it to rise for an additional 45 minutes to 1 hour. -- Preheat the oven to 425° F with the empty dutch oven or pot inside. This may take 20-30 minutes. -- Prepare the bread dough for the oven: make a few knife marks on the top of the load. I typically make 3 straight lines or an "X" but feel free to get creative. This allows the heat to escape and the bread to expand. -- Remove the preheated dutch oven from the oven. Set the bread and parchment paper into the dutch oven. Cover with the lid and place it back into the oven. Bake for 30 minutes with the lid on, remove the lid and bake for another 15-18 minutes, until the crust is golden-brown. -- Place the loaf on a wire rack to cool.

description.en-US:
This recipe here is a deliciously nutty twist to the classic chocolate chip cookie. 

Growing up my mama always baked fresh cookies for us, it was her way of sending LOVE to our bellies, we sure felt the love! 

These delicious chocolate chip cookies are made with almond flour, tahini, coconut sugar, and a handful of other ingredients. 

Hope you enjoy these soft and chewy chocolatey treats... If you make it and love it, don't forget to  "share the love" with your friends and family!

ingredients.en-US: 1 egg (or flax egg) -- 1/3 cup tahini -- 1/4-1/3 cup coconut sugar (adjust for preferred sweetness) -- 2 tbsp coconut oil, melted (may use butter) -- 1/2 tsp vanilla -- 1 cup almond flour -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 1/2 tsp baking soda -- 1/2 cup chocolate chips

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350°F, line a baking sheet or stone with unbleached parchment paper. -- In a medium bowl add the egg, tahini, coconut sugar, coconut oil, and vanilla. Mix well using a whisk. -- Add the almond flour, sea salt and baking soda. Using a spatula, mix to combine. -- Now fold in the chocolate chips. -- Form 12 equal size balls out of the cookie dough. Set the balls onto the lined baking pan and flatten to your  desired thickness, using the back of a fork (or your fingers). -- Transfer to the oven and bake for 11-12 minutes or until golden along the edges.

recipeNotes.en-US: For a vegan swap, replace the egg with a flax egg. -- 1 flax egg = 1 tbsp ground flax and 3 tbsp room temp water

title.en-US: Purple Cabbage and Green Apple Refresher

description.en-US:
I love fresh juice, I usually experiment with different varieties (whatever produce I have in my fridge), however, I  have a few "go to" recipes that leave me feeling so refreshed and energized. 

This beautiful Purple Cabbage and Green Apple juice is a perfect blend. I cannot drink cabbage on its own (blahh), but the addition of green apple balances the cabbage juice perfectly. For improved digestion and absorption of the nutrients I consume my juices on an empty stomach. 

You will need a juicer for this recipe. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 3 cups purple cabbage, chopped -- 3 green apples

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare your produce for your juicer (wash and cut). -- Juice and enjoy immediately!

title.en-US: Sweet Potato and Red Bell Pepper Soup

slug.en-US: Sweet Potato, Red Bell Pepper and Carrot Soup

description.en-US:
It's January and there's a lovely CHILL in the Texas air, and I am loving it! 

I find it funny that I am cold in Texas. I am Canadian born and have been through some freezing cold winters. However, after 8 years away from "home", this chilly weather still brings forth the desire for some warm, nourishing soup. 

So with that being said, I present to you my Sweet Potato and Red Bell Pepper Soup! I personally love soup with a side of crusty french bread but feel free to change it up. This soups pairs well with a sandwich, toast, naan bread, with pasta, or eat as is. 

For leftovers, store in the refrigerator in an airtight container for up to 3-4 days. 

Bon Appetit!

ingredients.en-US: 4 tbsps butter (or oil of choice) -- 1 medium onion, chopped -- 3 cloves garlic, diced finely -- 1/2 inch piece ginger, diced finely --  3 red bell peppers, seeded and chopped -- 1 lb sweet potatoes, peeled and diced -- 1 tsp sea salt -- 1 tsp turmeric powder -- 1 cup dried red lentils -- 4 cups vegetable stock -- 1 can coconut milk

recipeDirections.en-US: Heat a medium size pot over medium heat, add the butter (or oil). -- To the heated pot, add the onion, garlic, and ginger. Cook for about 5 mins, onions will be translucent. -- Now add the red pepper and sweet potato. Cover the pot and cook for 10 minutes, stir to prevent it from burning to the bottom. -- Stir in the sea salt, black pepper, turmeric, vegetable stock, and lentils. Bring the soup to a boil. Reduce to a simmer, and cover the pot. Cook for 12-16 minutes or until the lentils are soft. -- Now stir in the coconut milk. -- Puree the soup with an immersion blender or allow it to cool and puree it in a blender (I use a Vitamix). -- Serve with a sandwich, toast, naan bread, over pasta or eat as is.

description.en-US:
If you are looking for a breakfast to leave for Santa and his reindeers, look no further. This Gingerbread Pancake recipe is the perfect holiday breakfast that I am sure you (and Santa) are going to LOVE! 

It's festively delicious with ginger, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and molasses. If you would like to make it vegan, take a look in my "Notes" section for easy swaps. 

Enjoy friends! xo

ingredients.en-US:
1 cup spelt flour (or other flour of choice) --1/2 tbsp baking powder
-- 1 tsp ground ginger -- 1 tsp ground cinnamon -- ¼ tsp ground nutmeg -- 1/8 tsp ground cloves -- ¼ teaspoon salt -- 1 cup (+1 tbsp) milk of choice, room temp -- 2 medium eggs -- 2 tbsp blackstrap molasses -- 2 tbsp maple syrup -- 2 tablespoons melted butter

recipeDirections.en-US: Heat a pan over medium heat. I use my cast iron pan but feel free to use whatever you like. -- In a medium size bowl whisk together all the dry ingredients. Set aside. -- In another medium size bowl whisk together all the wet ingredients. -- Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and whisk to combine the mixture well. Be sure there are no crumbs of flour left over (If needed, add more milk to the mixture). -- Butter/grease the pan if needed. Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, add the pancake mixture to the pan. Cook on the first side for about 1 minute, bubbles will form. Then flip the pancake and cook on the other side for about 1-2 minutes. The pancake should be golden brown in color. -- Serve warm with a drizzle of maple syrup.

description.en-US:
If you are looking for a healthy and satisfying veggie burger, look no further! 

This baby is loaded with nutritious ingredients like sweet potatoes, quinoa, almond flour, cilantro, garlic, etc. It has ground flax seeds to help bind the burger so it's not as crumbly as many others can be (feel free to add more if needed). 

If you want some extra protein, feel free to add 1/4 cup of cooked black beans, I often do and it tastes amazing. 

For toppings, go wild! I love a variety of toppings like tomatoes, lettuce, sprouts, onions, avocados, you get my point... 

I really hope you love this beauty as much as we do!! 

Cheers to good health!

ingredients.en-US: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into cubes -- 1 cup quinoa -- 1/4 cup almond flour -- 1/2 cup fresh cilantro, washed and chopped -- 2 tbsp flax seed, ground -- 1/4 cup red onion, diced -- 3 cloves garlic --  1 1/2 tsp cumin powder -- 1 1/2 tsp smoked paprika -- 1 tsp chili powder -- 3/4-1 tsp sea salt or to taste -- black pepper -- 2 tbsp olive oil

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 375° F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and add the cubed sweet potatoes in a single layer. Bake for 30 minutes or until soft. Once finished, puree them with a mixer (or by hand). -- Cook the quinoa according to package. -- In a medium size bowl, add the pureed sweet potato, quinoa, almond flour, cilantro, flax seed, onion, garlic, cumin powder, paprika, chili powder, sea salt black pepper, and olive oil (just 1 tbsp, save 1 tbsp for brushing the burgers). Mix it well with your hands. Form 8 equal size  burgers. -- Heat a large heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Brush a little bit of olive oil onto each burger (both sides). Cook each burger for 6-8 minutes on each side. They will be golden brown in color. -- Serve on bun of choice, loaded with fresh veggie toppings! -- Enjoy!!

description.en-US:
Are you looking for a boost of energy? Then you will love this juice!! 

It's fresh, delicious, full of energy boosting nutrients and leaves you feeling amazing! 

Included in this recipe are vitamin and antioxidant rich ingredients like wheat grass, green apple, lemon, and kale. 

So go ahead, pull out your juicer and prepare to feel energized! 

Cheers! 

![ingredients](https://i.imgur.com/uAPPY8z.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 2 oz wheat grass -- 2 green apples -- 1 lemon (skin removed) -- 2 cups of kale

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash and prepare all produce and run it through a juicer. -- Drink immediately.

description.en-US:
This dessert is perfect for the holiday season. It not only tastes amazing but has such a beautiful presentation! 

It is super easy to put together with a handful of staple ingredients (you likely already have them in your cupboard). After approximately 35 minutes baking in the oven, it is ready to serve. 

I prefer serving ours warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream... hmmmm melted ice cream!!! 

The type of apple you use is entirely up to you but I definitely recommend using a sweet apple like Honeycrisp, Pink Lady, Fuji or Gala.

ingredients.en-US: 4 crisp apples -- 1 3/4 cups old-fashioned rolled oats -- 5 tbsp softened butter (vegan if desired) -- 1/3-1/4 cup coconut sugar -- 1 tbsp maple syrup -- 1 tbsp cinnamon -- pinch of sea salt -- 2 cup apple cider (depends on size of baking dish) -- vanilla ice cream for serving

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 400°F. -- Slice the tops off the apples and hollow them out using a melon baller (save the apple). -- In a medium size bowl, add the oats, butter, coconut sugar, maple syrup, cinnamon, and sea salt. Stir it all together. -- Stuff the apples with the oat mixture and place the apples into a baking dish. -- Pour the apple cider into the bottom of the baking dish. -- Bake the stuffed apple in the oven for 35-40 minutes. They should be soft. -- Serve warm, topped with vanilla ice cream.

description.en-US:
Let me introduce you to our Raspberry Smoothie, a creation born straight from the imaginative minds of my kids. With a little tweaking on my part to amp up the health factor, it's now a staple in our household.

Picture this: the tartness of raspberries, the creamy goodness of banana, a hint of lemon zest, and the richness of almond milk and kefir (or swap for yogurt if you're keeping it vegan). Toss in some almonds, hemp seeds, and a drizzle of honey (or skip it for a vegan twist), and you've got yourself a powerhouse of flavor and nutrition.

But here's the fun part – feel free to mix it up! Swap out the raspberries for strawberries or any other berry you like. And if you're feeling adventurous, throw in a handful of spinach for an extra nutrient boost – I promise, you won't even taste it!

So pull out your blender, raise your glass, and here's to happy blending and sipping! Cheers, friends!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cup almond milk (or milk of choice) -- 1 banana -- 1 1/2 cups raspberries -- 1-2 tbsp lemon juice -- 3/4 cup kefir or plain yogurt (use vegan alternative if desired)-- 2 tbsp almonds -- 2 tbsp hemp seeds

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients and blend until smooth. Adjust liquid as needed. -- Enjoy right away!

metaDescription.en-US: Raspberry smoothie recipe, homemade smoothie, healthy smoothie, raspberry banana smoothie, breakfast smoothie, nutrient-rich smoothie, blender recipe, fruit smoothie, customizable smoothie recipe, family-friendly smoothie.

description.en-US:
As a Canadian who now lives in the USA, we celebrate Thanksgiving for both the American (in November) and Canadian (in October) Turkey Days. So our festive meals need some variations! 

Traditionally we would serve a pumpkin and/or apple pie, but to spice things up, we like to create other varieties of pie as well. I present to you, this OMG delicious Chocolate Avocado Pie. 

When picking out an avocado, you should be able to hold them in your hand and when you press gently with your thumb, it makes a slight indent. You do not want it to be too soft or too firm, avocado selection is really important - you got this!! 

This pie is so simple to prepare. The no bake crust is made with just dates and nuts. The filling is smooth, chocolatey and it's so delicious! It's not only delicious but contains "good for you" ingredients that leave you feeling energized rather than wanting to fall asleep on the couch after dinner. 

So go ahead and turn on one of your favourite Thanksgiving movies (if it's Thanksgiving), pull out your pie pan and let's get this pie-party started!!

ingredients.en-US:
*For the crust:--1 1/4 cups raw unsalted almonds (or other nut of choice) -- 3 tbsp hemp seeds -- 1 cup dates, pitted -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 2 1/2 tbsp unsweetened cocoa--*For the Chocolate Avocado Filling:--
2 medium ripe avocados, peeled and pitted -- 2-3 tbsp pure maple syrup -- 3 tbsp dutch cocoa powder -- 8 oz chocolate chips, melted -- 1/4 tsp sea salt --*Toppings:--coconut whipping cream, fresh berries, nuts, seeds...

recipeDirections.en-US: *To make the crust: -- Place the almonds, hemp seeds, pitted dates, sea salt and cocoa into the bowl of a food processor. Process the mixture until it forms a thick paste like consistency (about 1-2 minutes). If it is too wet, add more dates or almonds. If it is too dry add a tsp of water at a time until desired consistency is reached.-- Using a 9 inch pie pan, press the almond-date mixture into the pan. Use the bottom of a cup to press it well, against the sides of the pan.  Set the pressed crust into the freezer to chill and solidify. --*To prepare the Chocolate Avocado Filling--In the food processor, add in avocado flesh, maple syrup, cocoa powder, melt chocolate chips and sea salt. Process until smooth, scrape the sides down if necessary. -- Pour the chocolate filling into the pie crust, using a spatula and smooth the top. Refrigerate for 4 hours.

title.en-US: Steel-Cut Oats in the Instant Pot

description.en-US:
This quick and easy method to prepare steel cut oats has been a game changer for my morning routine!! All you need are steel cut oats, water, sea salt and whatever toppings you choose to add to your hearty bowl of goodness... 

If you haven't had steel-cut oats before, they are a bit chewy and have a nutty flavor.  One serving of steel-cut oats (1/4 cup) has about 5 grams of protein and 5 grams of fiber. We enjoy topping our oat bowls with fresh berries, raisins, ground nuts and/or seeds. Oat bowls are a great way to start your day and an easy way to fuel your cells!

![oats](https://i.imgur.com/DeX3SeX.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup steel cut oats -- 3 cups water

recipeDirections.en-US: Rinse the oats under water in a strainer or mesh colander. -- Add the rinsed oats and water to the pot of the Instant Pot. -- Set the Instant Pot on "Pressure Cook" for 4 minutes, allowing the pressure to release naturally for about 15-20 minutes. -- Remove the lid when safe, top as desired and sprinkle a little love!

description.en-US:
Peanut butter and chocolate, in my honest opinion, is the most amazing combination in the entire world (big claim to make, I know)!!

These homemade healthy peanut butter cups are not only easy to make, they are easy to store, easy to clean, and so delicious! 

These are made with four simple ingredients, a few minutes of your time... What are you waiting for? Let's get the party started!!

ingredients.en-US:
1 (3.5 ounce) dark chocolate bar -- 
½ cup creamy peanut butter (if unsalted, add a pinch of sea salt) -- ½ tablespoon maple syrup -- 3/4 tbsp almond flour (or coconut flour)

recipeDirections.en-US: Line a mini muffin tin with liners, set aside. -- Melt the half of the chocolate bar (or chips) in a saucepan over medium heat.Stir frequently to prevent the chocolate from burning to the bottom of the pan. --  Once the chocolate is melted, add about 1 tsp to the bottom of each liner in the pan. -- Place the pan in the freezer for a few minutes to harden. -- To make the peanut butter filling, add the peanut butter, maple syrup and almond (or coconut) flour to a medium bowl, mix until smooth. Place about ½ tablespoon of the mixture in each cup/liner on top of the chocolate mix. Place the muffin tin into the freezer for a few minutes until it hardens.  -- Melt the remaining chocolate. Pour the chocolate (approx 1 tsp) over top of the chocolate and PB layer. Set the muffin tin back into the freezer for about 10-15 minutes. -- Serve cold.

description.en-US:
"Ohhhhh hunny can you puhleeaaas make me some Aloo Tikki?" Something I hear too often in our home. 

Aloo tikki are simple delicious spiced potato patties. 

After several attempts at these delicious tikkis, I have learned one important tip... do not over cook the potato! Just cook until they are fork tender. A friend suggested leaving the skins on (peel after cooking), that works well also. 

You can eat the Aloo Tikkis as a snack, a burger, or placed on top of an Indian inspired rice/quinoa bowl. 

I love serving our Aloo Tikkis topped with yogurt, Cilantro-Mint Chutney, chopped onion, cilantro and tomato with a squeeze of lime. AND if in season, BE SURE to add some pomegranate seeds!! The sweet/sourness of the pomegranate seeds are "the icing on the cake!"

ingredients.en-US: 4 medium potatoes, boiled with skins on -- 1/2 inch piece ginger, smashed in mortar -- 3 cloves garlic, smashed in mortar -- 3/4 tsp garam masala -- 1 tsp lemon juice -- 3/4 tsp sea salt -- black pepper -- 1/2 tsp red chili powder -- 1green chili, chopped fine -- 3 tbsp cilantro leaves, chopped fine -- 5-6 tbsp breadcrumbs -- 1-2 tbsp avocado or ghee (for cooking)

recipeDirections.en-US: *Preparation Work -- Boil the potatoes with the skins on. Boil just until cooked, do not overcook or you will have a soggy tikka. -- Let the potatoes cool and then peel the skin off. -- In a medium size mixing bowl, mash the potatoes. Mash them well until no chunks of potato remain. -- Add the ginger, garlic, garam masala, lemon juice, sea salt, black pepper, red chili powder, green chili and cilantro. -- Add the breadcrumbs. Mix and make a firm ball, it should not be sticky. You may add more breadcrumbs in needed. Set aside. --*Making the Aloo Tikki--Divide the potato (aloo) mixture into 7-8 equal parts (or adjust size/quantity as desired). -- Flatten them using your hand. Set them aside on a plate. -- On a pan, over medium heat, heat approx 1-2 tbsp of oil, butter or ghee (I use my cast iron pan). -- Once hot, place a tikki onto the pan. Cook for 1-2 minutes until it is golden and the base is crisp. Flip the tikki and cook until golden and crisp (1-2 mins). -- Remove the tikki from the pan. -- Dress the tikki as desired or serve on a bun as a burger.

description.en-US:
Last weekend we went on a road trip to Waco, Texas, and visited the Magnolia Market and the Waco Downtown Farmers Market.  Although we did not get to meet Chip and Joann in person, I did find some great pieces for the kitchen and around the home. 

I picked up a beautiful pie dish and could hardly wait to put it in use, so I doubled the recipe and baked this delicious Mixed Berry Crisp! 

Sweet and tart with hearty oats, a hint of coconut sugar, and cinnamon to top it off... Believe me when I tell you, your tastebuds will be dancing! If you are like us, add a generous spoon of vanilla ice cream to the finished product (preferably when warm, so it melts). 

As the season changes into fall and apples are in abundance,  cut them into bite size pieces or increase the cook time just a little. 

![eating apple crisp](https://i.imgur.com/hdqqs5H.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: *Blackberry blueberry filling: -- 1 cup fresh blueberries -- 1 1/2 cups fresh blackberries (or use all the same type of berry) -- 2 tbsp pure maple syrup -- 1 tsp fresh lemon juice -- 1/2 tsp fresh lemon zest -- 3/4 tsp vanilla extract --*Oat and almond topping: -- 2/3 cup old-fashioned rolled oats -- 1/4 cup almond flour -- 1/4 cup chopped raw almonds (slivered almonds work also) -- 2 1/2 tbsp coconut sugar -- 2  tbsps cold vegan butter, cut into small pieces -- 3/4 tsp ground cinnamon -- 1/4 tsp sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: *To Get Started -- Preheat the oven to 350°F and prepare an 8x4 pan by adding a thin layer of coconut oil, set aside. -- Wash and dry the berries, you want to make sure the berries have no excess water or the crumble will have extra (unwanted liquid) --*To Make the topping -- In a medium size bowl, combine the rolled oats, almond flour, almonds, coconut sugar, ground cinnamon, and sea salt. Now add the chopped butter and combine until the mixture is crumbly (I highly encourage you use your hands for this, squeeze it together and get it really crumbly). --*To make the blackberry filling:-- In a medium size bowl, combine the blueberries, blackberries, maple syrup, lemon juice, lemon zest, and vanilla.-- Mix to coat the berries in the liquid. -- Take 1/3 cup of the oat/almond mixture and toss with the blackberry mixture. Add to your prepared pan. Then, add the remaining oat/almond topping to the top, sprinkle it on evenly. --*To bake the crisp -- Add the crisp to the oven and bake for 45 minutes, until the topping is golden brown. -- Remove the crisp from the oven and allow it to cool for 10-15 minutes. Serve warm, topped with ice cream of choice. -- I hardly doubt you'll have left overs BUT if you do, store in the fridge in an airtight container for 3-4 days.

description.en-US:
Palak tofu (Spinach & Tofu) is my version of Palak paneer. Palak Paneer is a spinach and paneer (Indian cheese) dish that my husband and kiddos love, and I mean L-O-V-E!! 

I on the other hand have a bit of a love-hate relationship with dairy so I worked hard to create a version of my hubbys childhood favorites that we could both enjoy! 

Drum roll please... I present to you Palak Tofu! I swapped that standard paneer for tofu and the heavy cream for coconut milk. 

I like to eat this vegan friendly dish with rice or quinoa, the husband prefers roti. Add a side of greens, red onions and my dear, you have yourself a meal! 

If you would like to use paneer, you can make it yourself with my recipe [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/paneer-indian-cheese).

ingredients.en-US: 3 tsp oil of choice (I have used coconut and avocado) -- 7 oz firm tofu, cut into cubes -- 1/2 tsp sea salt -- 1/2 tsp garam masala -- 1/2 tsp cumin -- 1/2 tsp cayenne powder -- heavy pinch of kala namak (may swap for nutritional yeast) -- 1/4 cup water -- 1/4 cup coconut milk, use full fat -- 4-5 clove garlic, minced -- 1-in piece ginger, minced -- 1 medium tomato, diced -- 2 cups baby spinach -- 1 tsp maple syrup -- 1/2 tsp garam masala -- 1 1/2 tsp kasoori methi (dried fenugreek) -- fresh lemon juice -- to serve: lime wedges, red onion, pepper flakes, etc.

recipeDirections.en-US: In a large skillet, over medium heat, warm the oil. Add the cubed tofu. Coat the tofu with the oil and cook for 3-4 minutes. -- Add 1/4 tsp sea salt, garam masala, cumin, cayenne and kala namak. -- Coat the tofu with the spices and then reduce the heat to medium-low. Allow the tofu to cook (partially covered) for about 8-9 minutes. -- While the tofu is cooking, in a high powered blender, add the water, spinach, coconut milk, remaining sea salt, garlic, ginger, tomato, baby spinach, and maple syrup. Blend until the mixture reaches a smooth puree. -- Add the spinach mixture to the cooked tofu, mix well. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed. Cook for 12-16 minutes on medium-low heat, covered. -- Serve over rice or quinoa, with naan or roti. -- Don't forget to sprinkle a little love! xo

description.en-US:
Fall is coming and you know what that means? Apple season!! What's your favorite thing to make with fresh and crisp, farm fresh apples? One of mine is this delicious apple crisp. 

Last year we drove to an amazing Minnesota Farm and picked our own apples, it was so much fun. If you haven't yet, I highly suggest picking your own for the experience and taste. 

We ate all things apple (donuts, crisp, cider, etc.), then headed out to the fields to pick and fill our bags. Fun was had by all! 

My preference (of apples) to use for the Apple Crisp are a tart Granny Smith and a sweet Honey Crisp.

ingredients.en-US: *Apples: --3 large granny smith apples, skinned, cored and sliced thin -- 3 large honey crisp apples , skinned, cored and sliced thin (pink lady or other crisp apples work well also) -- 2 tablespoon pure maple syrup -- 1-2 tbsps butter or coconut oil -- 1 1/2  tsp cinnamon -- pinch of nutmeg --*Crumble Topping--1 cup old-fashioned or quick oats (gluten-free if necessary) -- 1/2 cup almond flour--

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare an 8x8 inch baking pan with butter or coconut oil, set aside. -- Set the oven to 350° and prepare all the ingredients while it warms up. -- Peel and core the apples, slice them into thin slices (or equal sized cubes, if you wish). -- In a medium size bowl, toss the apples, maple syrup, butter, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Pour the apple mixture into the 9x9 inch baking dish. --*Apple Crumble--In a medium size bowl add all the ingredients, mix together with your hands until it is combined and crumbly. -- Pour the crumble on the apple mixture in the 9x9 inch pan. --*Baking the Apple Crisp--Place the apple crisp into the oven and bake for 45-50 minutes, or until the top is golden brown. -- Remove the baking dish from the oven, allow it to cool for about 10 mins. -- Serve with ice cream or a whipped cream. -- Enjoy

description.en-US:
A hearty bowl of oats is the typical  breakfast of choice in our home. They started off pretty plain but over the years we have certainly taken our standard bowl and created a powerhouse of nutrition! 

You see, my son had a texture thing and would not eat any of the nuts and seeds that I added to his oat bowl so I decided to grind them and it has been a hit since then! 

I have been asked by family and friends to share how we make our oats. I decided to add it to our site so that anyone can access this simple, easy to make, nutritious breakfast bowl. 

With oats as a base, we add nuts and seeds, honey, butter, dried fruit and/or fresh fruit. 

This recipe is such a great bowl for yourself and/or the littles, a delicious and nutritious, satisfying start to a busy day at work or school. 

If you have extras, simply store it in an airtight container in the fridge for 3-4 days. Do not add the “add-ins” until you are ready to eat them.

ingredients.en-US: *Oats--3 cups water -- 1 cup steel cut oats, rinsed -- pinch sea salt--*Ground Nut Blend--1 cup nuts of choice (I like almonds and walnuts) -- 1/4 cup hempseeds -- 1/4 cup chiaseeds--*Toppings (optional)--butter--honey--bananas--blueberries--strawberries--raisins--cranberries--dried coconut--milk of choice (cools down a hot bowl)--anything goes!!

recipeDirections.en-US: Rinse and cook the oats as directed on the package (mine is 3 cups of water, 1 cup of oats and an inch of sea salt). -- While the oats are cooking, grind the nuts in a blender or spice grinder. Be sure not to over grind or they will turn into a nutbutter. add the hempseeds and chia seeds to the ground nut mix. Set aside. -- Once the oats are done cooking, portion them into individual bowls. Add 1-2 tablespoons of the nut mix, honey, butter, and other toppings you wish to use. -- For the kids, I prepare the toppings and allow them to create their own bowls (after I have stirred in the nut mix of course). -- You will have extra ground nuts leftover, store them in a sealed container in the fridge. Use within 1 week. -- Enjoy!!!

description.en-US:
I love potato salad, especially my Mama's potato salad! It was one of her "go-to" sides and to be honest she served it a lot but I never get sick of it!

Our version of her salad is simple to make and goes well as a side, on a wrap, topped on a green salad, etc. My favorite way to serve this delicious potato salad is with a bbq'd veggie burger and grilled corn with butter (my mouth is watering). 

Whether you choose to serve this dish hot or at room temperature, it is delicious. I am sure you will love our version of her traditional German Potato Salad.

ingredients.en-US: 2 pounds yukon potatoes (red potatoes work as well) -- 1/2 teaspoon sea salt (for boiling the potatoes) -- 1/3 cup apple cider vinegar -- 2 tablespoons honey (or traditionally, use sugar) -- 1/2 a red onion, roughly chopped or diced (own preference) -- 1 tablespoon dijon mustard -- 1/2 teaspoon sea salt -- freshly ground black pepper -- 2-3 large cloves garlic minced fresh garlic cloves, minced -- 1/2 cup chopped fresh parsley (or chives)

recipeDirections.en-US: Clean the potatoes well and place them into a large pot with water (I like to leave the skins on). Bring the water to a boil and add 1/2 tsp of sea salt. Cook for 20-25 minutes. The potatoes should be soft when poked with a fork. -- Once complete, drain the water and allow the potatoes to dry. Then, using a knife slice the potatoes into 1/2 inch thick slices. -- In a small bowl, add the vinegar, honey (or sugar), red onion, dijon mustard, sea salt, pepper, and minced garlic. Mix well. -- In a medium size bowl, add the sliced potatoes, vinegar mix, and parsley (or chives). Stir to cover the potatoes with the dressing, be careful not to smash the potatoes. Adjust the salt/pepper/ dijon as needed.

description.en-US:
Hey there! Let me share with you a little secret: I have a major love for stuffed grape leaves! It all started back in my teenage years in Canada, a close friends mother shared her secret picking location and showed us how to make these delightful roll! 

So, what's the deal with stuffed grape leaves, you ask? Well, imagine tender grape leaves stuffed with a flavorful "lemon and herb" rice mix, rolled up into cute little parcels, and boiled to perfection. It's a taste sensation, let me tell you!

Now, here's the thing: you can go traditional with meat fillings, but as a veggie lover, I keep it strictly plant-based. And let me tell you, it's still bursting with flavor!

Sure, the whole stuffing and rolling part might sound a bit daunting, but who says cooking can't be fun? Crank up your favorite tunes, let loose in the kitchen, and turn it into your very own dance floor!

So, are you ready to join me on this culinary adventure? I promise it'll be a deliciously good time! 

![rice stuffing](https://i.imgur.com/eWn3AdX.jpg)

ingredients.en-US:
1 jar grape leaves (about 70 leaves)--2 cups short grain rice (white rice works best)
1 large tomato, finely chopped--1 bunch parsley, finely chopped (stems removed)--3 tbsp fresh dill, finely chopped--1 bunch green onions, finely chopped--¼ green pepper, finely chopped--3 cloves garlic, minced--1 ½ - 2 teaspoons crushed red pepper flakes (adjust to taste)--Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste--¾ cup olive oil, divided--2-3 lemons, juiced (approximately ¾ cup)

recipeDirections.en-US:
Prepare Grape Leaves: Carefully remove the grape leaves from the jar and place them in a large bowl or pot of hot water. Let them soak for 10-15 minutes to soften. Then, drain the leaves in a colander and pat them dry with a kitchen towel. Set aside.--Prepare Filling: In a large mixing bowl, combine the uncooked rice, chopped tomato, parsley, dill, green onions, green pepper, minced garlic, crushed red pepper flakes, salt, pepper, and ¼ cup of olive oil. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly distributed. Set the filling mixture aside.--Stuff the Grape Leaves: Lay a grape leaf flat on a clean surface, vein side up. Place about 1-2 teaspoons of the rice filling near the stem end of the leaf (the amount may vary depending on the size of the leaf). Fold the bottom of the leaf over the filling, then fold in the sides, and roll it up tightly into a compact cylinder, similar to rolling a burrito. Repeat with the remaining grape leaves and filling.-- Cooking in Instant Pot: Line the bottom of the Instant Pot with a few grape leaves to prevent sticking.
Arrange the stuffed grape leaves in layers in the Instant Pot, making sure they are snugly packed to prevent unraveling during cooking. Drizzle the remaining olive oil over the stuffed grape leaves and season with a pinch of salt and pepper. Squeeze the juice of half of the lemons over the top.
Add enough water to just cover the stuffed grape leaves, usually about 4-5 cups. Close the Instant Pot lid and set the valve to the sealing position. Select the "Pressure Cook" or "Manual" setting and cook on high pressure for 20 minutes.
Once the cooking cycle is complete, allow the pressure to release naturally for about 10-15 minutes before carefully switching the valve to the venting position to release any remaining pressure. -- Serve and Enjoy: Carefully remove the stuffed grape leaves from the Instant Pot and arrange them on a serving platter. Squeeze the remaining lemon juice over the top for an extra burst of flavor.--Serve the stuffed grape leaves warm as an appetizer or part of a meal. They can also be enjoyed cold. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 4-5 days. If reheating, add a splash of water and lemon juice to maintain moisture.--Enjoy your delicious homemade stuffed grape leaves!

metaDescription.en-US: Discover how to make delicious stuffed grape leaves (dolmas) with our foolproof recipe! Follow our step-by-step instructions for flavorful rice-filled grape leaves cooked perfectly in an Instant Pot.

description.en-US:
Paneer is a fresh Indian cheese made from milk, known for its soft yet firm texture and beautifully mild flavor. Naturally rich in protein and calcium, it’s a nourishing vegetarian staple that brings both substance and simplicity to everyday cooking. Versatile and satisfying, paneer can be enjoyed in everything from warm, comforting dishes to fresh, modern meals. From traditional favorites like palak paneer and paneer tikka to simple grain bowls, wraps, and fresh salads, paneer adds both warmth and substance to everyday cooking.

## Why You’ll Love This Paneer Recipe

- High in protein for satiety

- Vegetarian and family-friendly

- Ready in under 30 minutes

- Great for meal prep

Go ahead and give it a try for yourself!

ingredients.en-US: 1 liter (1000ml) whole milk -- 1 lemon, juice (2-3 tbsp)

recipeDirections.en-US: Heat the milk in a heavy bottom pan over medium heat. -- Bring the milk to a boil, stirring often to prevent the milk from burning to the bottom of the pan. -- Once the milk has come to a boil, turn of the heat and add the lemon juice, little by little (about 1 tbsp at a time). Give it a stir. -- The milk will begin to curdle. You will see the whey separate from the water. Stop adding the lemon juice and let it sit for 4-5 minutes until the milk has completely curdled. -- Once the milk has completely curdled, drain the milk into a cheese cloth. Rinse it under cold water to get rid of the lemon taste. -- Bring the ends of the cheese cloth together and squeeze out as much of the water as possible. -- Place the paneer onto a plate (while still in the cloth) and flatten it, place a plate on top. Place a heavy item on top of the plate to help make it firm and shape it. Leave it on the counter to sit for 1.5-2 hours. -- Once the paneer is finished carefully remove the cheese cloth. -- Store it as is or cut it into cubes and use in one of our delicious dishes!

recipeNotes.en-US: Feel free to swap the lemon for another acidic agent, white vinegar works well.

description.en-US:
I love lemon, I also love lavender (who doesn't) - what a perfect pair for a fresh and delicious cookie. I have made so many desserts with this wonderful combo but this cookie here is one of my all time favorites. 

This cookie is not only delicious but health(ier) than your average cookie... ohhh and the kids LOVE them!! 

Let's talk about ingredients, they are gluten-free, vegan friendly and are sweetened with monk fruit sweetener. I have had success using Bob's GF 1:1 and as a white spelt flour. 

These beauties make a delightful gift or a great treat for a baby shower and/or other spring/summer celebration. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup GF all-purpose flour (I used Bob's 1:1) -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 1/3 cup monk fruit sweetener  (or other sweetener of choice) -- 1 1/2 tsp culinary lavender -- 1 tsp lemon zest, grated finely -- 1/2 cup unsalted vegan butter, softened (I used Miyoko's)

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 325° F. Prepare a baking sheet with a non-bleached sheet of parchment paper, set aside. -- In a large bowl, stir together the softened butter, monk fruit sweetener, and lemon zest. Mix until it is combined well. -- Sift in the flour and sea salt. Mix to combine.  -- Shape the dough into a disk and wrap with plastic wrap. Let it sit in the refrigerator for 20-30 minutes. -- Using a rolling pin, roll the dough out to 1/4 inch thickness. -- Cut the cookies from the dough using cookie cutters of choice. Place the cookies on the prepared baking pan. -- Bake the cookies at 325° F for 12-14 minutes. -- Allow them to cool on the backing sheet for 5-10 minutes and then transfer them to a wire rack. -- Store in an airtight container for 4-5 days.

recipeNotes.en-US: Make sure you use culinary lavender. -- When using lemon zest, it is important to use organic lemons to avoid pesticide residue.

description.en-US:
Have you ever had a Mango Lassi?

Our mango Lassi is made with simple ingredients that I played around with to bring you a healthier version of this delicious (spice busting) drink! 

To get started, all you need is fresh mango, yogurt (plain or coconut yogurt for vegan), milk of choice, monk fruit sweetener, cardamom, and saffron... 

Whether you are enjoying this beverage with a meal or alone, you are definitely going to fall in love!

ingredients.en-US:
1 cup fresh mango from 2-3 sweet, ripe mangoes -- 3/4 cup plain yogurt 
3/4 cup milk of choice (adjust to reach desired consistency) -- 1 cup ice -- 1-1 1/5 tablespoons monk fruit sweetener (adjust to desired taste) -- seeds from 2 cardamom pods, opened and seeds crushed (or use cardamom powder) -- saffron and crushed pistachio to garnish (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Place all ingredients into a blender, bend until smooth. -- Adjust ingredients as needed.

recipeNotes.en-US: If you have leftovers, this recipe freezes well into a popsicle.

description.en-US:
A fresh, nourishing staple in everyday cooking.

If you are looking for an easy way to add more spice to your life, you have Cilantro mint chutney is a fresh, vibrant Indian condiment made with herbs, citrus, and simple spices. Bright, herbaceous, and lightly tangy, it adds a refreshing burst of flavor while helping balance richer dishes.

Made with fresh cilantro and mint, this chutney naturally contains antioxidants and plant compounds that support overall wellness. It’s light, flavorful, and a simple way to add more fresh herbs into your meals while enhancing taste without heavy sauces.

This versatile chutney pairs beautifully with dishes like samosas, pakoras, paneer, drizzle on pizza, and can be spread onto sandwiches and wraps, spooned over grain bowls, or served alongside roasted vegetables and rice dishes. Whether used as a dip, drizzle, or spread, it’s an effortless way to elevate simple, nourishing meals.

Add some spice and zest to you life!

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup mint leaves -- 1 cup cilantro, just the leaves -- 1 green chili -- 1/2 inch ginger, skin removed -- 1/3 teaspoon cumin powder -- sea salt, to taste -- 1 teaspoon fresh lemon juice -- water as needed

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a high power blender or food processor, except water. -- Blend until a fine mixture is created, slowly adding water to get the base moving. -- Store in an airtight container, refrigerate for 3-4 days.

description.en-US:
If you haven't had Orzo Salad before, you are in for a new grab and go favourite!! It's fresh, delicious, nutritious, and keeps you feeling satisfied!! 

Orzo is a small gem shaped pasta typically made out of wheat semolina flour, to be honest... it kind of looks like rice. For those who prefer gluten-free variety, it can be also available in gluten free. 

In this recipe I use orzo (cooked and cooled), cherry tomatoes, red onion, kalamata olives, cucumber, freshly chopped basil, baby spinach, and sun dried tomatoes. For the dressing I make my own oil based Italian recipe (see ingredients/direction) but feel free to use a store bought version if you like). 

If you are preparing in advance, this salad keeps well refrigerated for 3-5 days.

ingredients.en-US: *Salad--12 oz orzo, prepared according to package details -- 1/2 cherry tomatoes, halved -- 1/4 cup red onion, diced -- 1/2 cup kalamata olives, sliced or halved -- 1 medium cucumber, chopped -- 2 tbsp freshly chopped basil -- 1/2 baby spinach, chopped -- Optional toppings: sun dried tomatoes, pine nuts, parmesan cheese, etc. -- *Dressing --1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil -- 5 tablespoons red wine vinegar -- 2 teaspoons honey -- 1 tablespoon fresh-squeezed lemon juice -- 2 cloves garlic, minced (optional) -- 1/2 teaspoon sea salt (adjust to preference) -- 1/4 teaspoon ground pepper -- 3 teaspoons Italian dried herb seasoning

recipeDirections.en-US: *Salad--Cook the orzo according to the package, allow it to cool. -- In a medium size bowl, add all the salad ingredients and mix. -- *Dressing --In a small bowl add all the dressing ingredients and whisk until combined well. -- Pour the dressing over the salad and top as desired. -- Serve cold.

description.en-US:
Fire up your grill and bring out the corn. This Mexican style corn is about to become one of your favorite ways to make this summer time staple! 

We often make this delicious side and serve it with our tacos and burritos. 

If we have leftovers, we remove the corn from the cob and add it to wraps, salads, or quinoa bowls... 

Happy Grilling!

ingredients.en-US: 4 ears of corn, shucked -- butter (vegan) -- ⅓ cup vegan-friendly mayonnaise -- 2 cloves garlic, minced -- 3/4 tsp chili powder and/or paprika powder -- 1 tbsp finely chopped cilantro -- 1-2 tbsp fresh lime juice -- ½ cup of crumbled vegan feta (or Cotija cheese if not vegan) -- ¼ tsp of sea salt -- pepper -- lime wedges and cilantro for garnish

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the bbq and cook the corn on the grill. Cook the corn until it is cooked through with light charring. -- While the corn is cooking, in a medium size bowl mix the mayo, garlic, cilantro, and sea salt. -- Once the corn is finished cooking, add a dab of butter on each piece. Then using a pastry brush, spread the mayo mixture over the cooked corn. Garnish with fresh lime juice, cheese and cilantro, sprinkle (a little love) chili powder or paprika, and pepper. Serve hot.. ENJOY!!

description.en-US:
Staying hydrated is one of the simplest (and most powerful) ways to feel your best, especially before or after activity. While it’s tempting to grab a sports drink, most are loaded with added sugars, dyes, and mystery “flavors.” When I need something quick, I’ll simply reach for water, but when I have a few extra minutes minutes, I love making this homemade Watermelon Electrolyte Drink.

It’s made with just a handful of clean, nourishing ingredients:

- Coconut water → naturally hydrating, rich in potassium

- Watermelon → cooling, high in water content, vitamin C, A, and magnesium

- Celtic sea salt → provides trace minerals and supports electrolyte balance

- Fresh lime juice → bright, zesty, and packed with vitamin C

We usually drink this about 1–2 hours before heading out, or after returning home from a workout or sports. It’s light, delicious, and so refreshing... and yes, it’s kid-approved, too! My kids love it, which makes it a win all around.

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup coconut water -- 3 cups chopped watermelon -- 1 lime, juiced -- 1/4 tsp celtic sea salt -- 1/2 cup ice (feel free to add more)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a blender, blend until combined. -- Enjoy cold!

description.en-US:
Aloo Gobi (Spiced Potato and Cauliflower) is a popular Indian dish. And to be honest, out of all the Indian dishes I have tried and learned to make, Aloo Gobi has become one of my favorites. 

I make my Aloo Gobi with ginger, garlic, onions, tomatoes and a variety of Indian spices. It is the perfect combination for this recipe. 

This delicious dish is vegan friendly and gluten free. You can pair this dish with roti, rice, quinoa or as part of a rice bowl. 

If you have leftovers, lucky you! This dish tastes even better on the 2nd or 3rd day. 

When cooking be sure not to over cook as it can become soggy.

ingredients.en-US: 1 large russet potato, peeled and cubed -- 1 cauliflower medium size, broken into small florets -- 3-4 tbsp avocado oil -- 1 onion, diced -- 2 tomatoes, chopped -- 3/4 teaspoon cumin seeds -- 2 cloves garlic, crushed -- 1 inch piece ginger, peeled and grated -- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder -- 1/2 teaspoon amchur/dry mango powder (ok to skip if you do not have) -- 1/4-1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, to taste -- 1/4 teaspoon garam masala powder -- 1 teaspoon coriander powder -- black pepper -- 1/2 tsp sea salt (add more once cooking is complete) -- 3 tablespoons chopped cilantro -- lime wedges

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare all the ingredients as noted. -- In a medium size pan, over medium heat, add 3 teaspoons of avocado oil. Once hot add the cumin seeds and cook until they crackle, stirring often so they do not burn (45-60 seconds). -- Add the onion, garlic and ginger.
