Cook for 3-4 minutes, stir often until onion is translucent. -- Add the tomatoes. Cook for 2-3 minutes. -- Add the turmeric (haldi) powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, amchur, garam masala and black pepper. Stir the spices into the mix. -- Add the potatoes and cauliflower to the masala mix. Cover the pot and cook for 16-22 minutes. The  cauliflower and potato should be tender (not soggy). -- Garnish with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice. Adjust salt as needed.

description.en-US:
This Lavender Scrub recipe is an absolute favorite of mine, and it’s easy to see why. I’ve been using it for years, and every time, I’m amazed by how soft and refreshed it leaves my skin. The best part? It’s made with just a few simple, all-natural ingredients!

__What You’ll Need:__
1. 1 cup cane sugar: A gentle exfoliant that helps remove dead skin cells, leaving your skin smooth and polished.
2. 2 tablespoons pink Himalayan salt (fine ground): Rich in minerals, this salt not only exfoliates but also helps detoxify the skin.
3. 14-15 drops lavender essential oil: Known for its calming and soothing properties, lavender oil also has antimicrobial benefits, making it great for your skin.
4. 1 1/2 tablespoons dried lavender: Adds a lovely texture and natural fragrance to the scrub, enhancing the overall spa-like experience.

__Directions:__
Mix It Up: In a medium-sized bowl, combine all the ingredients. Stir until everything is evenly blended, ensuring the lavender oil and dried lavender are well-distributed.

Add to Jar: Transfer the scrub into a large glass jar, or divide it into smaller jars if you’d like to share this lavender-scented goodness with friends. It’s the perfect gift for anyone in need of a little self-care!

Use this scrub in your shower or bath, gently massaging it onto your skin to exfoliate and hydrate. You’ll step out feeling pampered and smelling amazing!

![Bath Scrub](https://i.imgur.com/BbdpWg4.jpg)

![My son tying ribbon on the gift jar](https://i.imgur.com/oaFwSCp.jpg)

body.en-US:
What you need:

- 1 cup cane sugar
- 2 tablespoons pink Himalayan salt (fine ground
- 14-15 drops lavender essential oil (mre if you prefer)
- 1 1/2 tablespoons dried lavender

Directions:

1. In a medium size bowl, add all the ingredients and mix until they are well combined. 
2. Add the scrub to one large glass jar or seperate into smaller jars and share with some friends in need of some lavender scented self-love/care.

Hope you enjoy,

Bobbie XO

![Bath Scrub](https://i.imgur.com/BbdpWg4.jpg)

![Kid tying a ribbon on the gift jar](https://i.imgur.com/oaFwSCp.jpg)

metaDescription.en-US: Create a soothing Lavender Scrub with simple, natural ingredients. This easy DIY recipe exfoliates, leaving your skin soft and fragrant. Perfect for self-care or as a thoughtful gift. Pamper yourself with this spa-like experience at home.

description.en-US:
If you are looking for an easy peasy, healthy, simple ingredient, gluten free, corn tortilla... my friends, look no further. I got it right here!
I would buy my corn tortillas from the store until a dear friend of mine showed me this simple recipe!

All you need is masa harina (corn flour), sea salt, warm water, and lime juice. Such simple and healthy ingredients. Prepare this tortilla mixture 45 minutes - 1 hour in advance.

Whether you are making corn tortillas for tacos, tostadas, enchiladas, this recipe is perfect!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups masa harina --  3/4 tsp sea salt -- 1 1/2 - 1 3/4 cup water, room temperature -- 1/2 lime, juiced

recipeDirections.en-US: Preparing the dough: In a large mixing bowl, whisk the masa harina and sea salt. -- Gradually add the water and lime juice (the amount of water may need to be adjusted depending on the type/brand of flour you

recipeNotes.en-US: The tortillas will store well in the refrigerator for 2-3 days -- To reheat, warm on skillet over medium-high heat for 15-20 seconds -- If the tortillas are sticking to the parchment paper, add a little flour to them before rolling out

description.en-US:
Six years ago, I moved to the USA from Canada. Still, every year, I celebrate Canada day on July 1st.  We celebrate by eating poutine, the official food of the Canadians (originating in the province of Quebec). 

Imagine... fries, with melted cheese curd and gravy on top, it is so delicious. Absolutely delicious! 

As a lover of this amazing dish, I was determined to make my own health(ier) recipe. We oven bake our fries (typically deep fried) and prepare our own vegan and gluten free friendly gravy. 

Poutine can be enjoyed alone or as a side. If you are like me (I need some extra crunch) go ahead and add some extra diced tomatoes, chives and green onions. -- If you haven't yet, be sure to give this delicious dish a try, it is something the whole family will love!

ingredients.en-US: *Fries--3-4 medium russet potatoes, cut into 1/4-1/2  inch pieces -- 4 tbsps avocado oil -- sea salt -- Black pepper--*GF Vegan Gravy--2 cups vegetable broth -- 1 tbsp tamari sauce (or soy sauce, if not gluten free) -- 1/2 tsp garlic powder -- 3/4 tsp onion powder -- 1/4 cup brown rice flour--*Toppings -- cheese curd or broken mozzarella -- optional: diced tomatoes, diced onions, chives, etc...

recipeDirections.en-US: Soak the cut potatoes in a bowl of cold water for a minimum of 1 hour - 24 hours in the fridge. Drain the water when complete. -- Preheat oven to 400°F - line baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper. -- In a medium size bowl, mix the potatoes, oil, sea salt and pepper. Mix til covered. -- Set the potatoes on the baking sheet in a single layer, bake for 15-20 minutes and then flip them. Bake for another 10-15 minutes. Check to ensure they do not burn. -- Easy GF Gravy: In a saucepan, over medium-high heat, add all the ingredients and bring to a boil. Lower the temperature to medium heat, stir often intolerant the gravy thickens, 3-4 minutes.--Assemble the Poutine: Set the baked fries into individual bowls, cover with gravy and cheese. If using additional toppings add now and serve.

recipeNotes.en-US: For vegan: swap cheese with vegan cheese.

description.en-US:
I don't know about you but this quarantine has me wanting to celebrate every single weekend... Can I get a "Happy 108th day of quarantine" pass the cake please!!" 

Ok, but in all honesty, what is a celebration without cake (birthdays, anniversaries or quarantine), why not make it health(ier)! 

This delicious double layer carrot cake has no refined sugar, is gluten-free and with a few simple swaps, it can be made vegan... "sign me up!" 

My kids and husband love this cake, I'm certain that you will also!

ingredients.en-US: *Cake--2 cups oat flour -- 1 cup almond flour (not meal) -- 1/2 cup brown rice flour -- 1/2 tsp baking soda -- 1/2 tsp baking powder -- 1/2 tsp sea salt -- 1 1/2 tsp cinnamon powder -- 1/4 tsp nutmeg -- 3 eggs, room temp -- 2 cups finely shredded carrot -- 1/2 cup pure maple syrup -- 1 cup unsweetened milk of choice (I have had success with coconut and almond milk) -- 1 tbsp pure vanilla abstract -- 4 tbsp coconut oil --1 tbsp apple cider vinegar -- 1/2 cup raisins (optional)--*Frosting -- 1 8 oz cream cheese, room temp (use vegan if necessary -- 1/3-1/4 cup powdered sugar or 1 1/2 tsp stevia I prefer using stevia)

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Prepare two 9 inch cake pans with coconut oil. -- In a medium size bowl, whisk together the first 8 ingredients (oat flour-nutmeg). Set aside. -- In a medium size bowl, mix the shredded carrots, eggs, maple syrup, milk of choice, vanilla, coconut oil, and acv. Mix until all ingredients are well combined. -- If using raisins or other suggested add-ins, now is the time to fold them into the batter. -- Add the dry ingredients to the wet, mix util combined. -- Evenly distribute the batter between the two cake pans. Bake for 24-27 minutes. To test doneness, stick a toothpick into the cake, it should come out clean. -- Allow the cake to cool for 25 mins in the cake pans and then carefully remove them from the pans. -- Transfer the cakes (out of pan) to a wire rack. Allow them to cool completely (approx 1 1/2 hours). During this time prepare the frosting. -- Frosting: In a medium bowl, add the cream cheese and stevia or powdered sugar, beat with an electric mixer on low til combined. Frost the cake and ENJOY!!!

recipeNotes.en-US: For vegan: -- swap the cream cheese for vegan cream cheese -- swap the 3 eggs for 3 flax eggs (3 tbsp ground flax and 9 tbsp water, whisked together)

tools.en-US: two 9' cake pans -- mixing bowls -- whisk -- spatula -- electric mixer

description.en-US:
It's summertime and the heat is on... so is peach season! Time to make a batch of this Refreshing Peach Lemonade! 

With only four, wholesome, good for you ingredients, this easy to make lemonade will help keep you hydrated through a hot summer's day. 

Cheers!!

ingredients.en-US: 4 peaches, pitted and coarsely chopped -- 4 tbsps pure maple syrup -- zest of 1 lemon (be sure it's organic) -- 2 lemons, juiced -- 3 cups water -- ice

recipeDirections.en-US: Combine the peaches, maple syrup, lemon zest and 1/2 cup of water in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Allow it to come to a boil and them reduce the heat, simmer for 3-4 minutes. Allow it to cool for 5-10 minutes. -- Place the peach mixture into a blender, blend until smooth (I do it with the lid off or remove the centerpiece of my vita-mix lid to allow the heat out). -- Once cool enough to handle, press the peach mixture through a sieve into a large jar, reserving the liquid and discarding the solids or you may save them to use in you baked goods. -- Add the lemon juice and water, stir/shake well, you may need to adjust sweetness. -- Serve cool with ice and garnish with peach slices or other summer berries! -- Happy sipping!

title.en-US: 5 Great Fruit Infused Water Recipes to Make Drinking Water More Interesting

description.en-US:
If you struggle with drinking water and meeting your daily quota, look no further... this post is for you!

I hear it all the time, "water is so boring" but it is (in most cases) the healthiest of beverages to consume. 

By adding some fresh fruit to your H20 it not only enhances the flavor, it looks fun, even the kids will enjoy it. 

I have added 5 of my favourite fruit/herb infused water combinations. Give them a try, leave a comment and share which one you enjoy the most.

## Ingredients:

1. Strawberry Basil Lemon Water 
 - 1/4 cup strawberries, stemmed and sliced
 - 1/2 lemon, thinly sliced
 - 4 large fresh basil leaves torn
 - 4 cups filtered water
 - ice (optional)

2. Blackberry Mint Water
 - 1/4 cup blackberries
 - 2 sprigs of mint
 - 4 cups filtered water
 - ice (optional)

3. Cucumber Lime Water
 - 1/4 cup cucumber, thinly sliced
 - 1/2 lime, thinly sliced
 - 4 cups filtered water
 - ice (optional)

4. Raspberry Lime Water
 - 1/4 cup raspberries
 - 1/2 lime, thinly sliced
 - 4 cups filtered water
 - ice (optional)

5. Blueberry Orange Water
 - 1/4 cup blueberries
 - 1/2 orange, thinly sliced
 - 4 cups filtered water
 - ice (optional)

## Directions:
1. Add all the fruit and/or herbs to a glass jar (with a lid). Break some of the fruit down using the back of a spoon. 

2. Add the water and ice, CHEERS!!

If you are looking for some great "on the go" glasses, our Working Glasses from Crate and Barrel can be found [HERE](https://www.crateandbarrel.com/set-of-12-large-working-glasses/s643663)

description.en-US:
Do you enjoy Indian food? This delicious Rajma recipe is for sure a crowd pleaser! Rajma was one of my husbands favorite dishes growing up, so I had no choice but to master our own mouth watering recipe. 

What exactly is Rajma... The word Rajma is Punjabi for kidney bean. Kidney beans are the heart of this delicious dish, along with tomatoes, ginger, garlic, onions, cumin seeds, chili powder, garam masala, and other tasty spices and ingredients. 

We typically serve Rajma with rice, quinoa or roti; be sure to serve with a side of fresh vegetables and (if you are like my hubby) a side of plain yogurt. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup kidney (rajma) beans, soaked in 3-4 cups of water overnight -- 2 tbsp avocado oil -- 3-4 green cardamoms -- 3-4 cloves -- 1 tsp cumin seeds -- 1/4 tsp asafetida (skip, if you do not have) -- 1 medium onion, finely chopped -- 1/2 inch piece ginger, peeled and minced -- 2 cloves garlic, peeled and minced -- 2 medium-large tomatoes, pureed in blender -- 2 bay leafs -- 1 tsp coriander powder -- 1 tsp cumin powder -- 1/4 - 1/2 tsp chili powder (optional, to taste) -- 1/2 tsp garam masala -- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder  -- 1 tsp sea salt -- black pepper -- 1 1/2 cups water -- 3-4 tbsp cilantro -- 2 tbsp butter or ghee (skip for vegan)

recipeDirections.en-US: Drain the water from the kidney beans that have been soaking overnight and rinse them well. Set them aside. -- Prepare all the ingredients. -- Select the saute mode on the instant pot. Once hot, add the oil, cardamoms, cloves, and cumin seeds. Cook for about 30 seconds, stir them throughout. Add the onion, garlic, ginger and asafetida. Cook for about 2-3 minutes. Add the tomatoes to the pot and mix well. Cook for about 3 minutes. -- Now add the bay leaf, coriander powder, cumin powder, chili powder (optional), garam masala, turmeric, salt and pepper. Give it a stir. -- Add the water and rajma beans and give it another stir. -- Apply the lid and close the pot. Select the Pressure Cook button and cook for 38 minutes on high pressure, allowing the pressure to release naturally (the bean should be soft). (be sure to follow the instant pot manual for proper usage). -- Once finished, open the lid. Remove about 1 cup of the rajma mixture and place into a blender and blend into a paste. Return the rajma to the instant pot and give it a stir (this thickens it). -- Add the fresh cilantro and butter, stir once again. --  Serve with roti, rice or quinoa, enjoy!

description.en-US:
There’s something about the delicious smell of food cooking on the grill on a warm summer's day... It brings on all the feels! Without a doubt, summer and barbecuing go hand in hand. What else can you bring to the grill? Summer fresh produce!

What's great about these kabobs is that they can be made using produce you have available in your kitchen (or garden). You may need to adjust the cook time a bit and cook until soft.

These kabobs make a perfect side dish, sandwich filler, or a great topper on a rice or quinoa bowl.

Go ahead and fire up your grill, this really is such a great way to enjoy those in season vegetables!

ingredients.en-US: *Corn Kabobs -- 2 cobs of corn, 1 1/2 in thick pieces -- 1 medium zucchini, cut into 1 inch pieces -- 1/2 medium red onion, cut into chunks -- peaches, cut into 8 even pieces -- Top with fresh squeezed lemon or lime juice, feta cheese or vegan cheese, sea salt, pepper, and cilantro

recipeDirections.en-US: Thread the vegetables onto the skewers. -- Heat the grill on medium heat. -- Grill the kabobs uncovered for 11-13 minutes. They should be cooked until brown and tender.

description.en-US:
Aloo paratha is basically a roti or flat bread made with a potato (aloo) and a spice filling. It makes a quick, easy and nutritious meal. 

Aloo paratha is fairly easy to make. 
1. Prepare the roti dough (atta). 
2. Boil the potatoes and add the spices. 
3. Roll the dough with the potato filling inside.
4. Cook it on the stove top. 
5. Voila, time to eat. 

This paratha serves well with butter, yogurt, and a side of vegetables.  To keep it vegan, replace the butter with oil. 

If you enjoy this paratha recipe, be sure to check out our other paratha recipes. 

For a drink to pair with this paratha, see my [chai recipe](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/masala-spice-chai)!

ingredients.en-US: *Dough (atta) -- 3/4 cups spelt flour -- 3/4 cups brown rice flour -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- warm water to knead the dough --* Potato (aloo) filling -- 2 medium potatoes, skin removed, boiled and mashed -- 1 green chili, chopped finely -- 2-3 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped fine -- 1/2 tsp cumin powder -- 1/2 tsp turmeric powder -- 1/2 tsp garam masala -- 1/8 tsp red chili powder -- 3/4 tsp sea salt -- avocado oil, to cook the paratha

recipeDirections.en-US: To prepare the dough: In a medium sized bowl, mix all the ingredients, add the warm water a little at a time. -- Continue kneading the dough until it reaches a soft and smooth consistency. -- Cover the bowl with a towel, allow it to sit for 20 minutes. -- To prepare the potato (aloo) filling: In a medium sized bowl, add all the filling ingredients. Mix well til combined. Do not add the avocado oil for cooking the paratha. -- Making the paratha: Set up a workable area to make the paratha. You will need a rolling pin and surface to roll the dough. -- Heat a flat pan (or tawa) on the stove top. -- Make 6 equal sized balls out of the dough. -- Take one of the balls and roll it into a circle (use a little flour so that it does not stick to the rolling pin or working surface). -- Add 2-3 tbsp of the potato filling to the center of the circle. -- Bring all the edges of the dough to the center and pinch them together, sealing the dough. -- Add a little flour to the workspace and roll the ball into a circle once again. -- Carefully transfer the paratha to the heated flat pan. Let it cook for 1-2 minutes and then flip it. -- Apply a little avocado oil to the exposed side (about 1/8-1/4 tsp), cook for a minute and flip it (oil side is faced down now). There should be golden brown spots on them. -- Repeat this process until all the paratha are complete. -- Serve with chai, plain yogurt, and/or achar (a combo of sliced fruits and vegetables pickled in oil and spices)

description.en-US:
Sweet summer afternoons call for simple and effortless snacks, like these!! 

Serve these delicious berry bruschetta breads as a side or appetizer. They pair well with just about anything, however, I prefer a fresh arugula salad (or pasta for the kid-lings and hubby). 

As for the baguette, I buy from our local baker,  BUT stay tuned, I have placed an order for a baguette pan, so a recipe will soon follow! 

For a vegan version, swap the cream cheese for a vegan friendly version (like Miyoko's). 

Enjoy!! 

![balsamic glaze](https://i.imgur.com/iHy1pMu.jpg)

![My son sneaking a bite](https://i.imgur.com/0Zh9A4W.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 1 12 inch long crusty baguette (sliced in 1/2 inch slices) -- butter (I prefer Kerrygold) -- 8 ounces cream cheese, softened to room temp (or swap for goat cheese) -- 1 tbsp fresh squeezed lemon juice -- berries for topping (strawberries, blackberries, pomegranate, blueberries, raspberries) -- fresh mint or arugula for garnish -- balsamic glaze

recipeDirections.en-US: Set the oven to 350°F, add a thin layer of butter to the sliced baguettes and toast for approximately 1-2 minutes, remove from the oven. -- Mix the cream cheese and lemon together. -- Spread the cream cheese mixture onto the toasted baguettes. -- Top the baguettes with fresh berries and mint/arugula. Drizzle some balsamic glaze. -- Serve immediately!

recipeNotes.en-US:
For 14 inch baguette, increase cream cheese to 10 ounces. 

For a sweet twist, add some honey to the cream cheese

description.en-US:
I will admit, I haven't always been a big fan of tofu. I now realize that I wasn't preparing it properly. 

My first experience with tofu (that I actually enjoyed) was at Whole Foods, it was crispy and baked to perfection. 

I tried hard to come up with my own version of their tofu, and now with this recipe, the taste and texture is very similar to what I was seeking. 

Tofu is quite versatile, you can add many spices or seasonings to create a flavor perfect for whatever else you are pairing it with. Here I share a basic. 

This baked tofu can be added to a salad, rice or quinoa bowl... you can even add it to a kabob stick (decrease the bake time by 10 mins), a great substitute for any paneer recipe, or simply add it to a vegetable stir fry. 

The possibilities are endless, my dear friend! 

![tofu](https://i.imgur.com/jyhiepU.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 1 package of tofu (14-16 oz) - prepared as in notes -- 2 tbsp avocado oil --  2 cloves garlic, minced (optional)1 tsp sea salt -- 1/4 tsp black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 400°F, line a baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper. Set aside. -- In a medium sized bowl, add the tofu (prepared as per notes), avocado oil, garlic, sea salt and pepper. Combine. -- Add the tofu to the baking sheet in a single layer. Place in the oven. -- Bake for 25 mins. At the 10 minute mark, turn the tofu over so it cooks on all sides

recipeNotes.en-US: To prepare the tofu: Drain the liquid from the packaged tofu. On a plate, place a paper towel down and set the tofu on it. Cover the tofu with another paper towel. (This is meant to help absorb the liquid). Let it sit for 10-15 mins

title.en-US: Kiwi and Strawberry Arugula Salad with Lime-Honey Dressing

description.en-US:
Hello SUMMER, hello salads! 

Warm weather is here and I don't know about you, but simple and lite, fresh and vibrant meals are on our weekly menu. 

This delicious arugula salad is just one of our many salads that we adore (sans strawberry for the hubby of course, he hates them). 

Arugula is a peppery green leaf that originated in the Mediterranean region. It is one of those foods that you either "super hate" or "super love", I am in love! 

This vibrant salad pairs PERFECT with my spelt flour pizza and a bottle of bordeaux! 

Enjoy!!

ingredients.en-US: *Salad--6 cups arugula (may mix with other green) -- 2 kiwi fruit, peeled and cut to bite sized pieces -- 1 cup strawberries, bite size --  4 green onions, sliced -- 1/2 avocado, diced -- 1/2 cup blueberries -- 1/2 cup pecans --* Honey and Lime Dressing-- 2 tbsp lime juice -- 2-3 tbsp olive oil -- 2 tbsp honey -- sea salt and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: In a medium size bowl, add the arugula, kiwi, strawberries, green onions, avocado, blueberries, and pecans. -- In a small bowl, mix the dressing ingredients. -- Pour the dressing over the salad and serve immediately!

recipeNotes.en-US: Feel free to swap the pecans or leave them out for a nut free meal. -- Feel free to swap the arugula for another green, spinach works well. -- Looking for some extra nutrition? Add some hempseeds and/or a crushed clove of garlic

title.en-US: Roasted Tomato and Red Pepper Soup

description.en-US:
I may be biased, but this soup is pure perfection. It is delicious, filling, and nutrient dense. This creamy soup is a staple in our home, I am sure after one mouth watering bite, it will become a staple in your home as well! 

The roasted tomatoes share their beautiful red color, the garlic and basil add to its unique flavor. 

This soup pairs well with fresh bread, a grilled cheese and/or pesto sandwich.

ingredients.en-US: * Roasted Tomatoes--2 lb plum or Roma tomatoes, quartered -- 1 red pepper, quartered -- 3 tbsp olive oil -- 1/2 tsp sea salt -- 1/2 tsp black pepper --* Soup--4 tbsp water (or olive oil) -- 1 cup yellow onion, chopped -- 3-4 cloves of garlic, minced -- 1/8 tsp crushed red pepper flakes (more for individual bowls when serving) -- 2 cups vegetable broth (homemade or store bought) -- 1/2 cup fresh basil

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper. -- In a medium size bowl, add the tomatoes, red peppers, olive oil, salt and pepper, mix it together. -- Spread the tomato and red pepper mix to the baking sheet in a single layer and roast for 40 minutes. -- In a soup pot over medium heat, warm the water, add the minced garlic and onion. Cook for 7-10 minutes, til the onions are translucent. --Add the chili flakes, tomatoes, roasted red peppers, basil, and vegetable broth. Give it a stir. -- Turn the heat to medium high, bring to a quick boil and then reduce the heat to medium low. Simmer uncovered for 35 minutes. Stir often. -- Once finished, remove the pot from the heat and allow it to cool for a few minutes. Carefully transfer the soup to a high powered blender, blend until smooth (may leave it a little chunky if you like). -- Taste and adjust sea salt, pepper and chili flakes, if needed. — Serve hot.

recipeNotes.en-US: Keeps well in the fridge for 3-4 days.

description.en-US:
A beautifully simple take on a plant-based classic, these Cauliflower Tacos are bursting with warm spices, vibrant flavors, and fresh toppings. Inspired by a favorite dish I once had at Bar Taco in Tampa, this homemade version is every bit as satisfying, without any of the fuss.

Crisp yet tender cauliflower is sautéed with aromatic garlic, red onion, and bold taco spices, then tucked into perfectly warmed taco shells and topped with fresh avocado, shredded purple cabbage, roasted corn, and a squeeze of lime. Light, wholesome, and effortlessly delicious, these tacos are as beautiful to serve as they are to enjoy.

Naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, and completely plant-based, this recipe is as nourishing as it is flavorful. Serve with chilled sparkling lime water for the perfect balance of fresh and cozy.

These tacos are perfect for weeknight dinners, Taco Tuesdays, or meal prepping for the week. Want extra heat? Drizzle with your favorite hot sauce or add a sprinkle of crushed red pepper flakes.

A simple, delicious meal that’s perfect for any night of the week.

ingredients.en-US:
__For the Cauliflower Filling:__ -- 3 tbsp avocado oil -- 2 cloves garlic, minced --  -- ¼ cup red onion, thinly sliced -- 1 medium head cauliflower, stems removed & broken into bite-sized pieces -- 4-5 tbsp taco seasoning (homemade or store-bought) --
__For Serving:__ -- 12-13 taco shells (hard or soft) -- Fresh cilantro, chopped -- Lime wedges -- Purple cabbage, thinly sliced -- Avocado, sliced -- Roasted corn -- Jalapeño peppers, thinly sliced

recipeDirections.en-US: Prep your ingredients: Slice, dice, and measure everything before you start cooking. -- Sauté the aromatics: Heat a medium-sized skillet over medium heat. Add avocado oil, garlic, and red onion, stirring frequently for about 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the garlic! -- Cook the cauliflower: Add the cauliflower florets and taco seasoning, stirring well to coat everything in the delicious spices. Sauté for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cauliflower is tender but still has a slight bite. -- Assemble your tacos: Load up your taco shells with the warm, spiced cauliflower, then pile on your favorite toppings, cilantro, lime, cabbage, avocado, roasted corn, and jalapeños. -- Serve immediately & enjoy! -- Want extra heat? Drizzle with your favorite hot sauce or add a sprinkle of crushed red pepper flakes

recipeNotes.en-US: If your taco seasoning does not contain salt, you will need to add some.

description.en-US:
What's your favorite beverage on a hot summer day? Mine is lemonade, ice cold lemonade (loaded with Vitamin C)! With summer around the corner, it's time to spice up our traditional lemonade and add a healthy boost of ginger and turmeric to the mix. 

Turmeric has traditionally been used as an anti-inflammatory, it is a traditional spice used in India that has recently become a popular "superfood" here (in the USA). 

Feel free to swap the honey for maple syrup to make this beverage vegan friendly. 

Cheers, to good health!!

ingredients.en-US: 4 lemons, skin removed -- 6-7 cups water -- 1 inch piece turmeric root, skin removed -- 1 inch piece ginger root, skin removed -- fresh cracked peppercorns -- honey or other sweetener of choice -- ice

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients as listed to a blender. -- Blend well until smooth. Adjust sweetness. -- Pour over ice, enjoy immediately.

description.en-US:
Do you love peanut butter? I sure do and if you are anything like me, success in discovering new ways to add creamy delicious PB into your treats equals a win! 

We are always experimenting with different granolas, most often unmeasured. A scoop here, a pour there... A few weeks ago I decided to make a peanut butter granola and my husband and daughter LOVED it. He suggested that I add some more sweetness to it and add it to our site, so here it is! 

Granola is such a great food to have on hand. It can be for breakfast, snacks, or dessert topping. Most store bought versions contain so many extra unnecessary ingredients (sugars, oils and other things you cannot pronounce). You can simply avoid them by making your own at home. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 3 1/2 cups rolled oats (gluten free if necessary) --1 cup unsalted peanuts -- 1 cup coconut flakes, unsweetened -- 1/3-1/2 cup maple syrup -- 1/4 cup peanut butter -- 1/3 cup coconut oil, melted -- 1 cup dried cranberries --  1/3 cup cacao nibs (or chocolate chips) optional

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 300°F. Line a baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper. -- In a medium sized bowl combine all the dry ingredients (except chocolate chips and cranberries). Melt the coconut oil and stir in the peanut butter and maple syrup, mix well). Add the coconut oil and PB to the granola and mix so the the oil glazes the oats. --  Spread evenly onto the lined baking sheet. -- Bake for 35-40 minutes, stir every 10-15 minutes. -- Add 3/4 cup dried cranberries, give it a stir - return to the oven for 5 minutes. -- Remove from the oven and allow it to cool before transferring it to an airtight container. Store for 2-3 weeks.

description.en-US:
I love a good oat bar, always have but most are packed with poor quality ingredients, loads of sugar and corn syrup.

This delicious PB Oat bar is made with rolled oats, hemp seeds, maple syrup, sea salt, raisins, peanut butter, etc. Good for you ingredients, to fuel your (and the kidddos) body!

Whether you are 'on the go' or have time to sit, sip and nibble away at this high fiber bar - it is a great choice to add to your day!
For gluten free, be sure your oats are GF certified.

Be sure to use a 9x6 pan, if the bars are pressed to thin (on a larger pan) they will be more crispy

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups rolled oats--1/2 tsp cinnamon--1/3 cup raisins--1/4 tsp sea salt (if peanut butter is unsalted)--2 tbsp hemp seeds--5 tbsp pure maple syrup--5 tbsps crunchy peanut butter

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350°F. -- In a saucepan, over low heat, add the peanut butter and maple syrup. Stir it often for about 2 minutes. Remove from heat. -- Add the remaining ingredients and mix it with a spatula. The mixture will be loose, like bread crumbs but will form a ball when pressed. -- Add the mixture to a 9x6 inch pan (I use a stainless steel one, will link it on my instagram post). Press the mixture down into the pan using your hands. Bake for 16 minutes. -- Slice the bars and allow to cool before removing from the pan.

description.en-US:
Buckwheat pancakes are an all time favourite of mine! They are hearty and filling. The kids enjoy this pancake as well. 

Buckwheat is naturally gluten-free, a great source of fiber, contains protein, and has many trace minerals - total powerhouse!
I top them with plenty of berries, nuts, seeds, etc.

This recipe here is dairy free. To make them vegan swap the egg for a flax egg (1 tbsp ground flax with 3 tbsp water).

For a thinner pancake add more milk (1 tbsp at a time).

To make your own almond milk, see my recipe [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/almond-milk).

ingredients.en-US: *Pancake -- 1 cup unsweetened (+ 2 tbsp) milk of choice -- 1 tsp apple cider vinegar -- 1 cup buckwheat flour -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon -- 1 medium banana, mashed -- 1 tbsp coconut oil, melted -- 1 egg (for vegan use flax egg)

recipeDirections.en-US: Heat a skillet over medium heat. -- In a medium sized bowl, add the milk and apple cider vinegar, stir and set aside for 4-5 minutes. -- In a medium sized bowl, sift in the buckwheat flour and add all the other dry ingredients, stir and set aside. -- In the medium sized (milk and vinegar mixture) bowl, add the mashed banana and coconut oil, and egg, whisk to combine. Now add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, stir to combine. Do not over mix. -- Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, add the batter to the skillet. Cook for approx 3-4 minutes on each side. Continue until all the better is finished. -- Serve with pure maple syrup, nuts, seeds, berries, and/or bananas.

description.en-US:
Whether you are vegan, dairy-free, lactose intolerant, or browsing some dairy alternatives... You have come to the right spot!

I myself am not a fan of cows milk and love experimenting with milk alternatives. For this great recipe all you have to do is soak your almonds overnight, then blend and squeeze it through a nut milk bag.

I prefer adding some dates for some natural sweetness, however, that is optional.

You can drink it on its own, add to smoothies, oats, and/or your latte... the possibilities are endless.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup raw almonds (soaked in filtered water overnight, then rinsed) -- 4-5 cups filtered water -- pinch of sea salt -- 2-3 dates, pitted (optional) -- dash of cinnamon (optional)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a high powered blender, blend until smooth (about 2 minutes) -- Strain the almond milk mixture using a nut milk bag. -- Store the almond milk in an airtight bottle  and refrigerate for 3-4 days, best if consumed by day 3. Shake well before consuming.

title.en-US: Feta And Walnut, Arugula Salad With Blackberry Dressing

description.en-US:
This salad is perfect, it is a simple and light salad that brings so much color to the table. It makes "eating the rainbow" easy and delicious! 

To increase that heartiness of the salad, add some cooked quinoa, hemp seeds, or any other protein of choice!

Whether you are entertaining guests or eating alone, this salad definitely sparks dinner table joy!

ingredients.en-US: *Salad--1/3 cup blackberries--1/3 cup strawberries--1/4 cup chopped walnuts--1/4 cup feta--1 cup cooked quinoa--2 tbsp balsamic vinegar--4 cups arugula--sea salt and pepper to taste--squeeze of lemon--hemp seeds (optional)--*Dressing--1 1/2 tbsp balsamic vinegar -- 1/4 cup blackberries--2 tbsp olive oil

recipeDirections.en-US: In a large bowl, add the arugula, sliced strawberries, blackberries, crumbled feta, walnuts, hemp seeds and quinoa (if using). -- For the dressing, add the dressing ingredients (1/4 cup blackberries, 2-3 tbsp olive oil, 1 1/2 tbsp balsamic vinegar and squeeze of lemon) to a blender. Blend until smooth. -- Serve the greens into serving bowls and drizzle with the dressing.

description.en-US:
I am not sure there is a greater comfort food then delicious, warm, fresh baked banana bread.

The sweet smell takes over the entire home... Five minutes after the pan is placed into the oven, my husband and kids will make their way into the kitchen to see what I have to fill their bellies!!

I have added walnuts, hemp seeds, millet seeds, and chia seeds to bump up the nutritional value and give it a little extra crunch.

Go ahead and pull out your 9 x 5 inch pan and let's start baking!

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups spelt flour (white spelt, feel free to mix other similar flours) -- 1/3 cup coconut sugar -- 1/3 cup melted butter (or vegan butter) -- 1 egg (use flax egg for vegan) -- 1 tsp pure vanilla -- 2 tbsp maple syrup -- 3 large ripe bananas -- 1 tsp baking soda -- 3/4 cup chopped walnuts -- 3 tbsp hemp seeds -- 3 tbsp millet seeds -- 1 tsp sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350° F. Prepare a 9 x 5 inch baking pan with butter or coconut oil. -- In a medium size bowl whisk together the coconut sugar, melted butter, egg, vanilla, maple syrup, mashed banana and baking soda. -- In another medium sized bowl, sift in the flour and then add the remaining ingredients. Stir to combine. -- Add the dry mixture to the wet mixture bowl. Stir just until the flour is no longer visible, do not over stir (the more you stir, the more tough the bread will become). -- Now add the mixture to the 9 x 5 inch baking pan. -- Place to pan into the oven and bake for 50-55 minutes. The bread's edges should be golden brown. Test the bread by inserting a toothpick into the center, it should come out clean. Let the bread cool for 10 minutes and then remove from the pan, slice and enjoy! -- Store in an airtight container for 3-4 days (definitely doesn't last that long in our home).

description.en-US:
Raw, vegan friendly brownies... ohhh-ho, yes please! I honestly snack on one of these delicious brownies on my way out the door, on-route to the gym.  They are that good for you, truly functional food.

Dates are a great source of energy, blended with walnuts, hemp seeds, and other amazingly nutritious ingredients. These brownies are not only nutrient dense and a great source of protein, they are also guilt free... And what a great option to satisfy any sweet craving.

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups walnuts -- 2 1/2 cups pitted Medjool dates, loosely packet -- 1/2 cup hemp seeds -- 1 /2 cup raw cacao powder -- 1/4 cup cacao nibs -- 1 tsp sea salt -- 1-2 tbsp water (if needed)

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare a 9x5 inch pan by lining with unbleached parchment paper, set aside. -- Using a food processor, add the walnuts and process into a fine crumb. -- Add the remaining ingredients to the food processor, process until combined well. Should be a sticky paste like consistency. If the desired consistency is not reached, add 1 tbsp of water at a time until met. -- Remove the mixture from the processor and add to the 9x5 inch pan, press it down using your hands. -- Place the pan into the refrigerator for 1-2 hours. Remove the pan and cut the brownies into squares. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 1 week or freezer for up to 1 month.

description.en-US:
I am not sure anyone could love guac as much as me!! Ok, so maybe there is someone, somewhere... like that lady who sings the guacamole song on youtube (go ahead and google it, you will be singing it for days).

Guacamole is perfect! Avocados are amazing, they are high in potassium, high in healthy fats, incredibly nutritious (vitamin K, C, E,, folate, B5, B6, etc), loaded with fiber, and taste amazing... Pure perfection! Now add garlic, lime, cumin, sea salt, red onions, jalapeno... see where I am going with this? Guacamole can be a powerhouse of nutrition!

We serve this delicious guacamole with our quesadillas, nachos, chili, possibilities are endless.

Use avocados that are ripe, slightly indent when pressed.

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup of red onion, diced--1/2 lime, juiced--1/2 tsp cumin powder--1/2 tsp sea salt--1/3 cup fresh cilantro, chopped--1 jalapeno, seeds removed and diced--2-3 cloves garlic pressed or minced--3 ripe avocados

recipeDirections.en-US: Peel and remove the seeds of the avocados, add the avocado meat to a medium size bowl and mash (I like to leave mine a little chunky). -- Add the remaining ingredients and mix well. -- Taste and season to personal preference.  -- Serve immediately!

description.en-US:
Oats (of all varieties) are a breakfast staple in our home. They are easy to make, convenient, and nutritious.

What makes these overnight oats so great is that there is minimal morning prep and no cooking required!!

Play around with the toppings and make it your own. Have kids? Involve them in creating their own creations!

Be sure to use GF oats, if necessary.

ingredients.en-US: 1 ripe banana -- 1/2 cup rolled oats -- 2 1/2 tbsp chia seeds -- 3/4 cups nut milk (see my almond milk recipe) -- 1/4 tsp cinnamon -- pinch of sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: In a small bowl, smash the ripe banana. --  Add the remaining ingredients and stir to combine. -- Cover with a lid and store in the refrigerator overnight. -- Top with desired toppings and enjoy!

description.en-US:
Ummmm I will take a side of pickled everything, puhhlease!!! Something about pickled onions returns me to my sweet childhood days. My mama would make the most delicious sandwiches loaded with delicious fresh veggies and very often topped with her homemade pickled red onions - mouth watering delicious!

She would offer to make me one, I would often decline thinking that I was not hungry... until I saw the veggie loaded creation that sat high on her plate. I would then creep over and sneak bites, eventually taking over her whole sandwich (thank-you mama-bear)!

So back to my pickled onions, they are more then just a condiment to me.
Whatever dish you choose to use these easy to make pickled onions on, I'm sure you will love them as much as I do.

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup apple cider vinegar--1/2 cup water--1/2 tsp coconut sugar--1/2 tsp sea salt--1 red onion, sliced--2 cloves garlic

recipeDirections.en-US: Place all the thinly sliced red onions into a heat safe jar (I use weck or mason). -- In a medium sauce pan, add the remaining ingredients (not the onions). Bring to a simmer over medium heat and then pour it into the jar over the onions. --  Using a spoon, press the onions down into the vinegar. Allow them to cool at room temperature (about 30 mins). -- At this point they are ready to eat or store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks (best if eaten within 5 days).

title.en-US: 7 Ways to Get Your Children to Eat More Vegetables

description.en-US:
One thing I often get asked is how I get my kids to eat all their veggies, to be honest it makes me laugh because it can be so challenging at times, especially with my daughter!!

You see, I have one kid who eats just about anything I place in front of him (he's 7). Then there is my daughter (she's 4), who forces me to negotiate what vegetables she will allow on her plate. We try to continue to offer all types of different vegetables, and over time we have reached some success. Yahoo! I will take that! 

### So what can you do to increase your children's vegetable intake? 

1. __Hide them__... seriously! I hide spinach, other greens and veggies in their smoothies and dishes. Smoothies are personally one of my favourite ways as they provide raw, whole food nutrition to nourish their little bodies, and yet it takes so little time to prepare. Besides, how cool is it to be sipping a super cool green smoothie!

Here are some of their favorite smoothies. 

Classic Green Smoothie [Here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/classic-green-smoothie)

Good Morning Green Goddess [Here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/good-morning-green-goddess)

Lemon Blueberry Shhhh Smoothie [Here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/lemon-blueberry-shhhh-smoothie)

2. __Educate them__. Teach them about vegetables and how important they are to help build strong bodies. There are so many books and online resources available. 

  This [here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ds6tUxatnTs) was one of my       favorite YouTube videos (proceed with caution, it is a catchy song).  

3. __Start a garden together__  This has become one of our favourite things to do. We visit the nursery as a family, pick out what we would like to grow, then water and harvest what we need for our meal. It's very rewarding as it teaches them the process from seed to table - such magic!

Our kids actually talk to the plants while watering them. My daughter tells them how nice they are and that she loves them! 

![little girl gardening](https://i.imgur.com/4MdhPCc.jpg)

![child gardening](https://i.imgur.com/dPDEjYr.jpg)

![pepper from our garden](https://i.imgur.com/wn1ofMk.jpg)

![fresh tomates](https://i.imgur.com/fHS0mEQ.jpg)

4. __Cook with them.__ Our children love kitchen work, they love to help prepare meals and do the dishes (sometimes they make more of a mess). I often let them pick the star veggie of the meal, they wash and cut the veggies using a kid-friendly knife set that we found on Amazon. 

5. __Offer healthy dips.__ One of my daughters favorite snacks is carrots and hummus. We make homemade hummus in a variety of combinations. Carrots and beets are a fun and natural way to change the color of the hummus. (recipes linked [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/hummus) and [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/roasted-beet-and-chickpea-hummus)). 

6. __Make it visually appealing__. Offer a variety of different vegetables, different colors, cut them into shapes. We have all seen those AMAZING lunch meals that those "Mama Picaso's" have created, I have tried and kinda failed... but hey, they ate it and thought I was the best mama ever - mission accomplished!! 

![kids food](https://i.imgur.com/HCYcFS1.jpg)

![food art caterpillar](https://i.imgur.com/gsTtjJ9.jpg)

![tic tac toe with vegetables](https://i.imgur.com/DG5WXMR.jpg)

7. __Model healthy eating habits.__ This is a big one. Our kids will most often eat what we eat or eventually have similar eating habits. I love salads and enjoy a large salad daily. It has taken quite some time but even my children and hubby have joined me and eat fresh and colorful salads (almost) daily. Provide a positive and healthy relationship with food, it will eventually pay off!

Teaching our children about nutrition and assisting them to choose healthy food options is essential and can help them live a healthier life.   

Many diseases we face today such as diabetes mellitus type 2, obesity, cardiovascular disease, etc. are often food related and can be avoided in many circumstances. We as parents/caregivers have one of the greatest influences on our children.

Do you have any other tips you would like to add? Leave a comment and share your experience!

Have you ever taken a look at the ingredient list of a store bought salad dressing bottle and wonder why they add so many of the ingredients to their products? Me too - so let's make our own healthy version instead!

This Basic ACV (Apple Cider Vinegar) dressing is our staple for our home,  I prepare it weekly. We use it on our salads, quinoa bowls, a little drizzle here and a little drizzle there... It's the perfect addition to any fresh veggie bowl.

This delicious dressing is not only quick and easy to make, it includes a handful of simple, "good for you" ingredients like extra virgin olive oil, apple cider vinegar, garlic, dijon mustard, sea salt, and black pepper.

Go ahead and double batch it, I store mine in the fridge for 3-4 days. Just give it a shake before use!

ingredients.en-US: 1/3 cup extra-virgin olive oil--1/4 cup apple cider vinegar--2 cloves garlic--1 tbsp dijon mustard--1/4 tsp sea salt-- 1/4 tsp ground black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all of the ingredients to a high powered blender. Blend until the garlic is broken down.-- Enjoy immediately or store in the refrigerator.

metaDescription.en-US: This delicious dressing is not only quick and easy to make, it has just a handful of simple, "good for you" ingredients like extra virgin olive oil, apple cider vinegar, garlic, dijon mustard, sea salt, and black pepper.

description.en-US:
If you are looking for a smoothie loaded with antioxidant rich blueberries, this is it!

This smoothie has a handful of nutritious ingredients like blueberries, mango, kefir, walnuts and hempseeds. It is honestly the perfect start to your day, and a tasty way to get a nutrient boost first thing in the morning!

For a vegan version, feel free to swap the kefir for water, coconut water, or plant based milk of choice. 

If you would like to make your own plant based milk, click [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/almond-milk) for my almond milk recipe.

![blueberry smoothie](https://i.imgur.com/v59etNf.jpg)

Cheers to health,

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup frozen mango -- 1 cup fresh or frozen blueberries -- 1 cup kefir (may sub for another liquid or yogurt) -- 2 tbsp hemp seeds -- 2 tbsp walnuts

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a blender, blend till smooth. -- Enjoy right away!

metaDescription.en-US: If you are looking for a smoothie loaded with antioxidant rich blueberries, this is it!  This smoothie has a handful of nutritious ingredients like blueberries, mango, kefir, walnuts and hemp seeds. It is honestly the perfect start to your day, a tasty...

title.en-US: Healthy Lifestyle Habits - 7 Tips to Stay on Track

description.en-US:
When starting a healthy lifestyle or taking on a new healthy habit (whether it is exercise or diet related), it is important to stay on track so that the new lifestyle or habit becomes part of your daily routine. 

I know we all have busy lives, parenting, jobs, school, we have so many responsibilities... Ohhh, and let’s not forget about that never ending to-do list! There is a lot going on, I get it, but we need to make time for our health and well-being.

Whether you are trying to eat more healthy or be more physically active, here are 7 tips that have helped me stay on track with a healthy lifestyle.

1. __Plan your meals in advance__. I am a paper and pen kind of girl. I love to write things out so there is no question that this is a great method for me. As the weekend winds down on a Sunday evening, I will sit down on my desk and write out the meal plan for the week ahead. This helps me plan my grocery list (keeping unnecessary items from my grocery cart), and also prevents me from making unhealthy choices when I’m hungry between meals. . 

2. __Have healthy choices on hand__. I prepare healthy snacks and meals in advance quite often. It is great to have a nutritious snack available to grab when hunger strikes between meals. A few of my favorite snacks are these [No-Bake Carrot Cake balls](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/no-bake-carrot-cake-balls), [Chocolate Date Balls](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/chocolate-date-balls), or these [Cookie Dough Balls](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/cookie-dough-balls).

3. __Pack your snack and meals ahead of time.__ Whether you pack your meals the night before or in the morning, preparing in advance sets you up for success. You will be less tempted to indulge in unhealthy snacks and temptations. At work, walking into the lunch room can feel like walking into a buffet, however, I remain dedicated to my lifestyle habits and come prepared with my pre-packed healthy snacks. I can defeat those unhealthy temptations. This takes time, but I promise, it will get easier with practice. 

4. __Schedule your workouts.__ I like to schedule mine into my day planner in the beginning of the week (I do not let anything get in the way of my planned workouts). I attend Jiu Jitsu 3 times weekly and we have a home gym in our garage (saves time from going to the gym). We bike as a family, and hubby and I take turns running while the other stays back with the kiddos (Yes, we are busy but together we make working out a priority and make it happen). 

5. __Prepare for your workouts the night before.__ I like to workout in the morning so this is important to me. I put together my outfit the night before and set it on top of my dresser. It helps keep my motivation high, I get dressed, and I am pumped to break a sweat. I drink my morning coffee (in my workout gear), take my son to school, by the time I get home I am itching to get my workout in. 

6. __Use your time wisely and be mindful.__ I can not stress this enough. Avoid unnecessary screen time that can distract you from reaching your goals and keeping with your daily plans. If this is something you struggle with, a day planner may be a good option to help you stay on track. I love using my [daily designer](https://daydesigner.com/pages/academic-year-planners-cover-reveal?gclid=Cj0KCQjwgtWDBhDZARIsADEKwgOiJZl7BpU9ho-jGS5A4q2pbja7eB9uGqRX-4D12c42-u8fN10hW6AaAtM3EALw_wcB "dayplanner") to help keep me on track and staying focused.

7.  __Don't give up.__ If you fall off the wagon, get back on. If you make unhealthy choices, correct them, keep striving to make healthy habits, and stay focused to living a healthy lifestyle. 

These tips have helped me stay on track and keep me on track with my lifestyle. What has worked for you? Share in the comments below! 

Note: It is important to always check in with your health care provider before changing your diet and beginning a new exercise routine.

metaDescription.en-US: Do you need help staying on track with a healthy lifestyle? Here are 7 tips to help you stay motivated and on track with your health and fitness goals.

description.en-US:
If you love cake and are looking for a healthy, easy to make recipe using real, wholefood ingredients, then you are in the right place. 

This cake is light and fluffy with a fresh hint of lemon. It is sweetened with just the right amount of honey.

All you need to make this delicious cake are 7 simple ingredients, and there's a good chance that you may likely already have them in your kitchen. 

You will need: butter or oil, eggs, honey, vanilla, lemon zest, almond flour, sea salt, and baking soda. 

If you would like to top the cake - do it!! Add some fresh berries, frost it with cream cheese and maple syrup frosting, or add some slivered almonds... There are so many wonderful ways to serve this cake!

To store, I keep it in a sealed container in the fridge for up to 4-5 days. 

I hope you love this cake as much as we do!

Enjoy,

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 tsp butter or coconut oil --4 large eggs, room temp -- 1/3 cup honey -- 1/2 tbsp vanilla (use a good quality one) -- 1 tbsp lemon zest-- 1 1/2 cup fine ground almond flour -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 1/2 tsp baking soda

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350˚ F. Using oil or butter, grease an 8in cake pan. Set aside.--In a large bowl, add the eggs, whisk them lightly. -- Add all the other ingredients to the bowl, mixing them in using a spatula. Mix til the are combined. -- Using the spatula, add the cake mixture to the prepared cake pan. -- Bake in the oven for approximately 23-26 minutes. To test doneness insert a toothpick to the middle of the cake, it should come out clean. -- Allow the cake to cool for 10-15 minutes, remove the cake from the pan and let it cool on a cooling rack for 15-20 minutes before serving.

recipeNotes.en-US: For the lemon zest use just the outer yellow part for the lemon, not the bitter white rind.

title.en-US: How to Clean Your Yoga Mat

description.en-US:
How often do you clean your yoga mat? You should be cleaning your mat after each use, especially if you are doing hot yoga!

Just think, we transport our yoga mats to and from class, workout and sweat on them - YUCK! I bet if we swabbed our mats and sent the test to the laboratory all sorts of __unwanted__ __bacteria__ would be growing on them. 

I know what you are thinking, you are ready to lay your mat out and start cleaning. Keep reading, cause I am about to share some tips on cleaning your yoga mat.

Step 1: Get a spray bottle

Step 2: Prepare the Cleaning Solution

I use a 3:1 water and vinegar solution and add a few drops of my favorite essential oils (my favorites are lavender and eucalyptus). 
Vinegar is a natural disinfectant with antimicrobial properties - BEGONE BUGS!

Ingredients:
- 3 parts filtered water
- 1 part vinegar
- Essential oil of choice 

Step 3: Spray, spray, spray... wipe, wipe, wipe!
Spray the mat with the cleaning solution and wipe it up using a clean cloth. Do not allow the solution to sit for too long before wiping or the mat may pick up the vinegar smell (no worries if you do, the smell will go away)

Step 4: Let it Air Out
After cleaning the yoga mat it is important to let it air out before storing it away for the next session. I give it about 30-40 minutes to dry. This helps prevent the growth of any harmful bacteria and/or mold. The last thing I want is a *funky smelling* mat, so this step is essential!! 

Step 5: Weekly'ish, Do the Deep Cleanse
The length of time between deep cleanings will depend on how frequently you do yoga. I use my mat often so I deep clean it every other week. 
I spray my mat with the cleaning solution above, allow it to sit for 10 minutes with the solution on it. Next, I fill the bathtup up with room temp water and let it soak for about 20 minutes. Then I let the water drain out and hang the mat to let it dry out (this may take a few hours to overnight). 

It is important to note that all yoga mats are created differently so it is important to try a small patch test prior to cleaning the entire mat!

Note: It is important to check your yoga mat for specific instructions since essential oils are not suppose to be used on certain mat materials.

metaDescription.en-US: How often do you clean your yoga mat? You should be cleaning your mat after each use. Bacteria can harbor in our mats, following these cleaning tips will help keep your mat clean and the unwanted smalls away.

title.en-US: 5 Delicious Smoothie Recipes to Start Your Day

description.en-US:
Smoothies for BREAKFAST?? Yes, PLEASE! 

Smoothies are one of my "go-to'' breakfast options when I am rushed and having a busy morning. These work exceptionally well when I am running behind and trying to get my son ready for school and in class before the tardy bell rings (he can drink the smoothie on the way to school).

I am sure we have all heard time and time again how important it is to eat a healthy breakfast, smoothies are a favorite of mine as you can add so many healthy ingredients to it. I am sharing 5 delicious smoothie recipes from Bobbieleelicious.com that you can use to start your day.  

1. [Classic Green Smoothie](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/classic-green-smoothie)
This vegan friendly Green Smoothie has been a part of my mornings for over 10 years. It's the perfect combination of spinach, mango, nut milk, almonds, chia seeds and hemp seeds. It is so refreshing and delicious.

![green smoothie](https://i.imgur.com/BCkQL2V.jpg)

2.  [Peanut Butter and Chocolate Breakfast Smoothie](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/peanut-butter-and-chocolate-breakfast-smoothie)
This is my daughter's most requested smoothie. It is so delicious that she calls it dessert! 

![peanut butter and chocolate smoothie](https://i.imgur.com/lxU5X5F.jpg)

3. [Berry-Good Smoothie](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/berry-good-smoothie)
The name says it all... it is most definitely a berry-good smoothie and a berry-good breakfast option! My kids actually helped create this strawberry, blueberry, and pineapple blend. It is so simple, healthy, fresh, and delicious!

![berry smoothie](https://i.imgur.com/dHQSx5g.jpg)

4. [Lemon Blueberry Shhhh-Smoothie](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/lemon-blueberry-shhhh-smoothie)
We have many "shhhh-recipes" in our home, this is one of them. Our kids are not a fan of cauliflower so I decided to hide it in a smoothie, this is it! The kids love this blend and I am sure you will also!

![blueberry smoothie](https://i.imgur.com/RZr4wEh.jpg)

5. [Good Morning Green Goddess](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/good-morning-green-goddess)
This smoothie is so fresh that it will leave you feeling like a goddess! It contains spinach, mango, ginger, turmeric, cilantro, and a variety of nuts and seeds.

![green goddess smoothie](https://i.imgur.com/25MYG4w.jpg)

Eating healthy should be a reward and not a chore. 

Which one of these smoothie recipes are you going to try first?

Enjoy and happy sipping,

metaDescription.en-US: I am sure we have all heard time and time again how important it is to eat a healthy breakfast. I am sharing 5 delicious (adult and kid friendly) smoothie recipes from Bobbieleelicious.com that you can use to start the day.

description.en-US:
Summer is just around the corner, are you ready for the summer heat? We all know how important it is to stay hydrated and I have the perfect fresh infused water recipe to share!! 

This cold, carbonated water tastes amazing with the perfect balance of cucumber and mint, I am in love with this refreshing infusion!

This drink can be enjoyed on your own but also makes the perfect beverage to serve at a dinner. I love to add cucumber garnish to make it appealing! It brightens up any table setting!

Seen in this picture: I use these c[ocktail picks](https://www.crateandbarrel.com/set-of-6-cocktail-picks/s669326) and these [glasses](https://www.crateandbarrel.com/impressions-cooler-glass/s310980?st=Impressions%20Cooler%20Glass) from [Crate and Barrel](https://www.crateandbarrel.com/).

ingredients.en-US: 1 English cucumber, shaved into long strips using a vegetable peeler --1 quart water -- splash of fresh squeezed lime juice -- 8 sprigs fresh mint -- crushed ice--32 oz. carbonated water

recipeDirections.en-US: Cut the ends off of the cucumber, then using a vegetable peeler, shave long strips (lengthwise) and weave the onto 8 skewers. Set the garnish aside. -- Stir the water and lime juice together in a pitcher.  Add any leftover cucumber strips and the mint leaves. Allow the cucumber-mint water to sit in the fridge for 1-2 hours. -- Fill serving cups half full with crushed ice, pour the infused water over the crushed ice, filling it halfway. -- Top the cup off with carbonated water. -- Garnish with a cucumber skewer, a mint sprig, and/or and slice of lime. -- Enjoy right away!

recipeNotes.en-US: For alcoholic beverage, add gin or vodka.

metaDescription.en-US: This cold, carbonated beverage tastes absolutely amazing and is so hydrating in the summer heat. With the perfect balance of cucumber and mint, it is a great beverage to serve all summer long!

description.en-US:
I love turmeric, I love ginger, and I love salads! So obviously, I love this savoury "Citrus, Ginger, and Turmeric Dressing" drizzled on top of a salad. If you are a salad lover than I am sure that you will as well! 

This dressing is great on a green salad, drizzled on a wrap, or drizzled on a quinoa/veggie bowl - endless possibilities with this nutritious dressing!

This dressing keeps well in the fridge for 4-5 days.

ingredients.en-US: 3 tbsps tahini -- 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil -- 1 lime, juiced -- 1 tbsp fresh grated ginger -- 2 cloves garlic -- 2 tbsp fresh cilantro leaves -- 1 tbsp honey (or maple syrup) -- juice of 1 orange -- 3/4 tsp turmeric powder -- 1/2 tsp Himalayan sea salt (or to taste) -- pinch of black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all the ingredients to a blender, blend until smooth. Taste and adjust salt and/or honey as desired.

recipeNotes.en-US: Stores in the fridge for 4-5 days.

metaDescription.en-US: If you love salads, turmeric, and ginger... you will love this savoury "Citrus, Ginger, and Turmeric Dressing".  It is great drizzled on a quinoa bow, a wrap or green salad.

description.en-US:
Waffles are a classic breakfast and brunch option. We also enjoy them as a dessert in our home. Typically waffles can be unhealthy, however, this one has no white sugar, instead it uses maple syrup. We also swapped the white flour with oat flour (feel free to swap flours). Don't be fooled, it tastes just as good!

To serve this delicious golden waffle as a breakfast, top with fresh in season fruit and pure maple syrup. 

To serve this waffle as a dessert, top it with a chocolate-hazelnut spread, whipped cream, chocolate drizzle, icecream, fresh fruit, etc. 

However you decide to serve this dish, it will definitely be a crowd pleaser.

We all love this easy waffle recipe and I am sure you will as well! 

![brunch and a book](https://i.imgur.com/84U8tTN.jpg)

![breakfast in bed](https://i.imgur.com/0MipV3z.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 1 1/2 cups oat flour -- 2 tsp baking powder -- 1/2 tsp sea salt -- dash of cinnamon -- 2 large eggs -- 2 tbsp pure maple syrup -- 3/4 cup milk of choice  (adjust as needed) -- 1/4 cup coconut oil, melted -- 1 tsp pure vanilla extract

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the waffle iron. Prepare the iron as directed. -- In a large bowl, whisk together the oat flour, baking powder, baking soda, sea salt, and cinnamon. Set aside.  -- In another large bowl, whisk together the eggs, maple syrup, milk, coconut oil and vanilla extract. -- Now add the egg mixture to the oat flour mixture, stir to combine (may need to adjust wet ingredients, 1 tbsp at a time). -- Now with the heated waffle iron, add 1/4 cup of the waffle mixture to the iron. Cook as directed, until the waffle timer goes off and the waffle is golden brown on each side. -- Top as desired and serve immediately.

metaDescription.en-US: Healthy, oat flour waffles! So versatile, prefect for breakfast, brunch or  dessert! Top with fresh in season fruit, pure maple syrup, anything!

description.en-US:
I'm always thrilled when we have guests over, especially because it means I get to prepare and serve delightful appetizers like these Caprese Crostinis.

We're fortunate to have a kitchen garden where we grow tomatoes and basil. There's something incredibly gratifying about harvesting fresh produce from our backyard and sharing it with our guests. It's a labor of love that truly pays off.

Caprese Crostinis look stunning when arranged on a wooden serving board or a pristine white plate. The vibrant colors of the tomatoes and basil add a striking visual appeal, especially when complemented by a drizzle of balsamic glaze. They're almost too beautiful to eat, but trust me, they taste just as amazing as they look.

The combination of melted mozzarella cheese, fragrant basil, juicy tomatoes, and sweet balsamic is simply divine. I'm confident you'll enjoy every bite.

For an extra crunch, consider adding sliced red onions, avocado, and/or some fresh berries.

Enjoy,

Bobbielee

ingredients.en-US: 1 long baguette sliced into 1/2" thick pieces -- 2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil (used to brush the baguette) -- 2 cloves garlic -- 2-3 Roma tomatoes sliced into 1/4" thick slices (may sub for tomato on hand) -- 8 oz fresh mozzarella cheese sliced into 1/4" thick pieces -- 1 cup fresh basil -- balsamic glaze -- sea salt and black pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350°. Slice the baguette. -- In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper. -- Slice the baguette and brush each side lightly with the olive oil mixture. Bake for 6-7 minutes to toast it lightly. -- Top the toasted baguette slices with a slice of mozzarella, fresh basil, and tomato. Drizzle on some balsamic glaze. -- Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Caprese Crostini!

metaDescription.en-US: "Discover a mouthwatering Caprese Crostini recipe that's perfect for parties or as a flavorful appetizer. Made with fresh ingredients like cherry tomatoes, basil, mozzarella, and balsamic glaze, these crostini are sure to impress!

description.en-US:
The purple sweet potato is a gem that many people are unfamiliar with. This variety of potato is known for its high vitamin C and anthocyanin properties. It is nutritious and delicious, such a fun way to add more nutrients to the dinner table. 

This potato tastes similar to the (more popular) sweet potato, it is more dense with a drier texture and has a bit more of an earthy flavor. Your taste buds will thank you, this style of potato is delicious and such a delightful way to add some purple hue to your plate.

There are so many ways to top an oven baked potato, this recipe here is one that we love. Feel free to add more toppings, the sky's the limit!

ingredients.en-US: 5 medium sweet potatoes -- 3 tbsp extra virgin olive oil -- 1 tsp sea salt -- 1 tsp crushed black pepper -- crushed chili flakes (optional) -- 1 cup sour cream (vegan if necessary) -- 1/2 cup cilantro, chopped -- juice of one lime -- Toppings: fresh chives (chopped), cilantro (chopped)

recipeDirections.en-US: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and preheat the oven to 400°F. -- Wash and scrub the potatoes (I use a veggie brush). Dry them using a towel and poke them 5-6 times using a fork. -- Place the potatoes onto the baking sheet and put them in the oven. Bake for 50-60 mins. To tell if they are finished baking, insert a knife into the potato, there should be no resistance felt. -- Once finished baking, slice the potato half way lengthwise so that you can stuff the potato. -- In a bowl, mix together the olive oil, sea salt, black pepper, and chili flakes. -- In another bowl, stir together the sour cream, cilantro, lime juice, and pinch of sea salt. -- Using a silicone brush, brush each potato with the oil mix. Next, using a spoon add the sour cream mix to the potatoes. -- Serve warm.

recipeNotes.en-US: For vegan use vegan sour cream, I like the Kite Hill brand.

metaDescription.en-US: This potato tastes similar to the (more popular) sweet potato, it is more dense with a drier texture and has a bit more of an earthy flavor. This style of potato is delicious and such a delightful way to add some purple hue..

description.en-US:
Life is short. EAT CAKE FOR BREAKFAST! Healthy cake that is!  

Who doesn't love cake? So why not swap the traditional "unhealthy" ingredients and make a healthier cake that can be enjoyed as breakfast!  

This delicious cake is infused with chamomile tea and sweetened with the perfect mix of coconut sugar and honey. I used almond flour because, well... It's almond cake! I love the dense texture that almond flour gives this style of cake. 

I like to enjoy this cake while sipping tea or on the weekend for breakfast (best momma EVER). The cream cheese frosting is optional but definitely recommended for entertaining! Be sure to have fun decorating this beauty!

Life needs to be celebrated, what better way is there to celebrate than with a beautiful, healthy cake! 

*For double layer cake, double the recipe.

Bon Appetit!

![slice of naked cake](https://i.imgur.com/UzKV3bF.jpg)

![flowers in a martini glass](https://i.imgur.com/o4FEg5Z.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 1/2 cup water, hot (be careful not to evaporate) -- 3 chamomile tea bags -- 2 tbsp fresh squeezed lemon juice -- 3 tsp honey -- 2 tbsp coconut oil --  1/4 cup coconut sugar -- 1 3/4 cups almond flour -- 1/4 cup arrowroot flour -- 1 tsp baking powder -- 1/2 tsp baking soda -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- zest of 1 medium sized lemon --*Cream Cheese Frosting:--1 8oz package of cream Cheese (room temperature)-- 2 tbsps honey -- 1 tsp lemon juice

recipeDirections.en-US: Start by adding the chamomile tea bags to the hot water. Allow it to seep for 20 minutes. --  Preheat oven to 350°F and prepare an 8 inch cake pan with coconut oil.  --  To the boiled chamomile water, add the lemon juice, honey, and coconut oil, stir well and set aside. -- In a medium size bowl, add the flour, coconut sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and sea salt, combine. -- Now add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredient bowl, stir to combine. -- Stir in the lemon zest to the cake mixture. --  Now pour the cake mixture into the prepared cake pain and bake for 40-50 minutes. -- Allow it to cool for 10 minutes in the cake pan and then remove it from the pan onto to a wire rack. To remove the cake, I turn the pan upside down and gently tap it. -- While the cake is cooling, make the frosting. Add the cream cheese, 2 tbsp of honey and lemon to a medium bowl. Using an electric mixer, mix until creamy (about 2 minutes). --  Once the cake has cooled decorate with the frosting! -- Enjoy!

recipeNotes.en-US: For a double layer cake, double the recipe.

tools.en-US:
8 inch Cake pan 
or
6 inch cake pan (may need to increase the cook time)

metaDescription.en-US: Celebrate life, eat cake! This delicious cake is infused with chamomile tea and sweetened with the perfect mix of coconut sugar and honey. With only a handful of healthy ingredients, this beauty adds so much love and magic to any tea party or brunch..

description.en-US:
Are you ready to feel like a goddess? Yes, a salad eating goddess! All the greens, all goodness, in one bowl! 

This beauty of a salad is made with spinach, arugula, red bell peppers, onion, mint, and basil. For protein I added some pine nuts, pumpkin seeds, and chickpeas (feel free to swap nuts and seeds). A plant-based, fiber rich bowl!!

I blended up a homemade dressing and drizzled 1/4 cup over the salad. I highly suggest making a homemade dressing for the full goddess effect!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups spinach -- 1 cup arugula -- 1/3 cup red bell pepper, chopped -- 1/4 cup red onion, chopped -- 2 tbsp pine nuts -- 2 tbsp pumpkin seeds -- 1 tbsp mint leaves, torn -- 1 tbsp basil leaves, torn -- 1/2 cup cooked chickpeas (may use canned, drained and rinsed) -- 1/2 avocado, chopped -- Optional: 1/4 cup feta cheese, cut in cubes

recipeDirections.en-US: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients. Add 1/4 cup of the dressing and toss the salad. Toss it well so the dressing covers all the greens. -- Enjoy as 1 or share (Yes, I eat this entire salad to myself)!!

description.en-US:
This delicious dressing can go on just about any salad but pairs exceptionally well with the [Green Goddess Salad Bowl](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/green-goddess-salad-bowl "Green Goddess Salad Bowl"). 

With healthful ingredients like basil, parsley, apple cider vinegar, olive oil, dijon mustard, garlic, sea salt, black pepper

You will definitely have leftovers (lucky you!). Store the leftover dressing in the fridge in an airtight container for up to 3-4 days.

ingredients.en-US: 1/4 basil leaves -- 1/4 cup parsley leafs -- 1/4 cup apple cider vinegar -- 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil -- 2 tsp dijon mustard -- 2 cloves garlic -- 1/2 tsp sea salt -- freshly ground black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a blender container. Blend on medium-high speed until the basil leaves and garlic cloves are broken down into the dressing (45 seconds-1 minute). Scrape down the sides of the container using a spatula as necessary, be sure the blender is not running while you do this. -- Use right away or refrigerate the dressing in an airtight container for 3-4 days.

description.en-US:
If you like beets, you will love this wonderful beet, orange, and ginger juice recipe. 

With only three whole food ingredients, this juice is easy to make, it's refreshing, and leaves you feeling energized. This is why it's one of my favourite pre-workout beverages.

What makes beets so wonderful? Here are a few reasons why beets are good for you.

1. Beets contain vitamin C, potassium, magnesium, folate, and many other amazing nutrients. 
2. Beets have antioxidants that help protect against oxidative damage.
3. Beets contain naturally occuring nitrates that have been shown to have a positive effect in lowering blood pressure and improving blood flow (nurse.com).

Cheers!!

ingredients.en-US: 3 medium beets -- 2 medium oranges -- 1/4 inch piece of fresh ginger

recipeDirections.en-US: Rinse and peel ingredients. -- Place them through a juicer. -- Drink immediately for best taste and benefits.

metaDescription.en-US: With only three whole food ingredients, this juice is easy to make, refreshing and leaves you feeling energized (which is why it's one of my favourite pre workout beverages).

title.en-US: 5 Ways to Stay Active With Kids

description.en-US:
Schools out and summer break is in session! Finally - YESS!! Finding ways to keep our kids active this summer is on me and it is time to turn up the creativity! 

Physical activity is very important, most children require 60 minutes a day. As adults we are recommended to get 30 minutes most days of the week (unfortunately these requirements are not always being met). 

Staying active helps improve sleep quality, helps maintain a healthy weight, and helps with mood and behavior.

Some of our favourite ways to stay active as a family are:

__1. Get Outside!__ 

Go outside, get some fresh air, get your body moving, and enjoy the outdoors! We love going on family bike rides, walks, and hikes. Be sure to pack some healthy snacks and water.  

![hike](https://i.imgur.com/Rb8vyGD.jpg)

![familybike](https://i.imgur.com/acy6IEV.jpg)

![bike](https://i.imgur.com/jgVvmHS.jpg)

![kidsatthebeach](https://i.imgur.com/DDpp1uZ.jpg)

__2. Turn screen time into physical activity.__ 

I know we can get busy, sometimes we give our children an hour of screen time and it turns into more. Yes, we are all guilty of this! I like turning on things like "Go Noodle", "Cosmic Kids", or other videos to get them moving their little bodies! 

__3. Crank up the music and have a dance party!!__ 

No judgement here Mama (or dad). Crank your favourite music and dance like nobody is watching. And if they are, who cares! 

We have a few playlists and they are a combination of our 90's music, the kids favourites, and our favorite punjabi music.

__4. Create a circut (WOD).__

We have a whiteboard set up in our garage that my husband and I write out our workouts on. The kids also use this board to create their own workouts. They love coming up with their own routine, "baby crossfit" type of WOD (workout of the day). We use this time as an opportunity to teach them new exercises and proper form. 

__5. Play a sport with the kids.__ 

We joined Jiu Jitsu as a family. Outside of class we roll together on the mats at home, practicing the moves, streching, etc. 

You do not need to join a sport, simply get a ball and play basketball or soccer. Even if the kids say they don't want to play, kick/throw them the ball. Sometimes the simple act of getting started will set the mood and get them moving! 

![jiujitsu](https://i.imgur.com/vw7r4OE.jpg)

As parents we set the expectations, we are role models. What we do sets the norm for our children. Be active, eat well, and take care of your physical and mental health. Our kids are always watching.

For some nourishing smoothies to enjoy after all that activity see some of our family favorites: 

[Post Workout Smoothie 
](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/post-workout-smoothie "Post Workout Smoothie")

[Berry Good Smoothie](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/berry-good-smoothie "Berry Good Smoothie")

[Peanut Butter and Chocolate Smoothie](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/peanut-butter-and-chocolate-breakfast-smoothie "Peanut Butter and Chocolate Smoothie")

metaDescription.en-US: Schools out and summer break is in session! Finally - YESS!! Finding ways to keep our kids active this summer is on me and it is time to turn up the creativity!

description.en-US:
Oven roasted chickpeas are such a tasty snack, they are easy to make and store. They are convenient to grab and go and are a nutritious addition to any meal. 

I like to add these roasted chickpeas to my wraps, quinoa and salad bowls, pizzas, soups, and stews. They are also a great snack to enjoy on their own. 

There are so many different spice combinations, this recipe is one of our favorites. 

I hope that you enjoy this delicious and healthy snack as much as we do!

![Chickpeas on salad](https://i.imgur.com/4V19EZB.jpg)

![chickpeas on pizza](https://i.imgur.com/obwF1KI.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups cooked chickpeas (I cook mine in the instant pot, see notes) -- 2 tbsp avocado oil (may swap for coconut oil) -- 1 tbsp lemon juice -- 1 tsp coriander -- 1 tsp cumin powder -- 1/4 tsp turmeric -- 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper -- 1/2 tsp sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper, set aside. -- Be sure the chickpeas have been drained and dried. -- In a medium bowl, add all the ingredients. Stir to combine, ensuring that all chickpeas are coated evenly. -- Add the chickpeas to the baking sheet. Spread them in a single layer. Add them to the heated oven. -- Bake for 25-30 minutes, stirring and tossing them after about 15 minutes.  -- Remove from the oven and allow them to cool. The chickpeas become crunchier as they cool. -- Store in a sealed container, best if eaten within 2-3 days.

recipeNotes.en-US:
To cook chickpeas:
