Soak chickpeas in water overnight (or 8 hours), rinse. Add the soaked chickpea to the instant pot and cover with water. Cook for 30 minutes. Drain and rinse.

metaDescription.en-US: Oven roasted chickpeas are such a tasty snack, they are easy to make and store. They are a convenient, vegetarian/vegan friendly protein option to grab and snack on while on the go. They are a nutritious addition to any meal.

description.en-US:
Today is INTERNATIONAL DONUT DAY, let's CELEBRATE with... Broccoli!! Just joking, chocolate glazed DONUTS obv!!!

We love donuts, who doesn't?! Problem is most are fried, loaded with sugar and contain other nasty ingredients! 

These are healthier donuts. They are baked, refined sugar free, and made with almond flour (gluten-free friendly). BUT don't you worry, these delicious donuts don't skimp on flavor! Your taste buds will thank you!!!

Ready to make some donuts? Turn on some music, get your donut pan out, and set your oven to 350°F. Lets get this PARTY started!

ingredients.en-US: 2 1/4 cups blanched almond flour -- 1/2 cup coconut sugar -- 1 tsp baking soda -- dash of cinnamon -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 3 medium eggs -- 1/4 cup milk of choice -- 1 tsp apple cider vinegar -- 1 cup chocolate chips -- 2 tbsp coconut oil

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat oven to 350°F and oil a 6 count donut pan using coconut oil. -- In a medium size mixing bowl add the flour, coconut sugar, baking soda, cinnamon, and sea salt. Stir to combine. Set aside. -- To a small bowl, add the milk of choice and apple cider vinegar. Stir and set aside (5 minutes). It will thicken and curdle. -- Add the eggs to the milk and apple cider vinegar (ACV) bowl. Using a whisk, stir to combine. -- Now add the milk, ACV, and egg mixture to the dry ingredient bowl. Using a spatula, stir to combine. It will be a thick dough. -- Divide the mixture amongst the donut pan, fill them 2/3 full. -- Bake for 13-15 minutes. A toothpick should come out clean when the donuts are poked. -- Allow the donuts to cool in the pan for 5-10 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack. Allow them to cool completely before decorating. -- To make the chocolate glaze, warm the chocolate chips and coconut oil, stir to combine. Once melted, remove from the heat. -- Dip, decorate and enjoy!

recipeNotes.en-US: These donuts can be stored in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3-4 days.

metaDescription.en-US: These healthy donuts are baked, refined sugar free, and made with almond flour (gluten-free friendly). BUT don't you worry, these delicious donuts don't skimp on flavor! You are welcome tastebuds!!!

description.en-US:
This salad is such a great dish to add to any summer meal. It's crunchy, fresh, vibrant, healthy, and the flavor of the beetroot and carrot brings your tastebuds alive.

This salad is easy to make and can be stored in the fridge in an airtight container for 3-4 days. 

If you have leftovers, great!! It can be eaten as a salad but also make a great wrap or sandwich filler/topping.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 3 medium raw beetroots, peeled and grated -- 3 carrots, peeled and grated-- 3/4 cup medium red onion, chopped finely -- 1/2 cup fresh cilantro, chopped finely -- 3/4 cup chopped walnuts --*For the dressing: -- 3 tbsp fresh lime juice -- 3 tbsp apple cider vinegar -- 4 tbsp olive oil -- 1/2 tsp sea salt (add more if desired) -- crushed black pepper -- 1/2 tsp cumin powder

recipeDirections.en-US: For the salad: Add all the salad ingredients to a medium size bowl, stir to combine. -- For the dressing: Add all the ingredients to a small bowl, whisk to combine. -- Now add the dressing to the salad and toss until well coated.

recipeNotes.en-US: If you like to add a sweet taste to the salad add some grated green apple. Be sure that it is firm, otherwise it will become too juicy when you grate it.

metaDescription.en-US: This salad is such a great dish to add to any summer meal. It's crunchy, fresh, vibrant, healthy, and the flavor of the beetroot and carrot brings your tastebuds alive.

description.en-US:
Snack time can often be one of the most challenging times to keep our children's healthy eating goals a priority. We like to use snack time as an opportunity to increase their fruit and vegetable intake. 

That tempting snack cupboard, lack of time, and midday cravings can influence us to make unhealthy choices. We all get hangry, I get it! It's important to have a "snack plan" prepared so that we can have healthier snacking options available for our children.

I have shared 10 of our children's favorite easy prep snacks that help keep us on track. 

## 1. Apple slices with peanut butter

This is one of my daughters favorite snack. We cut/slice the apple and add peanut butter to it. Easy peasy!! 

Tip: Be sure to read the ingredient list on the PB and choose one without added sugar.

## 2. Carrots and hummus

Carrots and hummus is such a great snack option. Not only are you providing a serving of vegetables but by adding hummus, you are also adding protein and healthy fats.

Tip: You can buy hummus from the store or make one of the recipes available on Bobbieleelicious. 

[Hummus](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/hummus "Hummus")

[Roasted Beet and Chickpea Hummus
](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/roasted-beet-and-chickpea-hummus)

![carrots and hummus](https://i.imgur.com/jWMR3JP.jpg)

## 3. Bowl of fresh fruit.

Fresh fruit is always a great snack option, most kids love fruit. I prefer picking fruit that is in season to get the most nutrition from it.  

Tip: Introduce new fruits often. 

![berries](https://i.imgur.com/GPyZneW.jpg)

## 4. Plain yogurt with fresh fruit

Tip: Choose plain over flavored yogurt varieties. Flavored yogurt often contains sugar as a top ingredient. 
If you would like to make your own homemade yogurt, see my recipe [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/homemade-yogurt).

![yogurt and fruit](https://i.imgur.com/lIHcgCB.jpg)

## 5. Crackers, cheese, with veggies. 

![crackers, cheese and veggie plate](https://i.imgur.com/9ep0wGw.jpg)

## 6. A cream cheese and cucumber sandwich.

![cream cheese and cucumber sandwich](https://i.imgur.com/VXGkERj.jpg)

## 7. Smoothie

Tip: It is best to make your smoothies fresh at home. There are many varieties sold in stores, they often contain a lot of sugar and unnecessary preservatives. Also, fresh made smoothies taste much better and contain much more whole food nutrition..

Here are a few of my children's favorite smoothie blends:

[Strawberry-Mango-Banana Smoothie
](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/strawberry-banana-mango-smoothie)

[Peanut Butter and Chocolate Breakfast Smoothie
](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/peanut-butter-and-chocolate-breakfast-smoothie)

## 8. Mixed veggie plate

Try new veggies often to keep it interesting. Add dips and cheese to the plate. My kids love helping in the kitchen so I let them do age appropriate tasks, like peeling carrots and/or washing and cutting the produce with their kids friend kitchen knife.  

## 9. Mini charcuterie board

## 10. Kefir with a side of fruit

Kids typically eat 2-3 snacks daily. Offering a variety of healthy snack options is a great way to help reach and/or exceed the recommended 5 servings of fruit and vegetables a day.

If you enjoyed this nutritious list leave a comment and share any other healthy snacks your little ones love!

metaDescription.en-US: Snack time can often be one of the most challenging times to keep our children's healthy eating goals a priority. We like to use snack time as an opportunity to increase their fruit and vegetable intake.

description.en-US:
Strawberry Lassi is a delicious, creamy blend of fresh strawberries and yogurt. It is a refreshing drink, perfect for the summer heat! A great way to add a serving of fruit and protein to your daily intake!

All you need to make this healthy drink is a handful of ingredients: fresh strawberries, banana, yogurt, milk, cardamom, and a blender.

![Strawberry Lassi](https://i.imgur.com/fYbHdvu.jpg)

If you enjoy this Strawberry Lassi recipe be sure to also check out our [Mango Lassi ](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/mango-lassi)recipe. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 3 cups strawberries, washed and tops removed -- 1 medium banana -- 3/4 cup yogurt -- 1/4 cup milk -- 1/4 tsp cardamom powder -- optional toppings: chopped pistachios and/or a mint leaf

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to the blender, blend until smooth! -- Pour into serving glasses and garnish with chopped pistachios and/or a mint leaf.

metaDescription.en-US: Strawberry Lassi is a delicious, creamy blend of fresh strawberries and yogurt. It is a refreshing drink, perfect for the summer heat!   All you need is a handful of ingredients, fresh strawberries, and a blender.

title.en-US: 10 Great Sources of Protein for Vegetarians

description.en-US:
A common issue that many vegans and vegetarians may face is not getting enough protein intake, I was one of them. Just like with any dietary lifestyle choice, with the right knowledge and proper planning, protein intake should not be an issue.

Whether you are vegan, vegetarian, or just looking for some non-meat based protein options to add to your diet, here are the top 10 Vegetarian food sources that we use to meet our daily protein requirements. 

## 1. Lentils 

Lentils are a protein superstar! They are one of my personal favorites. There is 18 grams of protein in 1 cup of cooked lentils. They do not require any presoaking and cook in a short amount of time. 

Lentils are known for their high fiber content, folate, iron, phosphorus, and potassium. 

There are may types of lentils available, the most common being red, brown, and green varieties. 

I have linked our favorite Red Lentil Daal recipe [HERE](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/split-red-lentil-daal). This soothing daal is a standard Monday meal in our home. I often double the recipe so I have leftovers for a second meal throughout the week.  

## 2. Eggs

Eggs are a part of the vegetarian diet (not vegan). A medium egg contains approximately 6 grams of protein. There are a variety of ways to use eggs such as omelette, fried, or in your baking, etc. 

Eggs are also known for their omega-3 fatty acids, B vitamins, and choline.

## 3. Yogurt

Yogurt is a great option when it comes to protein. Regular yogurt contain 8 grams of protein per cup. Greek yogurt contains a whopping 23 grams per cup. However, check the ingredient section and nutrition label, there is often many sugars hiding in yogurt. To avoid unnecessary ingredients we choose plain yogurt over flavored varieties.

I like adding plain yogurt into our smoothies, parfaits, fruit bowls, and as dips. 

Want to make your own yogurt, here's my recipe for [Homemade Yogurt](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/homemade-yogurt).

## 4. Quinoa (Keen-wah)

Quinoa is quite popular in the world of health and for good reason. It is a great substitute for rice and caries amazing nutrient benefits. Quinoa is one of the few plant foods that caries all 9 of the essential amino acids making it a complete protein. 1 cup of cooked quinoa provides 8 grams of protein - pretty amazing!!

Quinoa is high in fiber, B vitamins, magnesium, iron, potassium, calcium, phosphorus, vitamin E and other beneficial antioxidants.

Quinoa is something I always have prepared in my fridge. I like adding 1/2 a cup to my salad bowls for some extra texture, crunch, and nutrient boost. 

## 5. Tofu 

Some love it, some hate it... I enjoy it in moderation! 

Tofu is a complete plant based protein made from soybeans, it also has iron and calcium. One serving of tofu contains 10 grams of protein. The problem that I often hear regarding soybeans is that they are highly modified. I get it, it's questionable. I therefore choose organic Tofu from companies I research and trust. 

Tofu doesn’t have much taste. When cooking with tofu it absorbs whatever you cook it and/or marinate it with. Choose favorable ingredients like onions, garlic, cumin and other spices to keep your dish interesting and your tastebuds happy.

## 6. Chickpeas/Garbanzo Beans (including other beans: black, kidney, etc.)

Whether you refer to them as a chickpea or a garbanzo bean, there is no doubt about the versatility and amazing nutrition that these magical beans can bring to your plate.

Chickpeas contain about 15 grams of protein per cooked cup. They are also an excellent source of fiber, complex carbs, iron, folate, phosphorus, potassium, and manganese. Cool beans!!

I like using cooked chickpeas in my chili, salads, hummus, and a variety of other Indian spiced daals. 

## 7. Green Peas

Green peas are great! In a 1/2 cup serving of green peas there is 4 grams of protein. They also contain fiber, potassium, vitamin C, vitamin A, and magnesium. 

If you are looking for an easy way to add more nutrition to your plate, green peas are a "winner-winner for that healthy dinner."

## 8. Hemp Seeds

Hemp seeds (actually are a nut) are a nutrition power house. There is 10 grams of protein in 3 tbsps of hemp seeds. They contain other great nutrients like phosphorus, potassium, magnesium, manganese, calcium, iron, and zinc.

Hemp seeds are eaten daily in our house, we add them to just about everything. I sprinkle them on our morning oats, parfaits, blend them into smoothies, add them to soups, and salads bowls. 

## 9. Chia Seeds

Chia seeds are a great addition to just about any diet. They are packed with nutrition and are an easy source of protein. A 28 gram serving of chia seeds delivers 4 grams of protein, fiber, omega 3, calcium, manganese, phosphorus and magnesium. 

Just like hemp seeds, I use chia seeds in smoothies parfaits, and in our morning bowl of oats. I also like to add them to our baked muffins, breads, etc.

## 10. Oats 

Oats are our morning go to, especially for the kids. They are easy to prepare and deliver many health benefits. A 1/4 cup of rolled oats provides 6 grams of protein. A 1/4 cup of steel cut oats provides 5 grams of protein. 

We enjoy our oats in a variety of ways. We eat them as a base for a breakfast bowl, we add them into our smoothies, or we grind them into a flour and use it to replace other flours (in our pancakes, muffins, breads, etc). 

To bump up that protein and nutrition level to your morning breakfast bowl of cooked oats, go ahead and add some nuts, seeds, and fresh fruit! 

### _Resources:_

[https://www.mayoclinic.org/healthy-lifestyle/nutrition-and-healthy-eating/expert-answers/lentils/faq-20058072
](https://www.mayoclinic.org/healthy-lifestyle/nutrition-and-healthy-eating/expert-answers/lentils/faq-20058072)

[https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/11-proven-benefits-of-quinoa#TOC_TITLE_HDR_2](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/11-proven-benefits-of-quinoa#TOC_TITLE_HDR_2)

[https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/protein-for-vegans-vegetarians](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/protein-for-vegans-vegetarians)

metaDescription.en-US: Top 10 Vegetarian food sources that we use to meet our daily protein requirements. Whether you are vegan, vegetarian or just looking for some meatless options to add some extra protein to your diet...

description.en-US:
This Oven Roasted Cauliflower dish is absolutely delicious! It is one of my favorite go-to side dishes. It is easy to make, packed with flavor, and can be used in many ways.

We use a delicious variety of Indian spices to make this dish, I am certain that you will love it as much as we do!

We typically eat this side dish with quinoa, on a veggie wrap, or as a side with roti and daal.

ingredients.en-US: 1 small cauliflower, cut into small florets--1 large red onion, cut into small wedges -- 5 tbsp tomato sauce (or 1 tomato blended) -- 3-4 tbsp extra virgin olive oil--1 tsp turmeric--3/4 tsp sea salt -- 1 tsp chili flakes--2 garlic cloves, very finely minced--1/2 lemon, juiced

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a baking pan with unbleached parchment paper, set aside. -- In a medium size bowl add the tomato sauce, olive oil, turmeric, sea salt, chili flakes, garlic, and lemon, whisk together. -- Add the cauliflower and onion to the bowl. Coat the cauliflower with the sauce. Be sure that the cauliflower and onion is evenly coated. -- Add the cauliflower and onion to the prepared baking pan. Add it to the oven and bake for 40-45 minutes. After cooking for 20 minutes, turn the cauliflower and onion over. -- Serve warm. -- Will keep well in the fridge in an airtight container for up to 4 days.

metaDescription.en-US: This Oven Roasted Cauliflower dish is absolutely delicious! It is one of my favorite go-to side dishes. It is easy to make, packed with flavor, and can be used many ways.

description.en-US:
Fresh juice is one of my favorite ways to boost energy in the afternoon. I often use this juice to  replace my afternoon coffee or tea.

This refreshing Kal'icious Blend is made with kale, pineapple, apple, celery, ginger, and lemon. It is easy to make, provides a boost of energy and nutrients, and adds a little ZING to your day!

I recommend drinking this juice right after running it through the juicer, but you may store it away in the fridge in an airtight bottle for up to 24 hours (if the juice sits, it will separate). Be sure to stir it before serving.

As always, choose organic when possible.

Happy Juicing, friends!

ingredients.en-US: 4 leaves kale, stems included -- 1 cup pineapple, chopped -- 1 apple, cored and sliced -- 4 stalks celery --  1/2 inch piece ginger, I leave the skin on -- 1/2 lemon, skin removed

recipeDirections.en-US: Start by washing all of you produce well, set aside. -- Add the ingredients to the juicer. -- Drink immediately or serve chilled.

tools.en-US: The Juicer that I use is an Omega 8006.

metaDescription.en-US: This refreshing Kal'icious Juice Blend is made with kale, pineapple, apple, celery, ginger, and lemon. It is easy to make, provides a boost of energy and nutrients, and adds a little ZING to your day!

description.en-US:
These delicious popsicles are easy to make and require just 4 whole ingredients. 

They are the perfect creamy blend of strawberries, coconut, lime, and maple syrup. Feel free to adjust the sweetness level. 

These frozen treats are vegan friendly and so delicious! 

![biten popsicle](https://i.imgur.com/ktypNmV.jpg)

If you love this recipe, be sure to check out our other tasty popsicle recipes.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 lb strawberries, hulled--1/2 cup full fat coconut milk--1/4 cup lime juice--1/4 cup pure maple syrup

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all of the ingredients for the popsicles to a high-powered blender. Blend until smooth. Taste mixture and adjust sweetness. -- Spoon the strawberry coconut mixture into popsicle molds. Place cover on the mold and insert popsicle sticks into the center of the popsicle. -- Set them in the freezer for 5 to 6 hours. -- When ready to eat, run them under the water for 10-15 seconds to release them from the popsicle mold.

metaDescription.en-US: These delicious popsicles are easy to make and require just 4 ingredients. They are the perfect creamy blend of strawberries, coconut, lime, and maple syrup. Feel free to adjust the sweetness level.   These frozen treats are vegan friendly and delicious!

description.en-US:
Applesauce is simply a delicious sauce made of apples and spices. Our kiddos love this recipe and I am certain that you will as well!

Our favorite apples to use in applesauce are MacIntosh, Fuji, Gala, and/or Jonagold. I often just mix whatever apples I have on hand!

We like adding cinnamon and nutmeg to ours but feel free to leave it out (I highly recommend adding the spices). 

If you prefer a more chunky applesauce do not add it to the blender. Use a potato masher or the back of a wooden spoon and break it up to your desired consistency. 

This sauce stores well in the fridge for 5-6 days or up to 2 months in the freezer (in an airtight container).

![apple sauce and spice](https://i.imgur.com/7RQVth1.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 8 apples, peeled, cored, and roughly chopped -- 1 tsp ground cinnamon -- 1/8 tsp ground nutmeg -- 1/8 tsp ground cloves -- 1 cup water

recipeDirections.en-US: Prepare  all the ingredients as listed and add them to a large pot. Give them a stir to combine. -- Over medium-high heat, bring it to a boil and then reduce it to low, cover and simmer. Occasionally stir the apples while they are cooking. Cook until they are tender (25-30 minutes). -- Once finished cooking the apples, transfer them to a blender and blend until smooth (about 1 minute). -- Applesauce can be served warm or cold, we prefer chilled.

metaDescription.en-US: Applesauce is simply a delicious sauce made of apples and spices. This sauce is a great snack option for school lunches. Our kiddos love this recipe and I am certain that you will as well!

description.en-US:
This Cuban Black Bean recipe is one of our "go-to" dishes when I know we are going to have a busy day. I simply prepare them in the Instant Pot, go about my business and have a meal ready in no time. 

Black beans are a great source of plant based protein. They contain approx. 8 g of protein per 1/2 cup of cooked beans. Black beans are incredibly versatile, we make extra and can also use them for 2-3 separate meals if needed during the week. We use them on our rice or quinoa bowls, in our burritos, on our loaded nachos, and for our quesadillas after ground up to a paste... Yummm!! 

These Cuban Black Beans store well in the refrigerator in an air tight container for about 3-4 days. They also store well in the freezer for 1-2 months. When freezing, I separate them into small (1/2 cup or 1 cup) portions so I can pull out just enough for a single meal.  

Hope you enjoy this delicious recipe as much as we do!

Bobbie, xo

![Quesadilla](https://i.imgur.com/FOVwwdQ.jpg
)

ingredients.en-US: 1 lb dried black beans (about 2 cups)-- 2 teaspoons avocado oil--1 small yellow onion, diced--1 green bell pepper, seeded and diced-- 3 cloves garlic minced--1 teaspoon salt -- 1 teaspoon ground cumin powder--1 teaspoon dried oregano--2 bay leaves--3 cups water-- Optional toppings for serving: cilantro, diced avocados, sliced jalapeno, sliced red onion, diced cherry, and/or lime juice

recipeDirections.en-US: Rinse and sort the beans. Discard any bad ones. Set aside. -- On the Instant Pot select the SAUTE  mode and add the avocado oil. Once the oil is hot and, add the onion, and bell pepper, and garlic. Cook until the vegetables soften. This will take about 5-6 minutes. Add in the salt, cumin powder, oregano, and bay leaves, give in a stir. Continue to cook until the spices are fragrant, about 25 seconds. -- Now add the black beans to the Instant Pot, give it a stir. Now add the water, stir again. -- Close the lid and seal the Instant Pot (refer to instruction manual for proper usage). Cook the beans on High setting for 33 minutes, allow the pressure to release naturally. -- Once the pressure is released, remove the lid and use the beans as desired.

metaDescription.en-US: This Cubin Black Bean recipe is one of our "go-tos" when I know we are going to have a busy day. I simply prepare them in the Instant Pot, go about my business and have a meal ready in no time.

description.en-US:
It is fall and I am here for all the delicious squash. Especially my favourite - butternut squash! This recipe here is an Indian dish that I was introduced to by my  mother-in-law, it was love at first bite. After just one bite I was hooked, this has become one of my favorite sabzis (Indian dish made with vegetables). I am sure that you will love this as well!

This sabzi is not only easy to make but requires just a handful of ingredients. Butternut squash is loaded with vitamins, minerals, fiber, and antioxidants. A tasty and healthy meal, double win!

You can serve this dish with rice, quinoa, roti, or spooned on top on a salad bowl! The possibilities are endless! 

Cheers to health,

Bobbie, XO

ingredients.en-US: 4 1/2 cups Butternut squash, peeled, seeded, and chopped -- 1 tsp cumin seed -- 1 large Onion,  sliced thin or chopped -- 2 tsp ginger, grated -- 4-5 cloves garlic, grated -- 3 tomatoes, diced --3 green chilies slit lengthwise --3/4 tsp turmeric --1 tsp red chili powder -- 1 tsp coriander powder -- salt and pepper, to taste --2 tbsp avocado oil -- 1/2 lemon, juiced (or if you have it, amchur, dry mango powder) -- 2 tbsp cilantro, chopped

recipeDirections.en-US:
Peel the squash (using a vegetable peeler), remove the seeds, and chop it into approx 1 inch pieces. -- In a large pan, heat the oil and add the cumin seeds. Once you hear the seeds crackle, add the chopped onions.  Cook them until they are translucent, about 4-5 minutes. -- Now add the grated ginger and garlic, and cook for a minute, stir often. -- Now add the green chilies and cook for a minute more, stir often. -- Now add the chopped tomatoes to the pan and sauté until the tomatoes are soft and mushy. -- Now add the turmeric, chili powder, coriander, lemon or amchur powder (if you have it), salt, and pepper. Sauté for 1 minute. --  Add the chopped squash to the pan and mix well. Cook the butternut squash subzi over a low flame until it is tender (about 20 mins). Add a few tsp of water to prevent it from sticking and burning to the bottom of the pan. -- Taste and adjust salt as desired. -- 
Garnish with cilantro and enjoy! -- Serve with a side of rice or quinoa, roti or naan.

metaDescription.en-US: It is fall and I am here for all the delicious squash, especially butternut squash! This Butternut Squash Sabzi here is an Indian dish that I was introduced to by my  mother-in-law. It's delicious and packed with healthy ingredients and a burst of spice!

title.en-US: Pumpkin Puree in the Instant Pot

description.en-US:
It's fall and I am ready for all things PUMPKIN! So today I am sharing the most easy way to cook a raw pie pumpkin in the Instant Pot! It is so easy, and in my opinion boosts the quality, freshness, and flavor of your baked treats (oppose to canned pumpkin)!

All you need is a pie pumpkin (aka sugar pumpkin) and water!
When choosing a pumpkin, I find a 3-4 lb pumpkin works best in our 6 quart Instant Pot. We pressure cook on high and allow the pressure to release naturally.

Do not throw out those pumpkin seeds, they are nutrition packed! 

Here's our [Roasted Pumpkin Seed](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/roasted-pumpkin-seeds "Roasted Pumpkin Seeds") recipe.

ingredients.en-US: 1 3-4 lb pie pumpkin (aka sugar pumpkin) -- 1 3/4 cup water

recipeDirections.en-US: Wash the pumpkin well and dry it. -- Using a paring or serrated knife, cut  a circle around the stem (top of pumpkin). Make the circle large enough to fit your hand in. -- Using a spoon and your hand, remove the seeds. Feel free to toss them or set them aside to bake at a later time. --  Set a wire rack into the instant pot. Add the water and place the pumpkin (top up) onto the wire rack. Close the lid so it locks and seal the pressure valve. Set the instant pot on high and cook for 20 mins, allowing the pressure to release naturally. -- Once complete, allow the pumpkin to cool and remove the pumpkin skin, leaving just the flesh. Mash it and find a delicious fall recipe to make!

metaDescription.en-US: Pureed pumpkin in the Instant Pot! It is so easy to make and boosts the quality, freshness, and flavor of your fall baked treats!

description.en-US:
If you are looking for the perfect fall snack, this Apple and Pumpkin Bread
is it! This has been one of our standard seasonal breads that we bake every year.  

This moist bread has just the right amount of natural sweetness and is balanced perfectly with the right amount of apple, pumpkin, and a handful of delicious spices. 

For this recipe I use my homemade Pumpkin Puree made in the Instant Pot ([recipe here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/pumpkin-puree-in-the-instant-pot "pumpkin Puree in the Instant Pot")) but feel free to swap and use a 16 oz can of Pureed Pumpkin. As well, I use my Homemade Applesauce ([recipe here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/homemade-applesauce "Homemade Apple Sauce")) but feel free to swap for any minimally processed store bought brand. Just be sure to watch out for any hidden sugars (Hint: check the ingredient list).

Feel free to play around with the flour. I typically make my loaf using a whole grain spelt flour mixed with a white spelt flour. I have also had success with a variety of other flours.

If you like some crunch to your bread, stir in some walnuts to the batter before baking.

Enjoy! 

![slice of the pumpkin and apple loaf](https://i.imgur.com/0ETXY8X.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 2 1/2 cups flour -- 1 1/4 tsp cinnamon powder -- 1/4 tsp nutmeg -- 3/4 tsp pumpkin pie spice -- 1/2 tsp salt --  1 tsp baking soda -- 1 tsp baking powder -- 1 3/4 cups pumpkin puree -- 2 eggs (may swap for 2 flax eggs) -- 1/2 cup pure maple syrup -- 1/2 cup coconut sugar -- 1/2 cup coconut oil -- 1/4 cup apple sauce -- 1/4 cup raw pumpkin seeds

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the oven to 350° F. Oil 2, 9 x 5 inch loaf pan. Set aside. -- In a medium mixing bowl, add the flour of choice, cinnamon, nutmeg, pumpkin pie spice, 1 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp baking powder. Whisk it all together and set aside. -- In a large size bowl, add the pumpkin puree, 2 eggs, 1/2 cup pure maple syrup, 1/2 cup coconut sugar, 1/2 cup coconut oil,  and 1/4 cup apple sauce. Whisk it all together. -- Now add the dry mixture to the wet mixture. Stir well so that there are no dry lumps remaining. -- Pour the batter into the loaf pans, sprinkle on some pumpkin seeds and bake for 50-60 minutes. Test the loaf to be sure that is is finished baking by inserting a toothpick into the middle. It should come out clean. -- Allow the loaf to cool in the pan before removing it, this will prevent the loaf from falling apart. -- The loaf can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3-4 days. Enjoy!

metaDescription.en-US: Welcome fall with this delicious Pumpkin and Apple Loaf. This moist bread has just the right amount of natural sweetness and is balanced perfectly with the right amount of apple, pumpkin, and a handful of delicious spices.

description.en-US:
Are you looking for something fun and easy to make with the kids or just for yourself? This Crispy Rice Treats recipe might be just what you are looking for! 

These tasty treats are made with just 3 simple ingredients. They are easy to make, vegan friendly, and do not require any baking.

Feel free to cut the Crispy Rice Treats into standard squares or use cookie cutters to make them into creative shapes and figures. 

If you are gluten-free, be sure to use certified gluten-free ingredients. 

Also, be sure to check the ingredient label on the peanut butter. There are often other ingredients added to nut butters such as oil, salt, and sugar. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1/3 cup unsalted, creamy peanut butter (check the ingredient list there should just be peanuts listed) -- 1/3 cup pure maple syrup -- 3 cups brown rice crisp cereal

recipeDirections.en-US: Line a 8-inch square baking pan with parchment paper. Set it aside. -- Heat a medium size pan over low heat. Add the peanut butter, stir until it melts. Should take 2-3 minutes. Now add the maple syrup and brown rice crisp cereal. Stir until the cereal pieces are covered with the melted peanut butter and maple syrup. -- Now transfer the brown rice crisps to the prepared 8-in square pan. Press it down tightly using a spatula or your hands. -- Cover the pan with a lip, foil, or wrap and place it into the freezer for 30 minutes. -- Remove, cut and enjoy! -- Store covered in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.

recipeNotes.en-US: For gluten-free, use gluten-free labelled Brown Rice Cereal Crisps.

metaDescription.en-US: Indulge in our easy-to-make Crispy Rice Treats - a delightful snack made with just 3 ingredients. Perfect for kids and adults alike, these vegan-friendly treats require no baking. Try them today!

description.en-US:
Satisfy your sweet tooth without the guilt! Our healthier oatmeal cookie recipe is a game-changer for anyone looking to enjoy a delicious treat that's as kind to your body as it is pleasing to your palate.

Made with the goodness of almond flour, coconut oil, and a hint of natural sweetness, these cookies are the perfect solution for health-conscious dessert lovers. Whether you're whipping up a batch for a cozy night in or preparing a healthy snack for the week, this recipe is your ticket to a guilt-free indulgence. 

Plus, with vegan and gluten-free options, it's a crowd-pleaser that everyone can enjoy. Let's bake a batch of these heavenly cookies that promise flavor in every bite without the added guilt.

![pink plates and cookies](https://i.imgur.com/CwpCBHj.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 1 large egg or egg alternative (1 flax egg = 1 tbsp ground flaxseed mixed with 3 tbsp water, mix and let i sit for 5 minutes) -- 3 tbsps coconut oil, melted -- 1/4 cup coconut sugar -- 2 teaspoons vanilla extract -- 3/4 cup almond flour -- 1/2 cup rolled oats -- 1/4 teaspoon sea salt -- 1 1/2 teaspoons cinnamon -- Optional mix-ins: 1/3 cup chocolate chips of choice, 1/3 cup raisins, or 1/3 cup chopped walnuts

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.-- Prepare the flax egg in a small bowl, if using, and set aside to thicken. -- In a large bowl, whisk together the wet ingredients until well combined. -- In another bowl, mix together the dry ingredients.-- Gradually combine the dry ingredients with the wet, mixing until just combined.-- Fold in the chocolate chips, nuts, and dried fruit, if using.-- Scoop dough onto the baking sheet, spacing cookies about 2 inches apart. --Bake for 12-15 minutes or until the edges are golden.-- Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. -- Enjoy!

metaDescription.en-US: Discover our delicious and nutritious oatmeal cookie recipe, perfect for health-conscious sweet tooths. Made with almond flour, coconut oil, and natural sweeteners, these cookies offer a guilt-free treat without compromising on taste.

description.en-US:
Looking for a quick and satisfying treat for those post-school munchies or a guilt-free dessert option? Well, look no further!

This delightful chocolatey smoothie comes together in mere minutes, boasting just a handful of wholesome ingredients. It's so scrumptious that my little ones practically beg for it every single day.

Feeling adventurous? Amp up the nutrition by tossing in half a cup of spinach—it blends seamlessly, adding a boost of green goodness without altering the taste one bit.

So, are you ready to whip up some goodness? Grab your ingredients, fire up that blender, and let's get started!

Cheers to happy sipping!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups milk of choice (I use almond or cashew milk) -- 2 frozen bananas (be sure to use frozen) -- 2 Medjool dates, pitted -- 3 tbsp raw cacao powder -- 2 tbsp nut butter (I like almond or peanut butter) -- Optional: 1/2 cup spinach, cacao nibs (sprinkle on top)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a blender. Blend till smooth and enjoy cold!

metaDescription.en-US: Indulge guilt-free with our quick and tasty chocolate smoothie recipe! Perfect for after-school snacks or healthy desserts, this nourishing blend requires just a handful of ingredients. Plus, add a nutritional boost with optional spinach for extra greens.

description.en-US:
This healthy and delicious Greek salad makes a great meal or side option. Packed with fiber rich veggies and topped with a homemade dressing this salad is a nutritious addition to any menu. 

For a vegan friendly version, swap the feta cheese for another ingredient, e.g. cubed tofu. 

For some extra plant based protein, try adding chickpeas, hemp seeds, and/or cooked quinoa.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: *Salad -- 2 romaine hearts, chopped into bite size pieces -- 1 red bell pepper, chopped -- 20 cherry tomatoes, quartered --  1/2 cup cucumber, chopped -- 1/2 cup pitted Kalamata olives, roughly cut -- 1/3 cup red onion, thinly sliced -- 1/3 cup walnuts (optional to roast them) -- feta cheese -- lemon wedges -- Optional: serve with pita bread --*Dressing -- ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil -- 4 tablespoons red wine vinegar -- 1 lemon, juiced -- 2 garlic clove, minced --½ teaspoon dried oregano -- ¼ teaspoon Dijon mustard -- ¼ teaspoon salt -- Fresh ground black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US:
For the salad: arrange on a plate or bowl (as shown in photo) or add all the prepared ingredients to a large bowl and toss together. -- 
For the dressing: add all the ingredients to a small bowl and whist together. -- Now add the dressing to the salad

metaDescription.en-US: This healthy and delicious Greek salad makes a great meal or side option. Packed with fiber rich veggies and topped with a homemade dressing this salad is a nutritious addition to any menu.

description.en-US:
If you love PB & J sandwiches, you gotta try this recipe!!

This fun blend of strawberries and peanut butter was created with the help of my daughter who LOVES strawberries more then anything in the world, well almost anything in the world!!

With just a handful of ingredients and a blender, this smoothie can be yours in less than a few minutes. 

For dairy-free, use a suitable milk and yogurt of choice. For nut-free, swap the peanut butter for a sunflower butter or omit completely! 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups frozen strawberries -- 1 banana (may use frozen or room temp) -- 1/2 cup yogurt -- 1 cup milk of choice -- 1 tbsp hemp seeds -- 2 tbsps peanut butter (may swap for another nut/seed butter or omit)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to a smoothie container. -- Blend til smooth and ENJOY!!

metaDescription.en-US: If you love PB & J sandwiches, you gotta try this recipe. A fun blend of strawberries, peanut butter, and a handful of ingredients.

description.en-US:
Indulge guilt-free with our delightful Lemon Coconut Date Balls – the ultimate healthy snack option. These tasty treats are perfect for satisfying your sweet cravings and can be conveniently prepared in advance for whenever hunger strikes.

Crafted from a handful of wholesome ingredients including raw cashews, Medjool dates, unsweetened shredded coconut, chia seeds, hemp seeds, and zesty lemon, these date balls offer a burst of flavor and nutrition in every bite.

For storage convenience, we recommend keeping them in the freezer for a refreshing chilled treat, although they can also be stored in the fridge. Note that they stay fresh for up to a week in the fridge and up to 2 months in the freezer.

Treat yourself to these delectable Lemon Coconut Date Balls whenever you need a nutritious energy boost or a satisfying snack.

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup raw cashews (almonds work as well) -- 1 cup Medjool dates, pitted -- 1/2 cup unsweetened shredded coconut (divided) -- 2 tbsp chia seeds -- 2 tbsp hemp seed -- zest from 1 lemon--2 tablespoon lemon juice--pinch of sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Add the cashews to a food processor, pulse til chopped. -- Add all the other ingredients, except 1/4 cup shredded coconut (save til the end). Pulse until it is well combined. -- Roll the mixture into 1 tbsp size balls and roll it into the remaining coconut. (If the date mixture is sticking to your hands, slight dampen your hands to roll the ball)-- We store the balls in an air tight container in the freezer and enjoy them cold. They will be good in the freezer for up to 2 months.

metaDescription.en-US: Satisfy your sweet cravings guilt-free with our Lemon Coconut Date Balls! Made with raw cashews, Medjool dates, coconut, chia, hemp, and lemon zest, these freezer-friendly snacks are perfect for meal prep.

description.en-US:
Muffins are such a great snack to have on hand for quick and healthy snacks to fuel both kids and adults! 

With just a handful of wholesome ingredients, these muffins are quick to make and easy to store away. This recipe contains eggs but can easily be converted to vegan by simply swaping the honey for maple syrup, and the eggs for flax eggs. 

Whether you are looking for a breakfast or snack idea, these muffins are a winner! 

Enjoy! 

![strawberry muffins](https://i.imgur.com/nF8qsdc.jpg)

ingredients.en-US: 2 cups almond flour -- 2 large eggs -- 1/4 cup coconut oil, melted -- 3 tbsp milk of choice -- 3 tbsp honey (or maple syrup) -- 3 tbsp lemon juice -- 1/2 tsp baking soda -- 1/4 tsp sea salt -- 1 cup fresh blueberries (may swap for chopped strawberries or raspberries)

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat the over to 350 . Line a muffin pan with paper liners, set aside. -- In a medium size bowl, add all ingredients (except blueberries). Whisk together. -- Now fold the blueberries into the muffin batter. -- Using a spoon, add the muffin batter to the lined muffin pan. Fill them about 3/4 of the way full. -- Transfer the pan to the oven and bake for approx 18-20 minutes. The edges should be golden brown and a toothpick should come out clean when inserted in to the middle of the muffin. Allow the muffins to cool in the pan for about 10 minutes and then remove -- Enjoy!

metaDescription.en-US: Muffins are such a great snack to have on hand for quick and healthy snacks to fuel both kids and adults! Whether you are looking for a breakfast or snack idea, these fruit filled muffins are a winner!

title.en-US: Strawberry and Quinoa Baby Kale Salad

description.en-US:
Indulge in the vibrant flavors of our Strawberry and Quinoa Baby Kale Salad, featuring a delightful blend of fresh strawberries, hearty quinoa, crunchy almonds, and nutrient-rich baby kale, all tossed in a tangy balsamic dressing. This refreshing salad is further enhanced with the addition of creamy avocado, savory feta cheese, aromatic basil leaves, and sweet dried cranberries. 

Perfect for a light lunch, picnic, or potluck, this salad is a nutritious and delicious option for any occasion.

Cheers sweet friend, enjoy! 

Bobbie, XO

ingredients.en-US:
*Salad -- 1 cup cooked quinoa--2 cups fresh strawberries, sliced--1/2 cup almonds, sliced or chopped--4 cups baby kale leaves--1/4 cup red onion, thinly sliced--1/3 cup crumbled feta cheese--1/4 cup fresh basil leaves, thinly sliced--1/4 cup dried cranberries--
1 avocado, diced --* Dressing --1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil--2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar--1 tablespoon honey--1 teaspoon Dijon mustard--Salt and pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: In a large salad bowl, combine the cooked and cooled quinoa, sliced strawberries, almonds, baby kale leaves, thinly sliced red onion, sliced basil leaves, dried cranberries or cherries, and diced avocado.--In a small mixing bowl, whisk together the extra-virgin olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper until well combined to make the dressing.--Pour the dressing over the salad ingredients in the bowl and toss gently to coat everything evenly.--Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the top of the salad and toss lightly once more to distribute the cheese.--Serve immediately as a vibrant and nutritious salad, or cover and refrigerate for later enjoyment. This salad is perfect for picnics, potlucks, or as a light and healthy lunch or dinner option.

metaDescription.en-US: Indulge in the vibrant flavors of our Strawberry and Quinoa Baby Kale Salad, a nutritious and delicious salad perfect for any occasion. This healthy salad features strawberries, quinoa, baby kale, avocado, feta cheese, and more, tossed in a tangy dressing.

description.en-US:
Introducing My Mother-in-Law’s Secret Sauce

This vibrant green chutney has become a staple in our home — a versatile, all-purpose drizzle that’s as nourishing as it is delicious.

Crafted with a powerhouse blend of avocado (rich in healthy fats and potassium), white onion (antioxidant-packed and immune-supporting), cilantro (a natural detoxifier and excellent source of vitamin K), ginger (calming for digestion and wonderfully anti-inflammatory), garlic (loaded with vitamins C and B6), lemon (bright with vitamin C and citric acid), vinegar (to aid digestion and help balance blood sugar), and a medley of cleansing spices, this sauce doesn’t just add flavor — it may support your body’s natural detox pathways too.

The beauty of this chutney lies in its versatility. Use it as a fresh salad dressing, a drizzle over bowls or wraps, a dip for nachos and veggies, or even as a zesty topping for burgers. However you serve it, it brings a burst of freshness, glow-boosting nutrients, and a touch of love to every dish.

Tip: For best flavor and freshness, store in an airtight container in the refrigerator and enjoy within 5 days.

Here’s to health, flavor, and a little detox magic in every spoonful.

Cheers,
Bobbielee

ingredients.en-US: 1 ripe avocado --  1 bunch cilantro leaves, stems removed -- 10 mint leaves -- 1 green chili, seeds removed -- 1 tsp sea salt (to taste) -- 1/2 medium white onion -- 4-6 pieces of garlic -- 1/2 inch piece ginger -- 1/2 lemon, juiced -- 3 tbsp white vinegar -- black pepper--optional: 1/4 cup mango (fresh or frozen will work)

recipeDirections.en-US: Add all ingredients to the blender, blend to smooth. May add extra liquid ingredients (lemon, vinegar) as needed, 1 tbsp at a time until desired texture is reached.

metaDescription.en-US: Discover the ultimate homemade sauce recipe—a versatile drizzle packed with nutritious ingredients like avocado, white onion, cilantro, ginger, and garlic. Great for salads, burgers, wraps, and more, this sauce adds flavor and health benefits to every dish

description.en-US:
It is July right now, we are in Texas and have a tree blessing us with an abundance of sweet, juicy peaches. It would only be right for me to add some tasty peach infused recipes on the blog! Starting with this fresh salad!

Peaches are such a great addition to any salad, they are sweet, full of fiber, and rich in vitamin C. They not only enhance the flavor of a dish but the increase the nutrient level as well. 

This beautiful and vibrant salad is a great addition to any summer BBQ or can be enjoyed on its own as a meal. 

If you would like to add some protein, feel free to add some oven roasted tofu or other protein source. 

For the dressing, you will likely have some leftover, store it in an airtight container for up to 5 days. 

Optional: if you want some extra nutrition, you may add 3-4 tbsp hemp seeds, broccoli sprouts, and or some ripe avocado. 

Cheers to health! 

Bobbielee, xo

ingredients.en-US: *Salad --8 cups spinach -- 2 peaches, sliced -- 1/3 cup red onion, slice thin --1 cup pecans -- 1/2 cup feta cheese, crumbled-- *Dressing --1/4 cups extra virgin olive oil -- 3 tbsp lemon juice, fresh squeezed -- sea salt and black pepper to taste

recipeDirections.en-US: In a large bowl, toss all the salad ingredients together. -- In a small bowl, add the dressing ingredients and whisk it together. -- Add the dressing to the salad combine and enjoy!

metaDescription.en-US: This beautiful and vibrant Peach and Spinach salad is a great addition to any summer BBQ or can be enjoyed on its own as a meal.

title.en-US: Peanut Butter and Date Pre-run Smoothie

description.en-US:
This smoothie serves as a great pre-run boost. The ingredients in this blend all have a benefit and are there to boost your energy and fuel your body for the work ahead.

Dates and bananas serve as a natural energy boost for your body, best when consumed a few hours before the activity. For hydration, I chose to use coconut water as it is electrolyte rich and provides a great base for the smoothie. Feel feel to adjust the amount to reach the desired consistency. If you are looking to add some protein to the mix, feel free to add a scoop of desired protein supplement. 

For best results, I recommend consuming this blend 1.5 to 2 hours before your run.

Cheers, stay happy and healthy!!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup spinach -- 3 dates, pitted -- 1/2 banana, frozen -- 1/2 avocado -- 1 tbsp peanut butter -- 1/2 inch ginger (optional) --  1/2 cup mango, frozen -- 1 cup coconut water

recipeDirections.en-US: Place all ingredients in a blender container, blend til smooth. -- Cheers!!

metaDescription.en-US: This smoothie serves as a great pre-run boost. The ingredients in this blend all have a benefit and are there for a reason, mainly to fuel your body for the work ahead.

description.en-US:
Elevate your mornings with our irresistible baked granola recipe, a staple in our kitchen year-round, especially during the cozy fall and winter seasons. Bursting with chocolaty goodness, this granola is a delightful blend of oats, nuts, seeds, and just the right amount of chocolate for a satisfying crunch.

While I love mine sprinkled over creamy yogurt and topped with fresh fruit, feel free to enjoy it as a classic cereal with your milk of choice. Whichever way you prefer, this granola is sure to add a deliciously wholesome touch to your breakfast or snacking routine.

Treat yourself to a nourishing and indulgent start to your day with our homemade baked granola. 

Enjoy!

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup rolled oats -- 2 cups coconut flakes -- 1  1/4 cup pumpkin seeds, shelled -- 1 1/2 cups almonds, chopped -- 1/4 cup maple syrup -- 3 tbsp coconut oil, melted -- 2 tbsp cocoa powder --  3/4 tsp cinnamon -- 1/2 tsp sea salt

recipeDirections.en-US: Preheat oven to 325°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and set aside. --  In a small bowl, add the maple syrup and melted coconut oil, cocoa powder, cinnamon, and sea salt, whisk together. -- In a large bowl, add the rolled oats, coconut flakes, pumpkin seeds, and almonds. -- Add the syrup mixture to the oat and nut mixture, toss well. Spread the mixture even onto the line baking sheet. Bake for 25 mins or until golden brown. Stir the granola half way through bake time. -- Allow the granola to cool before storing. For best quality, store at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 3 weeks.

metaDescription.en-US: Start your day with our delicious Chocolatey Granola recipe! Made with rolled oats, coconut flakes, pumpkin seeds, almonds, cocoa powder, and warming spices, this homemade granola is the perfect  way to start your day.

title.en-US: 9 Tips to Incorporate More Plant-Based Foods to your Diet

description.en-US:
There is a lot of hype right now about eating a plant focused diet to improve ones health and prevent lifestyle diseases. Many nutritional studies show a strong relationship between increased plant (fruits, veggies, legumes, etc.) intake and prevention of many lifestyle diseases. It is important to note that I am not talking about highly processed plant based foods. Instead, include fresh wholefood ingredients like those found in the produce aisle. 

Plants  offer vitamins and minerals, phytochemicals and antioxidants, as well they help you increase fiber intake, something many Americans appear to be falling short on. Going plant based is not for everyone, but adding more plants to your diet should be a goal and can be easier than you may think. 

## 9 Tips to Include More Plant-based Foods:

__1. Start your day with a plant-based meal__, such as a smoothie, oatmeal with nut milk and fruit, or pancakes.
Here is a great smoothie recipe to try, [Classic Green Smoothie](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/classic-green-smoothie "Classic Green Smoothie")

__2. Make vegetables the priority of your meals.__ You can saute, grill or roast them. You may add vegetables to soups, pastas, and casseroles. Greens are also a great addition to smoothies.

__3. Experiment with plant-based protein sources.__ Lentils, tofu, and beans are all great options to include. Daals, black bean burgers, burritos, and quesadillas are great meal options.

__4. Incorporate more fresh fruit into your diet.__ You can add it as a breakfast side, in smoothies and juices, salads, snacks, etc.

__5. Include more whole grains into your diet.__ Quinoa, brown rice, farro, millet, and barley, are all great options. They contain many nutrients. Grain can be used in replacement for rice and can be used in salad bowls. 

__6. Build a meal around a salad.__ Salads are GREAT and make an easy, and nutritious meal. Start with your vegetable base, add some fruit, nuts and/or seeds, grains, etc. Prepare and use a delicious dressing  recipe like this one [here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/basic-acv-dressing).

__7. Spice it up.__ Experiment with different spices and herbs to add some flavor (and nutrients) to your plant-based dishes. 
Here is a sauce I love to add to just about everything, [Avocado Chutney (Fo'shizzle) Drizzle Sauce](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/avocado-chutney-foshizzle-drizzle-sauce "Avocado Chutney (Fo'Shizzle) Drizzle Sauce"). This delicious sauce tastes great on salad bowls, burgers, tacos, etc. 

__8. Plan it out.__ Planning out your meals in advance is always a great way to succeed and achieve your goals. Be sure to have a variety of plant based foods available at home and when on the go. 

__9. Try some of the many delicious plant-based meat alternatives.__ Black bean burgers, lentil loafs, veggie balls, etc. Tip: make your own as many varieties have unwanted ingredients and are often loaded with sodium.

Everyone is different, we have different dietary needs and preferences. Be creative, stay focused, and have fun experimenting with new, exciting foods and recipes! 

Cheers to health!

Bobbielee, Xo

metaDescription.en-US: There is a lot of hype right now about eating a plant based diet to improve ones health outcome. It is everywhere! Many nutritional studies show a strong relationship between increased plant, are you getting enough?

description.en-US:
If you are looking to take your homemade pizza game to the next level, this is it! This pizza dough is chewy, crispy, and loved by everyone!  

Pizza is a Friday staple in our home. The kids and hubby will not let a Friday go by without reminding me that Friday equals PIZZA. Usually I prepare my dough in the morning and allow it to rise throughout the day, but I have also had great success preparing it just a few hours before bake time.

Whether you prefer thin or thick crust, this recipe will work for you. I prefer baking on a pizza stone but a baking pan will work well too.

I highly recommend using oat bran, but if you do not have it, go ahead and replace it with one of the flours mentioned in the ingredients. The oat bran gives the crust a delicious crispy texture!

ingredients.en-US: 1½ cup warm water (warm water activates the yeast)--2 teaspoons honey or maple syrup-- 1 package active dry yeast  -- ½ cup semolina flour, more for dusting--½ cup oat bran (if you do not have this you may use semolina)--3 cups 00 flour (if your cannot find it, all-purpose flour will do)--1 teaspoon salt-- 2 tablespoon plus extra-virgin olive oil

recipeDirections.en-US: In a small bowl, whisk together the warm water and honey (or maple syrup). Now sprinkle the yeast on top and allow it to activate and become foamy. This will take about 5 mins. -- Now in a large bowl, add the flours, oat bran, and salt, stir to combine. Add the yeast mixture and 1 tbsp of the olive oil to the flour bowl, use a wooded spoon or your hand to combine it all together. -- Remove the dough from the bowl and place it on a lightly floured surface, knead the dough using your hand for about 4 minutes. Knead it into a smooth ball and place it into a large bowl. (If the dough is too dry to form a ball, add 1 tablespoon of water at a time until the dough smoothes together. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour) --Using the remaining 1 tbsp of olive oil, brush it onto a large bowl. Place the dough ball into the bowl and cover the bowl with a clean dish towel or plastic wrap and set aside. Allow it to sit for 1 hour or more until the dough doubles in size. -- Once the dough is ready, punch it down and transfer the dough to a lightly semolina floured surface, cut the dough in half (or thirds for thin crust pizza). -- Preheat the oven to 420 F (if using a pizza stone, warm it in the oven as it is heating up). --  Using your hands, stretch the dough out the fit the pizza stone or pan of choice (I use a pizza stone or 14-inch pizza pan). -- Top the pizza as desired and bake until the pizza is golden brown and the cheese bubbles, about 16-18 minutes. Cut and serve hot! Enjoy!

metaDescription.en-US: If you are looking to take your homemade pizza game to the next level, this is it! This pizza dough is chewy, crispy and and loved by everyone!

title.en-US: Instant Pot Method - Green Moong Daal

description.en-US:
Green Daal, also known as Green Moong Dal, is a popular Indian dish made with split green mung beans. It is a nutritious and flavorful dish that can be served with rice, roti, or quinoa.

Green Daal is a mildly spicy and comforting dish that is rich in protein and dietary fiber. It is a healthy and delicious vegetarian option that can be enjoyed as a main course or as a side dish. Green Moong Daal is also a popular choice for those following a vegan or gluten-free diet. 

This dish is easy to make. With just a handful of simple ingredients, it can be ready to serve in under 30 mins with this Instant Pot recipe.  

I hope you enjoy this nutritious and delicious recipe.

ingredients.en-US: 1 cup green moong daal (mung bean), rinsed -- 1 tbsp avocado oil, ghee (for oil-free, substitute with 3 tablespoons water) -- 1 tsp cumin seeds --  ½  green Serrano pepper (optional), diced -- 1 medium yellow (or purple) onion diced -- ½ tbsp fresh ginger grated or crushed into paste -- ½ tbsp garlic minced or crushed into paste -- 1 medium tomato, chopped -- 3 cups water -- 2 tbsp lemon juice -- cilantro to garnish --*Spices--1 tsp ground turmeric powder -- 1 tsp coriander powder -- ½ tsp red chili powder (Optional) -- ½ tsp garam masala -- 1 tsp salt -- black pepper

recipeDirections.en-US: Turn on the Instant pot and select SAUTE mode. Add the oil (or water), and cumin seeds. Sauté for 20-30 seconds, until the cumin seeds begin to sputter. -- Add the Serrano pepper, onions, ginger and garlic. Sauté until the onions become translucent, about 3-4 minutes. If needed add a few tbsp of water to keep it from sticking to the pot. -- Add the chopped tomato and all the spices, give it a stir. -- Add the washed green moong bean and water, stir it again to combine all the ingredients. -- Cancel the SAUTE mode and place the sealed lid onto the Instant Pot. Close the vent so that it is in the sealing position. -- Select Pressure Cook mode and set the time to 18 minutes and set aside. Allow the pressure to release naturally. -- Once cooking is finished, remove the lid and stir in the lemon juice and fresh cilantro. Taste and adjust spices as needed. Serve with roti, rice, quinoa, or enjoy as a soup.

metaDescription.en-US: Green Lentil Daal is a comforting dish that is rich in protein and dietary fiber. It is a healthy and delicious vegetarian option that can be enjoyed as a main course or as a side dish.

title.en-US: 6 Tips to Help Improve Your Sleep Quality as a Night Shift Worker

description.en-US:
This post is for my fellow night shifters. I have been there, did it for a few years and felt the struggle! Always feeling tired and groggy, sun deprived, falling asleep in the chair when trying to chart in the early morning, and then once you get home after being up all night, you get that second wind and have difficulties falling asleep! 

Getting quality sleep can be a big challenge due to the disruption of your natural sleep-wake cycle. However, there are several strategies you can employ to improve your sleep and overall well-being. 

Here are six tips that helped me and may help you sleep better as a night shift worker:

__1. Establish a Consistent Sleep Schedule:__ 
Try to maintain a regular sleep schedule by going to bed and waking up at the same time every day, even on your days off. I know this may be hard to commit to on your days off but this helps regulate your body's internal clock and promotes better sleep quality. 

__2. Create a Sleep-Friendly Environment:__ 
Make your bedroom as dark, quiet, and comfortable as possible. Use blackout curtains or an eye mask to block out daylight, wear earplugs or use white noise machines to minimize noise disturbances, and ensure your mattress and pillows are supportive and comfortable.

__3. Practice Relaxation Techniques:__ 
Develop a pre-sleep routine to signal your body that it's time to unwind. This can include activities such as reading a book, taking a warm bath, practicing deep breathing exercises, meditating, or listening to calming music. Find what works best for you to relax and prepare for sleep.

__4. Limit Exposure to Stimulants:__
Avoid consuming stimulants like caffeine and alcohol close to your bedtime, as they can interfere with your ability to fall asleep and stay asleep. These substances can disrupt your sleep patterns and make it harder to achieve deep, restful sleep.

__5. Maintain a Healthy Lifestyle:__ 
Engage in regular exercise, but try to do it at least a few hours before your planned sleep time, as exercise can initially increase alertness. Additionally, pay attention to your diet and avoid heavy meals close to bedtime. Instead, opt for lighter, nutritious snacks. 

I often made a bowl of steel cut oats about 30 mins before sleeping, [See recipe here](https://www.bobbieleelicious.com/recipe/steel-cut-oats-in-the-instant-pot). Find what works for you. 

__6. Seek Support:__ 
Discuss your sleep concerns with your supervisor or colleagues who have experience with night shifts. Sharing strategies and seeking support can help you navigate the challenges and find solutions specific to your work environment.

__Bonus tip -__ __Keep a note pad and pencil on your bedside table:__  I have had many people tell me that they have difficulties sleeping as they have so many thoughts come to mind once their head hits the pillow. Writing these thoughts out will free your mind and help promote better sleep quality. 

Remember, it may take time for your body to adjust to a night shift schedule. Be patient and consistent, and consult with a healthcare professional if you continue to struggle with sleep.

__Resources:__
[Source: National Sleep Foundation - How to Sleep Better]
 [Source: Mayo Clinic - Healthy Lifestyle: Adult health - Sleep tips for night-shift workers]

 As always, take care and stay well!

metaDescription.en-US: Getting quality sleep can be a big challenge due to the disruption of your natural sleep-wake cycle. However, there are several strategies you can employ to improve your sleep and overall well-being.

description.en-US:
In a fast-paced world, prioritizing our well-being is crucial. Yet, a staggering 60 percent of Americans fall short of meeting the recommended levels of physical activity, as highlighted by the esteemed Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). The implications of this statistic are profound, as it can potentially lead to negative health outcomes.

Engaging in regular physical activity offers many benefits for both physical and mental well-being. According to NIH “Adults need at least 150-300 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity or 75-150 minutes of vigorous-intensity physical activity per week.” 

### Here are some key benefits of physical activity:
1. May assist with weight management: Regular exercise, combined with a well balanced diet, may help maintain a healthy weight or may help support weight loss by burning calories.
2. May boost the immune system: Regular physical activity can strengthen the immune system, making you less susceptible to certain illnesses and infections.
3. May improve mental health: Exercise releases endorphins, also known as "feel-good" hormones, which can improve mood, reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression, and enhance overall mental well-being.
4. May increase energy levels: Engaging in physical activity regularly may boost energy levels and combat feelings of fatigue or lethargy.
5. May improve sleep: Regular exercise may promote better sleep quality, helping you fall asleep faster and enjoy more restful sleep. Be sure to not workout too close to bedtime or it may actually keep you awake  
6. May enhance cognitive function: Physical activity has been linked to improved cognitive function, including better memory, focus, creativity, and overall brain health.
7. May reduce stress: Exercise may be an effective stress reliever, helping to reduce tension, improve mood, and provide an outlet for stress management.
8. Social interaction: Participating in group activities or team sports may provide opportunities for social interaction, fostering connections and a sense of belonging.
9. May promote long-term health benefits: Regular physical activity may be associated with a lower risk of chronic lifestyle related conditions, such as type 2 diabetes, obesity, hypertension, certain types of cancer, and age-related cognitive decline. 

Finding activities you enjoy and incorporating them into your daily routine can help you experience these health benefits and maintain a healthy lifestyle. It's important to note that the specific benefits may vary depending on the type, intensity, and duration of physical activity. 

Grab a workout partner or friend and get moving towards better health! 

Note: this article was written for entertainment purposes, be sure to speak with your healthcare provider before beginning any new workout regimen. 

### Resources:

[https://www.nhlbi.nih.gov/health/heart/physical-activity/benefits](https://www.nhlbi.nih.gov/health/heart/physical-activity/benefits)
